justification_1: This card can revive a Shaddoll monster from the GY, continuing plays after an initial monster has been summoned and facilitating further combos. Its second effect, while situational, also offers additional utility to extend plays by manipulating monster battle positions.
justification_2: This card's first effect allows for the revival of a "Shaddoll" monster from the GY, which can be used to continue combos or extend plays after an initial monster has been summoned or an effect has been activated. The second effect also facilitates further plays by manipulating monster positions.

card_text: This card gains 100 ATK for each monster your opponent controls.
justification_1: This card's effect provides a static ATK boost based on the opponent's board state and does not have an activation condition that allows it to initiate plays, extend them, disrupt the opponent, break their board, or serve as a combo enabler.
justification_2: This card has a continuous effect that buffs its ATK based on the opponent's board state. It does not have an activation condition that fits the defined categories (Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, Garnet) and does not directly disrupt, extend, break boards, or act as a combo enabler that is bad to draw.

card_text:
1 "Amazoness" Fusion Monster + 1 "Amazoness" monster
Your opponent cannot target other "Amazoness" cards you control with card effects, also those cards cannot be destroyed by your opponent's card effects. If this card was Fusion Summoned using "Amazoness Empress" or "Amazoness Queen" as material, it can make a second attack during each Battle Phase. If this card is Fusion Summoned: You can Special Summon 1 "Amazoness" monster from your Deck. You can only use this effect of "Amazoness Augusta" once per turn.
justification_1: As a Fusion Monster, "Amazoness Augusta" requires resources (Fusion Materials and a Fusion Spell) to be expended first, meaning it cannot initiate a main combo by itself. However, once Fusion Summoned, its effect to Special Summon an "Amazoness" monster from the Deck significantly extends the player's board presence and further their game plan, fitting the role of an extender that builds upon existing plays.
justification_2: This card is an Extra Deck monster whose primary effect triggers upon Fusion Summon, allowing the player to Special Summon another "Amazoness" monster from their Deck. This effect serves to continue or extend existing plays by adding more monsters to the field, but it requires the player to have already committed resources to perform the Fusion Summon, thus fitting the definition of an Extender.

card_text: This card is used to Ritual Summon "Elemental Mistress Doriado". You must also Tribute monsters whose total Levels equal 3 or more from the field or your hand.
justification_1: This card is a Ritual Spell that facilitates the summoning of a specific monster but does not activate in response to opponent actions, begin a combo independently, extend plays beyond the initial summon, or break a board. It is a necessary component for a Ritual Summon but doesn't fit the defined categories of starter, handtrap, extender, board breaker, or garnet.
justification_2: This card is a Ritual Spell that requires specific tributes for a Ritual Summon. It does not meet the criteria for any of the defined categories (Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, Garnet) as its effect is solely to facilitate a Ritual Summon and does not inherently disrupt, extend, or break boards on its own without the presence of its specific Ritual Monster and the necessary tributes.

card_text: Once per turn: You can target 1 other card you control; send that card to the GY, also your opponent cannot activate cards or effects in response to your Spell Card activations for the rest of this turn (even if this card leaves the field). (You can only gain the previous effect once per turn.) Once per turn, during the End Phase: You can Set "Sky Striker" Spells with different names from your GY, up to the number of "Sky Striker" Spell Cards you activated this turn while this card was face-up on your field, but banish them when they leave the field.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand on its own to grant protection for spell activations, allowing for uninterrupted plays to begin a combo. Its second effect, which sets spells from the GY, also facilitates further plays.
justification_2: This card’s effect allows for reactive disruption – negating opponent’s responses and controlling the flow of the game by limiting their spell activations. It’s a reactive effect designed to disrupt the opponent's plays, characteristic of a handtrap.

card_text: If you control a face-up "Fabled" monster: You can send this card from your hand to the Graveyard; add 1 "Fabled" monster from your Deck to your hand, except "Fabled Grimro".
justification_1: The card’s effect requires an already-established board (a “Fabled” monster) to use, allowing additional combo progression after a play has started.
justification_2: This card’s effect requires an already-established board (a “Fabled” monster) to utilize its search effect, extending plays and providing additional value after a main combo has started.

card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
During each Standby Phase: You can add 1 face-up Pendulum Monster from your Extra Deck to your hand. You can only use this effect of "Symphonic Warrior Rockks" once per turn. When an opponent's monster declares an attack: You can destroy both that monster and this card.

[ Monster Effect ] 
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
If this card in the Monster Zone is destroyed by battle or card effect: You can place this card in your Pendulum Zone. You can only use each of the following effects of "Symphonic Warrior Rockks" once per turn. If this card is Special Summoned: You can add 1 face-up Pendulum Monster from your Extra Deck to your hand. If "Symph Amplifire" is in your Field Zone: You can destroy 1 card on the field.
justification_1: As a Synchro Monster, "Symphonic Warrior Rockks" requires prior monster presence to be Special Summoned. Its monster effect to add a face-up Pendulum Monster from the Extra Deck to hand upon Special Summon allows the player to recover resources and continue or expand their combo after committing to its summon, fitting the definition of an Extender. Its other effects are conditional or reactive, supporting this role by providing further utility or disruption once established.
justification_2: This card is an Extra Deck monster whose primary Monster Effect to add a Pendulum Monster from the Extra Deck to hand activates upon its Special Summon. This means it requires other monsters to already be on the field to facilitate its Synchro Summon, enabling further combo progression or resource recovery after an initial play has been made. Its Pendulum Effect also extends resource generation by adding a face-up Pendulum Monster from the Extra Deck.

card_text: Cannot be Normal Summoned, unless you control a "Ghostrick" monster. Once per turn: You can change this card to face-down Defense Position. When a Level 4 or higher monster(s) is Normal or Special Summoned: Change that monster(s) to face-down Defense Position. There must be another "Ghostrick" monster on the field to activate and to resolve this effect.
justification_1: This card's primary effect of flipping monsters face-down requires another "Ghostrick" monster on the field to activate, indicating it's used to extend existing plays or maintain a board state rather than initiating them. While it can be summoned without a "Ghostrick" monster, its main disruptive/utility effect is conditional on having a board presence.
justification_2: This card cannot be Normal Summoned unless a "Ghostrick" monster is already on the field, meaning it requires an established play to be used. Its effect to flip monsters face-down further extends existing plays by disrupting the opponent's board.

card_text: Each time an opponent's monster effect is activated, place 1 Spell Counter on this card when that effect resolves. You can send this card with 6 or more Spell Counters to the GY; add 1 Spell from your Deck to your hand. You can only use this effect of "Mythical Bestiamorph" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be activated on its own to start generating Spell Counters, which are then used to search for other Spell cards from the Deck, initiating a line of play.
justification_2: This card accumulates Spell Counters based on opponent's actions, and then can be used to search a Spell card. While it doesn't start a combo, it provides additional resources and consistency, extending plays by adding a key Spell to the hand.

card_text:
2 "Gem-" monsters
If this card is Link Summoned: You can add 1 "Gem-Knight" card from your Deck to your hand. You can pay 1000 LP; Fusion Summon 1 "Gem-Knight" Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, by shuffling Fusion Materials in your possession listed on it into your Deck, that are banished or in the GY, but it cannot attack directly this turn. You can only use each effect of "Gem-Knight Phantom Quartz" once per turn.
justification_1: The card can add a "Gem-Knight" card from the deck to the hand upon Link Summoning, directly facilitating the start of plays and the deck's primary strategy.
justification_2: This card's effect allows it to search for any "Gem-Knight" card upon being Link Summoned. This search effect can initiate a combo by providing the necessary pieces to continue plays, qualifying it as a starter.

card_text: If a "Chronomaly" monster(s) you control would be destroyed by battle or your opponent's card effect, you can discard this card instead. If this card is in your GY: You can pay 1000 LP; Special Summon this card, but return it to the hand during the End Phase. You cannot Special Summon monsters the turn you activate this effect, except "Chronomaly" monsters. You can only use this effect of "Chronomaly Acambaro Figures" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's primary Special Summon effect requires it to be in the GY, allowing it to provide an additional monster for Xyz Summons after initial plays have sent it there or used it as material. The restriction to "Chronomaly" Special Summons reinforces its role in extending archetype-specific combos, fitting the definition of an extender that continues plays rather than starting them.
justification_2: This card can Special Summon itself from the GY after it has been sent there, providing an additional monster for "Chronomaly" Xyz Summons and allowing for further combo progression within the archetype. Its protective effect also helps maintain board presence, indirectly aiding in pushing through interruptions.

card_text: Remove from play 1 card from your opponent's Graveyard.
justification_1: This card's effect is a simple interaction with the opponent's graveyard and does not fit the criteria for starting combos, disrupting hand plays, extending existing plays, breaking boards, or being a necessary but undesirable draw. It's a straightforward removal effect that doesn't align with the defined strategic roles.
justification_2: This card’s effect only removes a card from the opponent's Graveyard, lacking the conditions or strategic impact to fit into any of the defined categories. It does not initiate plays, disrupt hands, extend combos, break boards, or serve as a necessary but undesirable draw.

card_text: If this card is sent from the hand or field to the GY: Add 1 each of "Ojama Green", "Ojama Yellow" and "Ojama Black" from your Deck to your hand.
justification_1: This card can be activated by sending it from the hand to the GY, which is an easy condition to meet by itself. Its effect to search for three specific cards makes it a powerful opening move that can begin a variety of plays by providing essential combo pieces.
justification_2: This card's effect allows for the addition of three specific cards from the Deck to the hand when sent to the GY, providing a significant resource advantage and enabling the initiation of plays.

card_text: When this card is targeted by an opponent's card effect, you can send this face-up card you control to the Graveyard to Special Summon 2 Level 4 or lower "Naturia" monsters from your Deck. They cannot declare an attack, and are destroyed during your End Phase.
justification_1: This card requires an opponent's card effect to target it for activation. Once triggered, it allows for the Special Summoning of additional monsters, extending your plays after a disruption.
justification_2: This card’s effect is reactive, activating when it's targeted by an opponent's card effect. It requires another "Naturia" monster to be on the field for the effect to trigger and then uses itself as material to summon other "Naturia" monsters, extending your plays after an initial setup or in response to an opponent's action.

card_text: When a "Galaxy" monster you control is targeted for an attack: You can Special Summon this card from your hand in Defense Position. When Summoned this way, you can: Immediately after this effect resolves, Xyz Summon 1 "Galaxy" Xyz Monster, using only "Galaxy" monsters you control.
justification_1: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand when a specific condition is met (opponent attacking a "Galaxy" monster), and it directly continues plays by allowing an Xyz Summon. This requires existing board presence or a specific opponent action to activate, fitting the role of an extender rather than a starter.
justification_2: This card is summoned from the hand when a specific condition is met (a "Galaxy" monster being targeted for an attack). Its effect then allows for further plays by enabling an Xyz Summon, extending an existing board state rather than starting one or disrupting the opponent.

card_text: (Quick Effect): You can discard this card, then target 1 "Aroma" monster you control; gain LP equal to half its ATK. If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can Special Summon 1 non-Tuner "Aroma" monster from your Deck, also you cannot Special Summon for the rest of this turn, except Plant monsters. You can only use each effect of "Aromalilith Rosalina" once per turn.
justification_1: This card's effect to Special Summon a non-Tuner "Aroma" monster from the Deck upon its Normal or Special Summon allows it to initiate the deck's primary combo line. By Normal Summoning it, a player can immediately advance their game state without prior setup, making it a classic Starter.
justification_2: This card's most powerful effect triggers on its Normal or Special Summon, allowing you to Special Summon another "Aroma" monster directly from your Deck. This is a classic starter effect, as it can be used on your first turn with a single card (its Normal Summon) to establish an immediate board presence and begin your main combos.

card_text: Target 1 Spellcaster Effect Monster in your GY; add it to your hand. You can only activate 1 "Dwimmered Path" per turn.
justification_1: This card's effect requires a Spellcaster Effect Monster to be in the GY, meaning a play must have already occurred for it to be activated. It then adds another piece to hand, extending existing plays.
justification_2: This card requires a Spellcaster Effect Monster to be in the Graveyard to activate its effect, meaning it relies on previous plays or setup to generate value, which is characteristic of an extender.

card_text: Once per turn, at the end of the Battle Phase: Return this card from the field to the hand. You can only use each of the following effects of "Ken the Warrior Dragon" once per turn. During your Main Phase: You can Special Summon 1 "Gen the Diamond Tiger" from your hand or Deck to your opponent's field in Defense Position. If this card is Special Summoned by the effect of "Gen the Diamond Tiger": Your opponent draws 2 cards, then they discard 1 card.
justification_1: Ken the Warrior Dragon's primary role is to be Special Summoned from the Deck by the effect of "Gen the Diamond Tiger" to enable further plays (like an Xyz Summon). Drawing "Ken the Warrior Dragon" is suboptimal, as it prevents this primary combo line and its own on-field effect is not advantageous. Therefore, it is a card required for an engine to function but is undesirable to have in the opening hand, fitting the definition of a Garnet.
justification_2: This card can be Normal Summoned to begin a deck's main line of play. Its effect to Special Summon "Gen the Diamond Tiger" from the Deck initiates a combo that generates significant advantage, making it a powerful one-card starter.

card_text: If a monster you control is destroyed by battle or card effect and sent to the Graveyard: You can Special Summon this card from your hand.
justification_1: This card's effect can only be activated if a monster you control is destroyed and sent to the Graveyard, meaning it requires a prior board state or an action to have already occurred, thus extending your plays.
justification_2: This card's effect can only be activated if a monster you control has already been destroyed by battle or card effect, meaning it requires a prior play or opponent interaction to be summoned and extend further plays.

card_text: If you control 3 or more EARTH Fairy monsters: Send as many monsters from the field to the GY as possible (min. 1), then each player can Special Summon monsters from their opponent's GY to their own field, up to the number of monsters sent to their opponent's GY by this effect, then, if a monster was Special Summoned by this effect and "Exchange of the Spirit" is in your GY, you can Set 1 Trap directly from your Deck. It can be activated this turn. You can only activate 1 "Exchange of Despair and Hope" per turn.
justification_1: This card's primary effect is to "Send as many monsters from the field to the GY as possible," which is a mass removal effect designed to clear established threats on your opponent's field. While it has a specific activation condition requiring 3 or more EARTH Fairy monsters on your field, its strategic role is to dismantle the opponent's board to create an opening or neutralize their setup, which aligns with the definition of a Board Breaker.
justification_2: The card's activation condition requires an established board of 3 or more EARTH Fairy monsters, meaning it cannot begin a combo. Instead, it leverages an existing board state to clear the field and potentially set up further plays (Special Summons and Trap setting), thereby extending or continuing a player's main line of play.

card_text:
During the End Phase, if this card was Normal Summoned this turn: Return it to the hand. You can only use each of these effects of "Yosenju Tsujik" once per turn.
● During either player's Damage Step, when a "Yosenju" monster you control battles an opponent's monster: You can discard this card; that monster you control gains 1000 ATK until the end of this turn.
● You can target 1 "Yosenju" monster on the field; it gains 1000 ATK until the end of this turn.
justification_1: This card can be discarded to boost a "Yosenju" monster's ATK during the Damage Step, extending a battle phase and potentially securing a win, but it doesn't initiate plays on its own. The second effect also requires another "Yosenju" monster on the field to extend its own effect.
justification_2: The card’s effect requires an already-established board (a “Yosenju” monster) to utilize its boosting effect, extending a combo after a play has started.

card_text: If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can add to your hand or Special Summon 1 "G Golem Pebble Dog" from your Deck, also you cannot Special Summon monsters for the rest of this turn, except Cyberse monsters. If this card is sent from the hand to the GY: You can add 1 "G Golem" card from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "G Golem Pebble Dog" once per turn.
justification_1: This card primarily functions to continue or extend plays. Its first effect allows it to bring out another copy of itself upon Normal or Special Summon, providing additional Link Material, though it imposes a strict Cyberse Special Summon restriction. The second effect, which allows it to search any "G Golem" card when sent from the hand to the GY, is a powerful way to cycle resources and gain card advantage during a combo, especially when discarded as a cost for another card's effect. Both effects help push through interruptions or expand board presence after an initial play has begun, fitting the definition of an Extender.
justification_2: The card's effect to add a "G Golem" card from the Deck to hand when sent from the hand to the GY allows it to turn discard costs into valuable searches, continuing or extending plays. Its on-summon effect also provides an additional body, useful for climbing into Link Summons and further combo progression, particularly within a Cyberse-focused strategy.

card_text: When this card is activated: You can take 1 "Amazoness" monster from your GY or your face-up Extra Deck, and either add it to your hand or (if it is a Pendulum Monster) place it in your Pendulum Zone. If a monster(s) is Special Summoned to your opponent's field while you control an "Amazoness" Monster Card (except during the Damage Step): You can target 1 of those opponent's monsters; gain LP equal to its ATK. You can only use this effect of "Amazoness Hall" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's primary effect on activation recovers "Amazoness" monsters from the GY or Extra Deck, allowing you to recycle resources and continue your plays after monsters have been used for summons or sent to the GY. This requires prior actions (having a monster in the GY or Extra Deck) and helps extend existing combos rather than starting them from scratch.
justification_2: The primary effect of "Amazoness Hall" allows you to recover an "Amazoness" monster from your GY or face-up Extra Deck, which inherently requires an Amazoness monster to have already been placed in one of those zones. This makes it ideal for continuing plays after a monster has been used for a summon, sent to the GY, or otherwise made available for recursion, rather than initiating a main combo line from an empty board.

card_text: You can target 1 "Nephthys" card in your GY, except "Chronicler of Nephthys"; destroy 1 card in your hand, and if you do, add that target to your hand. During your next Standby Phase after this card was destroyed by card effect and sent to the GY: You can add 1 "Nephthys" card from your GY to your hand, except "Chronicler of Nephthys". You can only use each effect of "Chronicler of Nephthys" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can initiate plays by adding a "Nephthys" card from the GY to the hand at the cost of discarding a card from hand, effectively functioning as a way to start combos by putting key pieces into play or the GY.
justification_2: The card's first effect requires another "Nephthys" card in the GY to activate, and it searches for a "Nephthys" card, allowing it to extend existing plays rather than initiating them. Its second effect can be activated after it has been destroyed, serving as a follow-up in later turns.

card_text: When a monster(s) (except a Spellcaster-Type monster) is Normal or Special Summoned to your opponent's side of the field while you control another face-up Spell Card and your opponent does not control any face-up Spell Cards: That monster cannot attack or activate its effects this turn. (This applies even if this card leaves the field.) If you do not control any Spellcaster-Type monsters, destroy this card.
justification_1: This card's effect can be activated from the hand when the opponent summons a monster under specific conditions (you control a face-up Spell, opponent does not), disrupting their plays by preventing the summoned monster from attacking or activating its effects. This aligns with the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.
justification_2: This card disrupts the opponent by negating monster effects and attacks under specific conditions (opponent summoning while you have a face-up Spell, and they don't). Its effect can be applied during the opponent's turn without needing to be on the field, allowing for reactive disruption.

card_text:
When a "Wind-Up" monster is Normal or Special Summoned to your side of the field: You can Special Summon this card from your hand. Once per turn: You can activate 1 of these effects.
● Increase this card's Level by 1, until the End Phase.
● Reduce this card's Level by 1, until the End Phase.
justification_1: This card's effect to Special Summon itself from the hand requires a "Wind-Up" monster to be already on the field, indicating it's used to continue or extend existing plays rather than start them.
justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand when a "Wind-Up" monster is summoned, allowing it to extend existing plays. Its own effects of changing its Level are also useful for continuing or advancing combos, rather than starting them.

card_text: If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can excavate the top 3 cards of your Deck, and if you do, you can add 1 excavated "Purrely" Spell/Trap to your hand, also place the rest on the bottom of the Deck in any order. Once per turn: You can reveal 1 "Purrely" Quick-Play Spell in your hand, and if you do, Special Summon 1 Xyz Monster that mentions it from your Extra Deck, by using this face-up card you control as material, and if you do, attach the revealed card to the Summoned monster as additional material. (This is treated as an Xyz Summon.)
justification_1: Upon Normal or Special Summon, this card searches for a "Purrely" Spell/Trap, providing key combo pieces. It then allows for an immediate Xyz Summon using itself and a "Purrely" Quick-Play Spell from hand, directly initiating the deck's main strategy and establishing its primary monsters from an opening play.
justification_2: This card can be Normal Summoned to immediately search a "Purrely" Spell/Trap, which is essential for the archetype's strategy. Its second effect then directly enables the summoning of a "Purrely" Xyz Monster, making it a powerful singular opening play that initiates the deck's main combo line and progresses towards its win condition.

card_text: When an opponent's monster declares an attack: Target the attacking monster; negate the attack, and if you do, inflict 800 damage to your opponent for each Fish, Sea Serpent, or Aqua-Type monster you control.
justification_1: This card is activated during the opponent's turn in response to an attack, negating it and dealing damage. This reactive disruption from the hand aligns with the definition of a handtrap.
justification_2: This card’s effect is activated from the hand during the opponent’s turn to disrupt their attack, fitting the definition of a reactive handtrap.

card_text: If this card is sent to the Graveyard for a Synchro Summon, select 1 monster your opponent controls and change its battle position.
justification_1: The card's effect triggers when it is sent to the Graveyard for a Synchro Summon and only affects the battle position of an opponent's monster. It does not activate from the hand, start a combo, extend a combo, break a board, or function as a "necessary brick."
justification_2: This card's effect activates when it is sent to the Graveyard for a Synchro Summon. It does not fit the activation criteria of a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet. Its effect is a minor positional change rather than a primary combo enabler or disruption.

card_text:
"Windwitch - Winter Bell" + 1 "Windwitch" monster
You can target 1 monster in either player's Graveyard; until the End Phase, this card's name becomes that target's original name, and replace this effect with that target's original effects. If this card in your possession is destroyed by your opponent's card (by battle or card effect) and sent to your Graveyard: You can target 1 "Windwitch - Winter Bell" and 1 Level 4 or lower "Windwitch" monster in your Graveyard; Special Summon them. You can only use each effect of "Windwitch - Crystal Bell" once per turn.
justification_1: This card’s effect requires an existing board (at least one "Windwitch" monster) to activate, extending the combos that have already been established. It doesn’t initiate a new play but builds upon an existing one.
justification_2: This card is a Fusion Monster that requires other "Windwitch" monsters on the field to be summoned. Its effects further extend plays by copying monster names and effects, or by reviving other "Windwitch" monsters when destroyed.

card_text: All FIRE Warrior monsters you control gain 500 ATK. Once per turn: You can pay 1200 LP; add 1 "Noble Arms" card from your Deck to your hand, except "Noble Arms Museum". Once per turn, if the previous effect was applied this turn: You can target 1 "Noble Knight" Monster Card in your Spell & Trap Zone; Special Summon it, also, if you activated this effect while you did not control "Infernoble Knight Emperor Charles", you cannot Special Summon monsters for the rest of this turn, except Warrior monsters.
justification_1: This card's primary effect allows you to search your Deck for a "Noble Arms" card, a key component for the "Noble Knight" and "Infernoble Knight" archetypes. This search initiates your main combo sequence. Its second effect is a direct follow-up that Special Summons a monster, further developing your board. Since it can start your plays without needing a pre-existing field, it functions as a core Starter for its deck.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand at the beginning of a turn to search for any "Noble Arms" card from the Deck. This search effect immediately provides a key combo piece (often an "Infernoble Knight" monster like Renaud) that is essential for initiating the deck's main lines of play, making it a fundamental card to open with to begin a combo.

card_text: Add 1 Level 5 or higher "Dragunity" monster from your Deck or GY to your hand. You can banish this card from your GY, then target 1 Monster Card in your Spell & Trap Zone equipped to a "Dragunity" monster; Special Summon it in Defense Position. You can only use each effect of "Dragunity Glow" once per turn.
justification_1: This card searches for a key "Dragunity" monster from the Deck, allowing it to initiate combos and establish a strong board presence from the start of the turn.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand to add a specific monster from the Deck, directly initiating a combo and progressing the player's game plan.

card_text:
You can Ritual Summon this card with any "Nekroz" Ritual Spell. Must be Ritual Summoned, without using any Level 8 monsters. You can only use each of these effects of "Nekroz of Valkyrus" once per turn.
● When an opponent's monster declares an attack: You can banish 1 "Nekroz" card from your GY and discard this card; negate the attack, then end the Battle Phase.
● During your Main Phase: You can Tribute up to 2 monsters from your hand and/or field, and if you do, draw the same number of cards you Tributed.
justification_1: The card can be activated from the hand during the opponent's turn to negate an attack, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap. The second effect, while not a handtrap, does not define the card's primary strategic role as an interruption.
justification_2: The card's first effect allows it to be discarded from the hand during the opponent's turn to negate an attack, directly disrupting the opponent's plays without being on the field.

card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
During your Main Phase: You can Special Summon 1 Level 4 or lower monster from your hand. You cannot conduct your Battle Phase the turn you activate this effect. During your Main Phase: You can destroy as many cards in the Field Zones as possible (min. 1), and if you do, gain 1000 LP, then you can add 1 Field Spell with a different name than the destroyed card(s), from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Ancient Fairy Dragon" once per turn.
justification_1: The card can Special Summon a monster from the hand during the Main Phase, which can initiate a combo or provide advantage. While it restricts the Battle Phase for the turn, the ability to extend plays by bringing out another monster from hand makes it a strong opening card.
justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand during the Main Phase to summon another monster from the hand, initiating a combo. While it restricts the Battle Phase, its ability to start plays makes it a Starter.

card_text: If this card is in your hand or GY: You can target 1 "Crystron" card you control; destroy it, and if you do, Special Summon this card, also you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck for the rest of this turn, except Machine monsters. If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can send up to 2 "Crystron" cards with different names from your Deck to the GY, except "Crystron Sulfador". justification_1: This card can be activated as a Quick Effect during the opponent's turn from the hand (by sending it to the GY) to disrupt plays by Special Summoning a "Plunder Patroll" monster and potentially equip itself, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.
justification_2: The card has a Quick Effect that can be activated during the opponent's turn from the Monster Zone, allowing it to disrupt the opponent's plays by Special Summoning a "Plunder Patroll" monster. While it also has an effect that triggers when sent to the GY, its primary disruptive capability from the hand or field during the opponent's turn aligns with the definition of a handtrap.

card_text: Equip only to a Warrior monster. It gains 300 ATK. During your Main Phase, if this card is in your GY: You can banish 2 Warrior monsters from your GY; add this card to your hand.
justification_1: The card's effect to add itself back to the hand from the GY requires prior setup of Warrior monsters in the GY, allowing for continued plays after a combo has begun.
justification_2: This card's effect to add itself back to the hand from the GY by banishing two Warrior monsters requires prior plays to have occurred to fill the GY, allowing for continued combo development.

card_text: Fusion Summon 1 Fairy Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, using monsters from your hand, field, and/or Pendulum Zones. If a "Melodious" Fusion Monster(s) is sent to your GY while this card is in your GY, even during the Damage Step: You can place this card on the bottom of your Deck, then draw 1 card. You can only use each effect of "Melodious Concerto" once per turn.
justification_1: This card's effect requires you to have monsters in your hand or on your field to use as Fusion Material. It cannot start a play by itself but rather continues a combo by using already established resources to summon a powerful monster, thereby extending your board and potential plays.
justification_2: This card is a Fusion Spell that requires you to have monsters in your hand or on the field to use as materials. It does not start a play on its own but rather extends an existing board state by combining your monsters into a more powerful Fusion Monster, thus continuing your combo.

card_text: If this card is sent to the Graveyard for the Synchro Summon of a Machine-Type monster: Special Summon 1 "Mecha Phantom Beast Token" (Machine-Type/WIND/Level 3/ATK 0/DEF 0), also, for the rest of this turn, you cannot Special Summon any other monsters, except WIND monsters. While you control a Token, this card cannot be destroyed by battle or card effects. You can Tribute 1 other "Mecha Phantom Beast" monster; increase this card's Level by 1. You can only use 1 "Mecha Phantom Beast Warbluran" effect per turn, and only once that turn.
justification_1: This card's primary function is to be sent to the Graveyard to Special Summon a Token, which directly aids in further Synchro Summons or provides a body on the field. This extends existing plays rather than initiating them on its own. While it can protect itself, its main utility lies in furthering a combo after a Synchro Summon has occurred.
justification_2: This card can be used as the initial piece of a play to special summon a token, which then allows for further plays by providing a monster to tribute or use as Synchro material. While it locks you into WIND monsters for the turn, it's often the first piece you'd want to see to begin your Synchro plays.

card_text: If you control an "Ancient Gear", you can Special Summon this card (from your hand) in face-up Attack Position.
justification_1: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand, but its activation condition requires control of an "Ancient Gear" monster, meaning it requires a prior play to have been made to be activated. This allows for further extension of combos after an initial play has started.
justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand, but its activation condition requires control of another "Ancient Gear" monster, meaning it needs an existing play to continue or extend.

card_text: During the Main or Battle Phase, if you took damage by battle or an opponent's card effect this turn (Quick Effect): You can send this card from your hand to the GY; Special Summon 1 "Dark Magician" or 1 "Dark Magician Girl" from your Deck or GY. If a face-up Spellcaster monster(s) you control is destroyed by battle or an opponent's card effect: You can add this card from the GY to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Magikuriboh" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's primary effect allows for the Special Summon of a key monster from the hand, but it requires a preceding condition (taking damage by battle or opponent's card effect). This makes it a reactive card that helps establish or re-establish board presence after an opponent's action, fitting the description of an Extender that continues or extends plays after something has started. Its second effect also supports this by recycling itself to hand after your Spellcaster monster is destroyed, further providing value and extending play options.
justification_2: This card's first effect is a Quick Effect that activates from the hand, but requires the prior condition of taking damage, and its purpose is to Special Summon one of your key monsters. Its second effect recycles itself from the GY when your Spellcaster monster is destroyed. Both effects enable recovery or further plays *after* a specific event has occurred, fitting the role of an extender that continues or recovers your game state rather than initiating a main combo or disrupting the opponent.

card_text: Cannot be Normal Summoned or Set. Must be Special Summoned by "Yubel - Terror Incarnate", and cannot be Special Summoned by other ways. This card cannot be destroyed by battle. You take no Battle Damage from battles involving this card. At the end of the Damage Step, if this face-up Attack Position card battles an opponent's monster: Inflict damage equal to that monster's ATK to your opponent, also destroy that monster.
justification_1: This card’s effect directly targets and destroys opponent’s monsters during battle, acting as a proactive board-clearing effect – a key function of a board breaker card.
justification_2: This card has no effect that can be activated to disrupt the opponent from the hand, nor does it initiate a combo by itself. Its effects are passive or retaliatory in nature, and it cannot be classified as a starter, extender, or board breaker according to the provided definitions.

card_text: You can Special Summon this card (from your hand or Deck) by sending 1 face-up "Aussa the Earth Charmer" and 1 face-up EARTH monster you control to the GY. If this card Special Summoned by its own effect attacks a Defense Position monster, inflict piercing battle damage.
justification_1: This card can be Special Summoned from the Deck, and its effect is self-contained, meaning it can initiate plays without requiring prior setup or other specific cards to be on the field first.
justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the Deck, allowing it to begin plays by establishing a presence on the field and enabling further combo potential.

card_text: If only your opponent controls a monster: Special Summon 1 "F.A." monster from your hand, and if you do, increase its Level by 3 until the end of this turn. If this card is in the GY and you control no cards in your Field Zone: You can banish this card from the GY; activate 1 "F.A." Field Spell directly from your hand or GY. You can only use this effect of "F.A. Overheat" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand when the opponent controls a monster to Special Summon another "F.A." monster from the hand, initiating a combo by itself and advancing the player's board.
justification_2: This card can be activated on its own under the condition that the opponent controls a monster, allowing it to Special Summon another "F.A." monster and increase its Level, which can initiate a combo.

card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
Once, while this card is in your Pendulum Zone, you can negate an activated card effect that targets another "Dinomist" card(s) you control, then destroy this card.

[ Monster Effect ] 
Banish any monster destroyed by battle with a "Dinomist" monster you control.
justification_1: This card's effects are primarily reactive or provide passive benefits to "Dinomist" monsters. The Pendulum Effect requires being in the Pendulum Zone to negate a targeting effect, and the Monster Effect banishes monsters destroyed by battle, neither of which initiates a combo, disrupts the opponent from the hand, extends existing plays, or breaks a board.
justification_2: This card's effects do not align with the activation conditions or strategic roles of Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet. The Pendulum Effect provides a targeted negation that requires another "Dinomist" card to be targeted, and the Monster Effect is a passive combat effect. Neither actively starts a combo, disrupts from hand, extends plays, breaks boards, or acts as a necessary brick for another engine.

card_text: Special Summon 1 Level 4 or lower Fish, Sea Serpent, or Aqua monster from your hand or GY, but you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck, except Xyz Monsters, while you control that face-up monster. If your opponent controls an Attack Position monster: You can banish this card from your GY, then target 1 Fish, Sea Serpent, or Aqua monster you control; it gains 1000 ATK until the end of this turn. You can only use each effect of "Aqua Jet Surface" once per turn.
justification_1: This card's effect requires a valid monster in the hand or GY to activate, meaning it cannot start a play by itself. Its role is to Special Summon an additional monster to the field, which "extends" a pre-existing board state or combo line, typically to facilitate an Xyz Summon.
justification_2: The card's primary effect Special Summons a monster from the hand or GY without requiring any prior field setup. This allows it to be used as an opening move to establish a monster on the field, initiating combos, particularly for Xyz Summons, which fits the role of a Starter.

card_text: When this card is Normal Summoned: You can Special Summon 1 non-LIGHT "Number" monster from your GY, but negate its effects, and if you do, equip both this card and 1 "Utopia" monster you control to it, each as an Equip Spell that gives it 1700 ATK, also you can only declare one attack for the rest of this turn. When the monster equipped with this card by this card's effect declares an attack on your opponent's monster: You can double the attacking monster's ATK, but destroy it during the End Phase.
justification_1: This card requires a "Number" monster to already be in the GY and a "Utopia" monster to be on the field for its main effect, meaning it cannot initiate a combo by itself. Instead, it utilizes these pre-existing resources to Special Summon and set up a powerful attack, extending plays and pushing for more value after a combo has begun.
justification_2: The card's effect requires a specific setup to be useful: a "Utopia" monster already on the field and a non-LIGHT "Number" monster in the GY. It activates upon Normal Summon, which typically occurs after initial plays have established some board presence or graveyard resources, allowing you to continue or extend your turn with an aggressive push, rather than initiating a combo from scratch.

card_text:
2+ Level 6 monsters
If you use a monster(s), with a Level, that is owned by your opponent for the Xyz Summon of this card, treat it as Level 6. Once per turn: You can detach 1 material from this card, then target 1 card your opponent controls; send it to the GY. Once per turn, if a Monster Card(s) is sent from the field to your opponent's GY by a card effect, or a monster is destroyed by battle and sent to your opponent's GY: You can detach 1 material from this card; Special Summon 1 of those monsters to your field in Defense Position.
justification_1: This card's primary effect allows it to target and send an opponent's card to the GY, directly removing a threat. The second effect allows for potential recursion by bringing back opponent's monsters. Both effects are proactive in dealing with the opponent's board state, making it a board breaker, especially when going second.
justification_2: The card's primary effect is to detach a material to send an opponent's card to the GY, which directly addresses and removes an established threat on the opponent's field, fitting the definition of a board breaker. While it has an additional effect to Special Summon monsters, its removal effect is the most prominent for breaking an opponent's board.

card_text: Reveal 3 Warrior-Type monsters from your Deck, including at least 1 "Black Luster Soldier" monster or "Gaia The Fierce Knight" monster, and have your opponent randomly pick 1. If they picked a "Black Luster Soldier" monster or "Gaia The Fierce Knight" monster, add it to your hand and send the rest to the Graveyard. Otherwise, send them all to the Graveyard. You can only activate 1 "Beginning of Heaven and Earth" per turn.
justification_1: This card allows you to reveal specific monsters from your Deck and add one to your hand, initiating your plays by fetching key combo pieces.
justification_2: This card initiates a play by revealing multiple monsters from the Deck and adding one to the hand, directly contributing to the deck's core strategy from the start.

card_text:
1 Tuner + 1 non-Tuner monster
Once per turn, during your opponent's Main Phase, you can (Quick Effect): Immediately after this effect resolves, Synchro Summon using this card you control. You can only use each of the following effects of "T.G. Mighty Striker" once per turn. If this card is Synchro Summoned: You can add 1 "T.G." Spell/Trap from your Deck to your hand. If this card is sent from your Monster Zone to the GY: You can send 1 "T.G." card from your Deck to the GY.
justification_1: As a Synchro Monster, "T.G. Mighty Striker" requires other monsters to be on the field before it can be summoned, meaning a combo must have already started. Once summoned, its effects (searching a Spell/Trap and milling a T.G. card to the GY) provide additional resources and set up further plays, while its Quick Effect allows for another Synchro Summon during the opponent's turn, extending your board presence and options.
justification_2: This card requires other monsters (a Tuner and a non-Tuner) to be summoned, meaning it cannot initiate a combo by itself. Once on the field, its "If Synchro Summoned" effect adds a Spell/Trap, continuing resource generation, and its Quick Effect Synchro Summon during the opponent's turn allows for further plays and interruptions after the initial setup, fitting the role of extending existing plays.

card_text: If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can add 1 "Dragonmaid" Spell/Trap from your Deck to your hand. At the start of the Battle Phase: You can return this card to the hand, and if you do, Special Summon 1 Level 7 or higher "Dragonmaid" monster from your hand or GY. You can only use each effect of "Chamber Dragonmaid" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be Normal or Special Summoned to add a key Spell/Trap to the hand, initiating a "Dragonmaid" strategy and advancing the deck's win condition.
justification_2: This card can initiate a combo by adding a key Spell/Trap to the hand upon being Normal or Special Summoned, allowing for immediate play progression.

card_text:
1 Tuner + 1 or more non-Tuner monsters
During your Main Phase 1: You can Tribute 1 monster; inflict damage to your opponent equal to the Tributed monster's Level on the field x 200. You can only use this effect of "Dark Strike Fighter" once per turn.
justification_1: This card requires monsters on the field to Tribute for its effect, indicating it extends existing plays rather than starting them. Its effect to inflict damage is a secondary benefit that can push for a win after a combo has been established.
justification_2: This card is a Synchro Monster that requires specific materials to summon. Its effect activates during the Main Phase 1 and requires tributing a monster to inflict damage. It does not fit the criteria for Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet as it doesn't directly initiate a combo on its own, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend existing plays, break boards, or serve as an undesirable combo piece.

card_text: Banish 1 "Destiny HERO" monster from your GY; add to your hand or Special Summon 1 "Destiny HERO" monster from your GY. You can banish this card from your GY, then target 2 "Destiny HERO" monsters you control; choose 1 of those monsters, its ATK becomes the other monster's ATK, until the end of this turn.
justification_1: The card's primary effect requires a "Destiny HERO" monster to already be in the GY, allowing for further Special Summons or resource recovery. The second effect also requires "Destiny HERO" monsters to be on the field. Both effects serve to continue or extend plays after the initial setup, rather than starting a combo from scratch or disrupting the opponent.
justification_2: This card's primary effect requires a "Destiny HERO" monster already in the GY, meaning it cannot initiate a combo from an empty board. Instead, it serves to recycle or Special Summon a "Destiny HERO" monster, effectively continuing or extending plays after an initial action has occurred, providing further resources or board presence. The second effect is also a utility effect for ongoing plays.

card_text: If you have 3 or more banished Fish, Sea Serpent, or Aqua-Type monsters: Target 1 card on the field; banish it.
justification_1: This card's effect is to target and banish a card on the field. Its activation condition requires having 3 or more banished Fish, Sea Serpent, or Aqua-Type monsters, which is typically achieved going second or after establishing a board that facilitates banishing those types, allowing it to remove an opponent's threat.
justification_2: This card's effect is used to remove a card from the field, which is typically done to disrupt an established opponent's board, fitting the role of a board breaker. It does not inherently start or extend your own plays.

card_text: When this card is Special Summoned: You can Special Summon as many "Crystal Beast" Monster Cards as possible from your Spell & Trap Zones. If this face-up card is destroyed in a Monster Zone, you can place it face-up in your Spell & Trap Zone as a Continuous Spell, instead of sending it to the GY.
justification_1: This card can Special Summon itself from the hand or Deck when another "Crystal Beast" card is activated or another effect allows it to be Special Summoned. Its primary effect is to then Special Summon multiple "Crystal Beast" monsters from the Spell & Trap Zones, which is the core of many "Crystal Beast" combos and can initiate a strong opening play.
justification_2: This card Special Summons itself and then Special Summons multiple "Crystal Beast" monsters from the Spell & Trap Zones, which is a strong opening play that can initiate a combo without requiring other cards.

card_text: You can Special Summon this card (from your hand) by Tributing 2 WATER monsters. Once per turn: You can target 1 face-up monster on the field; place an Ice Counter on that target. You can Tribute this card; destroy all monsters with an Ice Counter(s).
justification_1: This card’s effect allows for reactive disruption – placing Ice Counters on opponent’s monsters and then destroying them. The ability to activate from the hand during either player’s turn, targeting an opponent’s monster, aligns with the definition of a handtrap designed to interrupt and disrupt the opponent’s plays.
justification_2: This card requires tributing two WATER monsters to be Special Summoned, meaning it cannot be played as a first move. Its primary function is to place Ice Counters which can later lead to a board wipe, extending plays or creating an opportunity to break the opponent's board.

card_text: Add 1 Level 4 or lower Warrior monster from your Deck to your hand.
justification_1: This card directly adds another monster from the Deck to the hand, which is a common starting point for many combos and can be activated on its own to make meaningful progress.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand to add another monster from the Deck to the hand, directly advancing your board presence and initiating your deck's strategy without requiring prior setup.

card_text: This monster can attack your opponent's Life Points directly.
justification_1: This card's effect only allows it to attack directly, which is a basic combat ability and does not fit the activation conditions or strategic roles of any of the defined categories.
justification_2: The card text provided only describes a monster's ability to attack directly. This does not describe an activation condition or a strategic role that fits any of the defined categories (Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, Garnet).

card_text: This card is used to Ritual Summon "Ruin, Queen of Oblivion" or "Demise, King of Armageddon". You must also Tribute monsters from your field whose total Levels equal or exceed the Level of the Ritual Monster you Ritual Summon. During your Main Phase, except the turn this card was sent to the GY: You can shuffle this card into the Deck; add 1 "End of the World" from your Deck to your hand, then, you can add 1 "Ruin, Queen of Oblivion" or "Demise, King of Armageddon" from your GY to your hand.
justification_1: This card enables the Ritual Summon of key monsters and can search for a spell that supports the strategy, allowing the player to begin their combo or establish their win condition.
justification_2: This card's primary effect allows for the Ritual Summon of specific powerful monsters, which is a key component in initiating a combo or line of play. Its ability to search for an additional card ("End of the World") further solidifies its role as a starter, enabling further plays.

card_text: Activate this card by targeting an opponent's monster; equip this card to it. Take control of it. Destroy this card unless you control an "Archfiend" card. During each of your opponent's Standby Phases: You take 800 damage.
justification_1: This card's effect allows you to steal an opponent's monster. While it requires an "Archfiend" card to remain on the field, its primary function is to remove a threat from the opponent's board by taking control of it, making it a board breaker. It does not inherently start a combo or extend existing ones by itself, nor is it a reactive handtrap.
justification_2: This card's effect allows the player to steal an opponent's monster, directly removing a threat from their field, which is characteristic of a board breaker, especially when going second.

card_text: This card can attack your opponent directly, but when it uses this effect, any Battle Damage it inflicts to your opponent is halved. If this face-up card is destroyed while it is in a Monster Card Zone, you can place it face-up in your Spell & Trap Card Zone as a Continuous Spell Card, instead of sending it to the Graveyard.
justification_1: This card’s effect—allowing it to be continuously activated after destruction—is a reactive effect designed to disrupt the opponent’s plays during either player's turn, fitting the definition of a handtrap.
justification_2: This card's effects focus on direct attacking and field presence, not on disrupting the opponent from the hand, starting a combo, extending a combo, or breaking a board. It does not fit the criteria for any of the provided categories.

card_text: If an Xyz Monster on the field would activate its effect by detaching its own Xyz Material(s), its controller can banish 1 card from their hand face-down instead of detaching 1 of those Xyz Materials. Each player can only use this effect once per turn.
justification_1: This card's effect is a conditional replacement for detaching Xyz Materials, which is a general game mechanic rather than a specific activation condition that starts, extends, disrupts, breaks, or is a necessary brick for a combo. It doesn't fit any of the defined categories.
justification_2: This card's effect activates when an Xyz Monster is on the field and is used to detach its own material, allowing for continued plays or resource conservation without needing to be activated from the hand or initiate a combo itself.

card_text: Special Summon 1 "Cyber Dragon" from your hand or GY. It cannot be destroyed by card effects.
justification_1: This card can be used to Special Summon itself from the hand or GY, extending existing plays or recovering from disruptions. It does not initiate a combo on its own but rather adds to an established board or combo.
justification_2: This card can be activated on its own from the hand to Special Summon a "Cyber Dragon," initiating plays by bringing out a key monster without requiring prior setup.

card_text: As long as you control the monster equipped with this card, your opponent cannot use the effect of a Spell, Trap, or Effect Monster that targets 1 monster, except the equipped monster.
justification_1: This card is an Equip Spell and does not have an activation condition that fits the defined categories (Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, Garnet). Its effect is passive protection for an equipped monster.
justification_2: This card provides a continuous protection effect to an equipped monster but does not initiate plays, disrupt the opponent, extend combos, break boards, or act as a necessary but undesirable draw. It simply offers passive protection.

card_text: If you have all 5 of "Exodia the Forbidden One", "Right Arm of the Forbidden One", "Left Arm of the Forbidden One", "Right Leg of the Forbidden One", and "Left Leg of the Forbidden One" in your Graveyard: Special Summon 1 "Exodia Necross" from your hand.
justification_1: The provided card effect describes a win condition for summoning "Exodia Necross" by having specific pieces of Exodia in the Graveyard. It doesn't fit the activation conditions or strategic roles of Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet. It's a condition for a special summon, not a typical combo piece or disruption.
justification_2: This card's effect is a win condition itself, not a starter, extender, handtrap, board breaker, or garnet. It does not initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend plays, break boards, or act as a searchable brick.

card_text: If this card is Special Summoned from the Graveyard: You can banish as many face-up Spell/Trap Cards your opponent controls as possible, and if you do, this card gains 200 ATK and DEF for each card banished. Once per turn, during the Standby Phase, if this card is in the Graveyard because it was sent there last turn: You can target 1 Level 7 or 8 Dragon-Type monster in your Graveyard, except "Arkbrave Dragon"; Special Summon it.
justification_1: The card's primary activation condition is being Special Summoned from the Graveyard, which implies that another effect or card has already set up the Graveyard. Its ability to banish the opponent's Spells/Traps then allows for further plays or disruption after an initial board has been established or the Graveyard has been utilized.
justification_2: This card's primary effect of banishing opponent's Spell/Traps activates when it's Special Summoned from the Graveyard, meaning it requires another card or effect to bring it onto the field first. This allows for further plays or disruption after an initial summon, fitting the role of an extender. The second effect, while powerful, also relies on the card being in the Graveyard from a previous turn, again indicating it's not an opening play itself.

card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
While you control an "Amorphage" monster, neither player can activate cards or effects as Chain Link 2 or higher. Once per turn, during your Standby Phase, Tribute 1 monster or destroy this card.

[ Monster Effect ] 
If this card is Pendulum Summoned or flipped face-up, neither player can Special Summon monsters from the Extra Deck while this card is face-up on the field, except "Amorphage" monsters.
justification_1: This card can be Pendulum Summoned by itself, and its effect immediately restricts the opponent's Extra Deck plays, allowing the player to establish their own board without immediate counter-plays. This ability to initiate a significant board presence and disrupt the opponent from the outset makes it a starter.
justification_2: This card can be Pendulum Summoned to establish a powerful lockdown on the opponent's Extra Deck, allowing for a strong opening turn and setting up future plays without requiring prior board presence.

card_text: Once per turn: You can pay 500 LP; add 1 "Volcanic Shell" from your Deck to your hand. This card must be in the GY to activate and to resolve this effect.
justification_1: This card’s effect is entirely dependent on being in the GY, and only serves to facilitate another card's effect (presumably a card that allows LP manipulation). It’s a “necessary brick” with no inherent strategic value when drawn, aligning with the definition of a “Garnet”.
justification_2: This card's effect, activated from the GY, allows for the addition of a key engine piece ("Volcanic Shell") to the hand. This facilitates the start of a combo by providing access to a necessary card for the engine's core plays.

card_text: If this card is Normal or Special Summoned: You can equip 1 Level 7 or 8 Dragon-Type monster from your hand or Graveyard to this card. This card gains ATK and DEF equal to half the ATK and DEF of the monster equipped to it by this effect. You can Tribute 1 monster and this card, then target 1 Level 7 or 8 Dragon-Type monster in your Graveyard; Special Summon it.
justification_1: This card’s primary effect requires it to be on the field or in the GY to be equipped to another monster, or to be Tributed to revive another monster. This means it needs another play to have already occurred to enable its use, fitting the definition of an extender.
justification_2: This card can be Normal or Special Summoned and then equipped with another monster from the hand or GY, or used to revive another monster from the GY. These effects allow for further combo progression after a play has already started.

card_text:
While you control another "Ancient Warriors" monster, your opponent cannot target this card with card effects, also it cannot be destroyed by your opponent's card effects. You can only use each of the following effects of "Ancient Warriors - Ambitious Cao De" once per turn.
● If a card(s) your opponent controls is destroyed by battle or card effect: You can send 1 other card from your hand or field to the GY; Special Summon this card from your hand.
● When this card destroys an opponent's monster by battle and sends it to the GY: You can Special Summon that monster to your field in Defense Position.
justification_1: The card’s primary Special Summon effect requires an opponent's card to be destroyed, indicating that a play or interaction must have already occurred. This allows "Ancient Warriors - Ambitious Cao De" to be summoned from the hand to continue or extend a combo by adding another monster to the field, fitting the definition of an Extender. Its protection effect also relies on already controlling another monster.
justification_2: The card's primary Special Summon effect requires an opponent's card to be destroyed first, meaning another action or combo must have already been initiated. It then extends your plays by Special Summoning itself and can further extend by stealing an opponent's monster it destroys, fitting the role of a card that continues or pushes plays after a start.

card_text:
1 Tuner + 1 or more non-Tuner monsters
If this card is Special Summoned, or a Level 5 or higher monster(s) is Special Summoned to your opponent's side of the field: Target 1 Special Summoned monster your opponent controls; return that target to the hand. You can only use this effect of "Black Rose Moonlight Dragon" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be Special Summoned using monsters already on the field, and its effect can disrupt the opponent's plays by returning their Special Summoned monsters to the hand, thereby extending your own plays or preventing the opponent from developing their board further.
justification_2: This card's effect is a powerful disruption that triggers upon its own Special Summon or when the opponent Special Summons a monster. While it can be activated in response to your opponent's plays, it primarily serves to extend your board presence and disrupt the opponent's plays by returning a monster to the hand, rather than being a primary disruption activated from the hand. It's a good follow-up or a card that can be summoned as part of a larger combo.

card_text: When this card is Normal Summoned: You can target 1 "Utopia" monster in your Graveyard; Special Summon that target. You can target 1 "Number C39: Utopia Ray V" you control; equip this monster you control to that target. Once per turn, while this card is equipped to a monster: You can detach 1 Xyz Material from the equipped monster; negate the equipped monster's effects (even after this card leaves the field or the monster becomes unaffected by card effects), and if you do, destroy all monsters your opponent controls, and if you do that, inflict 1000 damage to your opponent for each monster destroyed by this effect.
justification_1: This card can be Normal Summoned to Special Summon a "Utopia" monster from the Graveyard, which is a strong opening play that begins a combo by establishing a monster on the field and providing resources.
justification_2: This card’s effect requires an already-established board (a “Utopia” monster) to use, extending the combo by enabling powerful effects and ultimately destroying the opponent's field.

card_text: If your opponent's Life Points are higher than 3000: Inflict 1000 damage to your opponent.
justification_1: This card has a simple effect that inflicts damage based on a condition (opponent's LP). It doesn't fit the criteria of a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet as it neither starts a combo, disrupts the opponent from hand, extends plays, breaks a board, nor is a necessary brick.
justification_2: This card's effect is a simple damage dealing effect that does not activate under specific conditions that define it as a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet. card_name: Number C15: Gimmick Puppet Giant Hunter

3 Level 9 monsters

justification_2: This card's effect is to destroy an opponent's card. While it requires specific materials to summon, its primary function is to remove an established threat on the opponent's field, fitting the definition of a board breaker, especially when going second.

card_text:
2 Level 8 monsters
You can detach 1 material from this card, then target 1 monster on the field; Tribute it. This is a Quick Effect if this card has a Plant monster as material. justification_2: This card's effect allows for the Tributing of a monster on the field, which can remove an opponent's established threat. This is particularly useful when going second and needing to break a board. The Quick Effect aspect further enhances its ability to disrupt the opponent's plays.

card_text: Target face-up cards your opponent controls, up to the number of "Ryzeal" Xyz Monsters you control; destroy them, then you can attach 1 "Ryzeal" card from your GY to 1 Rank 4 Xyz Monster you control. justification_2: The card's primary effect is to target and destroy face-up cards your opponent controls. This is a form of proactive removal designed to eliminate established threats, which is the core function of a Board Breaker. card_text: During damage calculation, in either player's turn, if a "Six Samurai" monster you control battles: You can send this card from your hand to the Graveyard; that monster cannot be destroyed by battle this turn.

justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand during either player's turn, specifically during damage calculation, to protect a "Six Samurai" monster from battle destruction. This fits the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.

card_text: Each time you draw a card(s) for your normal draw in your Draw Phase, place 1 Greed Counter on this card. You can send this card with 2 or more Greed Counters to the GY; draw 2 cards.

justification_2: This card's effect is passive and depends on drawing cards during the Draw Phase. It doesn't initiate plays, disrupt the opponent, extend combos, or break boards on its own. It simply accumulates resources over time.

card_text: If you control no monsters, you can Special Summon this card (from your hand), but its original ATK and DEF become halved. During each of your Standby Phases: Increase the Level of this card by 1. There can only be 1 "Solar Wind Jammer" on the field.

justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand when the player controls no monsters, allowing it to initiate plays by itself and progress towards a win condition.

card_text: If you control no monsters, you can Special Summon this card (from your hand). justification_2: This card can Special Summon itself from the hand if you control no monsters, making it an excellent opening play. It then Tributes itself to Special Summon multiple monsters from the hand/Deck, immediately generating materials for Xyz Summons and allowing the player to begin their main combo line by establishing a significant board presence.

card_text: You can Ritual Summon this card with any "Vendread" Ritual Spell. This card's name becomes "Revendread Slayer" while on the field. While this Ritual Summoned card is on the field, your opponent cannot target cards you control with card effects, except this card. justification_2: This card's effect is a powerful disruption and protection tool once on the field, but it does not have an effect that can be activated from the hand or primarily initiate a combo. Its primary function is to strengthen an established board or recover after being destroyed, fitting neither the Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, nor Garnet categories.

card_text:

justification_2: This card, when Synchro Summoned, provides an immediate ATK boost to all "Gusto" monsters on the field. This effect directly supports a "Gusto" strategy by strengthening the monsters on the field, making it a card that can begin and advance a "Gusto" deck's game plan.

card_text: When this card is sent from your hand or Deck to your Graveyard by an opponent's card effect: Special Summon this card.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand when an opponent's card effect sends it to the Graveyard, allowing for disruption during the opponent's turn and fitting the definition of a handtrap.
justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand in response to an opponent's card effect that sends it from the hand to the Graveyard. This allows it to disrupt the opponent's plays by appearing on the field, fitting the criteria of a reactive disruption from the hand.

card_text: If a Field Spell Card is activated: You can Special Summon this card from your hand, then, if face-up cards are in both Field Zones, this card cannot be destroyed by battle or card effects this turn. You can only use this effect of "Meowseclick" once per turn. During your turn, the effects of the face-up card in your opponent's Field Zone are negated. During your opponent's turn, the effects of the face-up card in your Field Zone are negated.

Once that condition is met, it provides an additional monster on the field, allowing for further Link, Xyz, or Synchro summons, thereby extending existing plays rather than starting them.
justification_2: This card can Special Summon itself from the hand when a Field Spell is activated, which can be done reactively if the opponent activates their Field Spell, or proactively by activating your own. Its primary strategic role is to neutralize the opponent's Field Spell effects during your turn, directly disrupting and weakening their established board or preventing their plays, fitting the definition of a board breaker that answers threats on the opponent's field.

card_text: If you Ritual Summon a DARK Ritual Monster, this 1 card can be used as the entire Tribute.

card_text: Once per turn: You can banish 1 "Genex" monster from your GY; this card gains that monster's Attribute, then if you banished a Tuner to activate this effect, you can treat this card as a Tuner until the end of this turn. card_text: If you control no monsters: You can Special Summon this card from your hand. While its second effect extends plays, its primary ability to initiate from an empty board defines its role as a Starter.
justification_2: While its first effect allows it to be Special Summoned from the hand if you control no monsters, its most impactful ability—Special Summoning up to 2 "Resonator" monsters from the hand or Deck—requires you to already control a DARK Dragon Synchro Monster. This second effect is crucial for continuing and extending your Synchro plays, allowing for further combo progression after a specific monster has been established on the field, fitting the definition of an extender.

card_text: If a face-up Beast monster you control was returned to the hand this turn, except "Melffy Pinny" (Quick Effect): You can Special Summon this card from your hand, then immediately after this effect resolves, Synchro Summon 1 Synchro Monster using only this card you control and "Melffy" monsters in your hand. This effect continues and extends existing plays, especially within the Melffy archetype which frequently returns monsters to hand, allowing the player to build upon their board or react during the opponent's turn by making a Synchro Monster.
justification_2: The card's primary effect to Special Summon itself and Synchro Summon requires a pre-existing condition (a Beast monster being returned to hand this turn), meaning other plays must have already occurred. It then extends the player's board presence and combo potential, fitting the definition of an extender.

card_name: Gate Guardian of Thunder and Wind

Must be Special Summoned (from your Extra Deck) by banishing the above cards you control. You can only use this effect of "Gate Guardian of Thunder and Wind" once per turn. Its effect then allows for a search of a specific Spell/Trap, which extends the combo or helps to progress the archetype's strategy by providing additional resources.
justification_2: This card requires two specific monsters ("Sanga of the Thunder" and "Kazejin") to be already on the field for its Special Summon, meaning it cannot initiate a combo by itself. Once summoned, its effect searches for an archetype-specific Spell/Trap, which then continues or extends the overall strategy by providing further resources or combo pieces, fitting the definition of an extender.

card_text:
2+ Level 4 "Ryzeal" monsters
When your opponent activates a card or effect (Quick Effect): You can detach 1 material from this card, then target 1 card on the field; destroy it. You can only use each of the following effects of "Ryzeal Detonator" once per turn. If this card is Special Summoned: You can attach 1 monster from your GY to this card as material. If an Xyz Monster(s) you control would be destroyed by battle or card effect, you can detach 1 material from this card instead.
justification_1: This card functions as an archetype's "boss monster" or "payoff." It is summoned from the Extra Deck as the goal of a combo, not as a piece that starts or extends it. Its effects provide disruption and protection from the field, a strategic role that does not fit the definitions of Starter, Extender, Handtrap, Board Breaker, or Garnet.
justification_2: This card is an Extra Deck monster that functions as an on-field disruption and protection piece. It requires specific materials to be summoned, meaning it cannot begin a main combo (ruling out Starter). It cannot be activated from the hand (ruling out Handtrap). It does not primarily clear an opponent's established board (ruling out Board Breaker). It is an Extra Deck monster, so it is never drawn (ruling out Garnet). While it provides value and can help deal with interruptions after a combo has started, it does not "continue or extend your plays" by facilitating further summons, searches, or combo progression in the way a typical Extender would (e.g., self-summoning from hand). Its role is more akin to an "on-field control/boss monster" which is not a defined category.

card_text: When your opponent's monster declares an attack, you can remove from play this card from your Graveyard to make the Battle Damage taken by 1 "Gazelle the King of Mythical Beasts" or "Phantom Beast" monster 0. When this happens, that monster is not destroyed by battle.

justification_2: This card can be activated from the Graveyard during the opponent's Battle Phase to negate battle damage, directly disrupting the opponent's offensive plays and protecting a specific monster, fitting the role of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.

card_text: When a monster effect is activated that activates when a monster is Normal Summoned: You can negate the activation, then the player whose effect activation was negated draws 1 card.

justification_2: This effect can be activated from the hand during either player's turn to negate an opponent's monster effect, specifically when a monster is Normal Summoned, fitting the description of a disruptive handtrap.

card_text: Target 1 Spell/Trap Card on the field; destroy it. Once per Chain, when a Trap Card is activated while this card is in your Graveyard: Special Summon this card as a Normal Monster (Aqua-Type/WATER/Level 2/ATK 1200/DEF 0). (This card is NOT treated as a Trap Card.) If Summoned this way, this card is unaffected by monster effects, also banish it when it leaves the field.

justification_2: The card's effect requires a Trap Card to be activated to Special Summon itself from the Graveyard, allowing for continued plays after a Trap Card has been played. It can also be used to destroy a Spell/Trap card on the field, which could be part of an opponent's setup that needs to be cleared to continue your own strategy.

card_text:
