justification_1: The card's most powerful effect activates when it is banished, allowing it to Special Summon itself and destroy multiple cards on the opponent's field. This role is primarily focused on proactively removing the opponent's established threats, which is the definition of a Board Breaker.
justification_2: The card's most powerful effect activates when it is banished, allowing it to Special Summon itself and then destroy multiple cards the opponent controls. This primary function is to eliminate established threats on the opponent's field, fitting the definition of a Board Breaker.

card_text: When you Tribute Summon a monster: You can Special Summon this card from your hand, and if you do, the Type, Attribute, and Level of this card become the same as that face-up Tribute Summoned monster.
justification_1: This card's effect requires a Tribute Summon to have already occurred, allowing for additional plays to be made after a previous action has been completed, fitting the role of an extender.
justification_2: This card’s effect requires a Tribute Summon to have already occurred on your turn, allowing for further plays to be made after an initial summon, thus extending your combo.

card_text: Special Summon this card as an Effect Monster (Machine/EARTH/Level 4/ATK 0/DEF 2000), then, you can equip 1 Level 4 Machine "Gadget" monster from your hand or Deck to this card. (This card is also still a Trap.) If Summoned this way, this card gains ATK equal to double the ATK of the monster equipped to it by this effect.
justification_1: This card is a Trap that Special Summons itself as a Level 4 monster and can equip another Level 4 monster from the Deck. As a Trap, it typically needs to be Set before activation, meaning it cannot begin a main combo on turn 1 going first. However, it provides additional on-field presence and deck thinning/setup for subsequent turns, effectively extending your plays and enabling further summons (like Xyz Summons) after initial actions have been taken.
justification_2: Although a Trap card, which requires it to be Set before activation, this card's effect to Special Summon itself and then equip a Level 4 Machine "Gadget" monster directly from the Deck serves as a powerful resource generation that can *begin* a deck's main combo line. It provides a key monster from the deck, allowing the "Gadget" engine to kickstart its recursive searches and summons, thus making meaningful progress towards the deck's win condition without needing other specific cards already established on the field.

card_text: FLIP: Send 1 EARTH monster from your Deck to the Graveyard.
justification_1: This card has a FLIP effect, meaning it is revealed on the field and can trigger its effect without needing to be paired with another specific card to begin a play. Sending an EARTH monster to the Graveyard often sets up further combos or GY effects.
justification_2: This card's FLIP effect allows it to send an EARTH monster from the Deck to the Graveyard. This can directly start combos by setting up the Graveyard for further plays or interactions, often without needing other cards.

card_text: If this card is sent from the Deck to your GY by a monster effect: You can Special Summon this card, then, if you control another Plant monster, you can halve the ATK/DEF of 1 monster on the field. You can only use this effect of "Fengli the Soldrapom" once per turn. Once per turn, if this card on the field would be destroyed by battle or card effect, you can send 1 Plant monster from your Deck to the GY instead.
justification_1: This card Special Summons itself from the GY when sent from the Deck by a monster effect, requiring another card to initiate the play. It extends an existing combo by adding another monster to the field and potentially facilitating further plays through its self-protection and additional milling effect, rather than starting a combo independently.
justification_2: This card's primary Special Summon effect activates when sent from the Deck to the GY, requiring another card to initiate this action. It cannot start a combo on its own, but rather helps to continue or extend plays after an initial setup (such as a monster effect that mills from the deck) has occurred, fitting the definition of an Extender. Its second effect also supports this by milling further Plant monsters.

card_text: During your Main Phase: You can detach 1 Xyz Material from a "Battlin' Boxer" Xyz Monster you control, and if you do, Special Summon this card from your hand. You can only use this effect of "Battlin' Boxer Shadow" once per turn.
justification_1: This card's effect requires control of another "Battlin' Boxer" Xyz Monster to Special Summon itself from the hand, enabling further plays after an initial setup has been established.

card_text: If your opponent controls a monster: You can discard this card; Set 1 "Fusion" Continuous Spell directly from your Deck, also you cannot Special Summon monsters from the Extra Deck for the rest of this turn, except "Gem-Knight" monsters. Going second, this card reliably begins your main combo line by providing the essential "Gem-Knight Fusion" card, making meaningful progress towards the deck's win condition without needing other specific cards to initiate the core engine. While its second effect is an extender (recovering resources after a Fusion Summon), its first effect is crucial for starting the deck's primary strategy.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand by discarding itself to directly Set a crucial "Fusion" Continuous Spell from the Deck. This immediately enables the deck's main combo line, particularly when going second where the condition "If your opponent controls a monster" is easily met. The restriction to "Gem-Knight" Extra Deck monsters reinforces its role in initiating the archetype's dedicated strategy.

card_text: During the Main Phase: Immediately after this effect resolves, Synchro Summon 1 Synchro Monster using monsters you control as material. During your Main Phase, except the turn this card was sent to the GY: You can banish this card from your GY, then target 1 Synchro Monster in your GY; return it to the Extra Deck, then draw 1 card. You can only use each effect of "Synchro Transmission" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's primary effect allows for a Synchro Summon using monsters already on your field, meaning it cannot initiate a combo by itself. justification_2: The card's primary effect allows a Synchro Summon using monsters already on the field, meaning it cannot initiate a combo from an empty board but rather continues or extends plays after monsters have been established. card_text: This card cannot be Normal Summoned or Flip Summoned unless you have an Archfiend Monster Card on your side of the field. If the result is 2 or 5, negate the effect and destroy the opponent's card. The effects of an Effect Monster that this card destroyed as a result of battle are negated.
justification_1: The card's effects are passive or require specific conditions that don't align with the definitions of Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet. justification_2: This card's effects are primarily passive or conditional (requiring an Archfiend on the field, rolling a die for negation, and negating destroyed monsters). It doesn't fit the definition of a starter, handtrap, extender, board breaker, or garnet as it doesn't initiate plays, disrupt opponents from hand, extend existing combos, break boards, or function as a necessary brick.

card_text: You cannot Summon monsters. You can only control 1 "Ido the Supreme Magical Force". If this card is destroyed by a card effect and sent to the Graveyard, Special Summon it from your Graveyard during the next turn's Standby Phase and destroy all other monsters you control.

justification_2: This card can begin a deck's main strategy by directly Ritual Summoning any Ritual Monster from the Deck, providing immediate access to a key monster without needing to draw it first, even though it requires specific Normal Monster tributes from hand.

card_name: Farfa, Malebranche of the Burning Abyss

justification_2: This card's effect is a simple gain of Life Points based on a condition already on the field. It does not initiate plays, disrupt the opponent, extend combos, break boards, or act as a necessary but undesirable combo piece.

card_text:
When an opponent's monster declares an attack: Roll a six-sided die and apply the result.
1: Halve your LP.
2: Make that attack a direct attack.
3: Choose 1 monster you control, change the attack target to it, and perform damage calculation.
4: Choose 1 other monster your opponent controls, change the attack target to it, and perform damage calculation.
5: Negate the attack, and if you do, inflict damage to your opponent equal to that monster's ATK.
6: Destroy that opponent's monster.
justification_1: This card's effect can be activated during the opponent's turn when they declare an attack, directly disrupting their offensive plays. While it doesn't negate, it offers a variety of disruptive outcomes that align with the strategic role of a handtrap.
justification_2: This card's effect can be activated during the opponent's turn when their monster declares an attack, allowing for disruption and interaction without requiring the card to be on the field. This fits the definition of a disruptive handtrap.

card_text: If you control exactly 1 Tuner and no other monsters: Special Summon 1 Level 4 or lower "Speedroid" monster from your Deck. Neither player can activate cards or effects when that monster is Special Summoned.
justification_1: This card can be activated by itself to Special Summon another "Speedroid" monster from the Deck, initiating a combo and progressing your plays from the very beginning.
justification_2: This card can be activated on its own during the Main Phase to Special Summon a monster from the Deck, initiating a combo and making meaningful progress towards a win condition.

card_text: Once per turn, you can activate this effect to remove from play the top card of your Deck and give this card 500 ATK until the End Phase. justification_2: This card's effects do not fit the criteria for Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet. Its first effect provides a minor ATK boost with a self-banish, and its second effect is a GY recovery effect that triggers on destruction, neither of which are primary combo starters, disruption tools, combo extenders, board breakers, or "necessary bricks."

Its primary function is to be a powerful monster on the field after being summoned, rather than enabling a combo, disrupting the opponent, or breaking a board with a specific trigger.

card_text: If a card(s) is sent from the hand and/or Deck to the GY: Target 1 card in either GY; shuffle it into the Deck. You can only activate 1 "Sweet Roommaid" per turn.

Its second effect then recycles or revives a Cyberse monster from the GY after being used as Link Material, further extending plays and generating value after a combo has begun. card_text:
"Cyberdark Dragon" + "Cyber End Dragon"
Must be either Fusion Summoned, or Special Summoned by Tributing 1 Level 10 or lower "Cyberdark" Fusion Monster equipped with "Cyber End Dragon". Unaffected by your opponent's activated effects. Once per turn: You can equip 1 monster from either GY to this card. This card can attack a number of times each Battle Phase, up to the number of Equip Cards equipped to it.
justification_1: This card serves as a final "boss monster" or "win condition" for the "Cyberdark" archetype. It requires significant setup and specific materials (two other Fusion Monsters) to be summoned, meaning it cannot begin a play (not a Starter). It has no quick effect for disruption from the hand (not a Handtrap). It does not directly clear the opponent's established board (not a Board Breaker). While it requires something to have started, its role is not to "extend" or "continue" combo lines to facilitate more summons or resources, but rather to be the powerful, protected payoff that aims to end the game (not an Extender in the typical sense). It is the desired outcome of a complex play, not an undesirable draw (not a Garnet). Therefore, it does not fit neatly into any of the provided categories.
justification_2: This card is a powerful boss monster that serves as a win condition or a major end-board piece. It requires significant setup (Fusion Summoning or a very specific Tributed monster condition) and cannot initiate plays by itself (not a Starter). It is not activated from the hand to disrupt the opponent (not a Handtrap). While it requires prior plays, it is typically the final step of a combo, not a card that continues to extend into further plays (not an Extender). It does not proactively eliminate or answer established threats on the opponent's field upon activation (not a Board Breaker). Lastly, it is a desired outcome of the deck's strategy, not an undesirable "brick" necessary for other combos (not a Garnet). Therefore, it does not fit any of the defined categories.

card_text:

card_text:
3 Level 8 monsters
You can also Xyz Summon this card by using a "Galaxy-Eyes" Xyz Monster you control as material, except "Galaxy-Eyes Full Armor Photon Dragon". (Transfer its materials to this card.) Once per turn: You can target up to 2 Equip Cards equipped to this card; attach them to this card as material. Once per turn: You can detach 1 material from this card, then target 1 face-up card your opponent controls; destroy it.
justification_1: This card's primary interactive effect allows it to destroy a face-up card controlled by the opponent once per turn by detaching a material. This is a proactive measure to remove threats on the opponent's board, fitting the definition of a board breaker, especially when it's summoned when going second.
justification_2: This card's primary effect allows for the destruction of an opponent's face-up card by detaching a material, which directly addresses and removes an opponent's established threat, fitting the role of a board breaker.

card_text: When this card is destroyed by battle and sent to the Graveyard: You can Special Summon 1 Dragon-Type monster with 1500 or less ATK from your Deck.
justification_1: This card’s effect allows for a straightforward opening play – Special Summoning a Dragon monster from the deck, which is a fundamental and desirable action for a Dragon-based deck, thus fitting the definition of a starter.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the deck through other cards, and its destruction on the field can lead to special summoning another monster, contributing to the start of a combo by bringing out another piece.

card_text: Send, from your side of the field to the Graveyard, the Fusion Material Monsters that are listed on a "Gem-Knight" Fusion Monster Card, then Special Summon that Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck. (This Special Summon is treated as a Fusion Summon.) When you do: Banish this card from the Graveyard to target 1 "Gem-Knight" Fusion Material Monster used for the Fusion Summon; the Fusion Summoned monster gains that monster's ATK until the End Phase.
justification_1: This card initiates plays by allowing the Fusion Summon of a "Gem-Knight" monster directly from the Extra Deck using monsters from the field, acting as a primary method to start the deck's core strategy.
justification_2: This card initiates plays by facilitating a Fusion Summon from the Extra Deck, allowing the player to begin their combo by bringing out a "Gem-Knight" Fusion Monster.

card_text: Target 3 "Harpie Lady" and/or "Harpie Lady Sisters" in your GY; shuffle them into the Deck, then draw 1 card. If you controlled a Level 5 or higher "Harpie" monster when you activated this effect, draw 2 cards instead. For the rest of this turn after this card resolves, you cannot Special Summon monsters, except WIND monsters. You can only activate 1 "Harpie's Feather Rest" per turn.
justification_1: This card can be activated on its own to shuffle specific monsters from the GY into the deck and draw cards, which is a key step to initiate plays for a Harpie strategy and improve hand advantage from the beginning of the turn.
justification_2: This card can be activated on its own to recycle key monsters in the GY and draw cards, directly advancing your game plan and enabling further plays by drawing into combo pieces.

card_text: Target 1 Dragon Xyz Monster you control; Special Summon from your Extra Deck, 1 "Hieratic" Xyz Monster with a different original name, by using that target as material. (This is treated as an Xyz Summon. Transfer its materials to the Summoned monster.) You can banish this card from your GY, then target 1 "Hieratic" monster in your GY; Special Summon it in Defense Position. You can only use 1 "Hieratic Seal of Creation" effect per turn, and only once that turn.
justification_1: The card's primary effect requires a Dragon Xyz Monster already on the field to perform another Xyz Summon, thus extending an existing play. Its secondary GY effect also facilitates further summons, continuing or extending plays after the card has been used.
justification_2: This card's primary effect requires an existing Dragon Xyz Monster on the field to Special Summon another Xyz Monster, thereby extending an established play. Its secondary GY effect allows for the Special Summon of a Hieratic monster from the GY, which further extends plays or helps recover resources. Both effects require prior conditions to be met, fitting the role of an Extender that continues or pushes through plays.

card_text: Discard 1 card, then target 1 of your "Mayakashi" monsters that is banished or in your GY; Special Summon it. Your opponent cannot target it with card effects this turn. You cannot Special Summon monsters from the Extra Deck the turn you activate this card, except "Mayakashi" monsters. You can only activate 1 "Mayakashi Metamorphosis" per turn.
justification_1: This card's effect requires a "Mayakashi" monster in the GY or banished to Special Summon, which implies a play has already been initiated, allowing for further combo extension or recovery.
justification_2: This card allows for an immediate Special Summon of a "Mayakashi" monster from the hand or GY, initiating a key combo and providing a strong opening play, thus fitting the definition of a starter card.

card_text: If your opponent controls a monster, and all monsters you control are face-up "Gimmick Puppet" monsters (min. 1), you can Special Summon this card (from your hand).
justification_1: This card’s effect directly enables a specific opening play – Special Summoning itself – contingent on controlling a set number of “Gimmick Puppet” monsters, making it a clear starter card for that archetype.
justification_2: This card's Special Summon condition requires the presence of "Gimmick Puppet" monsters on your field, meaning it cannot start a play by itself but can extend an existing board of "Gimmick Puppet" monsters.

card_text: If this card is Normal Summoned: You can target 1 Union monster in your GY; Special Summon it. Once per turn, you can either: Target 1 Machine monster you control; equip this card to that target, OR: Unequip this card and Special Summon it. Your opponent cannot target a monster equipped with this card with card effects, also if the equipped monster would be destroyed by battle or card effect, destroy this card instead.
justification_1: This card's primary effect is to Special Summon a Union monster from the GY when Normal Summoned. This requires the player to already have a Union monster in the GY, indicating that another card or effect has already initiated the play. It then provides further utility by allowing itself to be equipped to or Special Summoned to extend plays.
justification_2: This card's primary effect to Special Summon a Union monster from the GY is activated upon Normal Summon. This implies that a monster has already been Summoned to the field, making it an extender that continues plays rather than starting them.

card_text: Tribute 1 LIGHT or DARK monster; Special Summon 1 LIGHT or DARK monster from your hand or Extra Deck with the same original Type and Level, but a different original Attribute. During your Main Phase: You can banish this card from your GY, then target 1 LIGHT and 1 DARK monster in your GY; shuffle both into the Deck, then draw 1 card. You can only use each effect of "Duality" once per turn.
justification_1: "Duality" requires a monster on the field to activate its primary effect (tributing a LIGHT or DARK monster to Special Summon another). This means it cannot initiate a combo from an empty board but instead extends or continues an existing play by swapping monsters, often facilitating further summons or combo progression. Its second effect also recycles resources after the card has been used, further supporting an existing strategy.
justification_2: The first effect of "Duality" requires a monster already on the field to be Tributed, meaning it cannot initiate a combo by itself. Instead, it allows for further plays by Special Summoning another monster (often a specific combo piece or a boss monster) from the hand or Extra Deck, effectively extending an existing board or combo line. Its second effect also recycles resources and draws, supporting sustained plays.

card_text:
If this card is Summoned: Activate 1 of these effects.
● Target 1 Equip Card on the field; destroy that target.
● Target 1 Equip Card on the field; equip that target to this card.
justification_1: This card's effect activates upon its Summoning, but it does not provide a strategic advantage as a starter, extender, handtrap, or board breaker in typical Yu-Gi-Oh! play. It does not fit the defined roles for any of the given categories.
justification_2: This card's effect activates upon being Summoned, indicating it needs to be on the field to initiate its effect. Its ability to manipulate Equip Cards suggests it's intended to extend existing plays or enable further strategies rather than start them independently.

card_text: This card can only be activated when both players have 2 monsters or less on their respective sides of the field. Both players can only Normal Summon up to 2 additional monsters on their sides of the field.
justification_1: This card’s effect is triggered by a specific board state (both players having 2 or fewer monsters), representing a reactive disruption. It’s a handtrap designed to disrupt the opponent’s plays when they are limited in their monster deployment, fitting the definition of a disruptive handtrap.
justification_2: This card's effect is situational and aims to disrupt the opponent's ability to establish a board by limiting their summons. It is most impactful when the opponent has already built a board, allowing the user to potentially break it or prevent further development, fitting the role of a board breaker.

card_text: A ferocious animal that sleeps all day. Sometimes it's better to let Sleeping Lions lie.
justification_1: This card’s effect is entirely reliant on another card’s activation (presumably a card that triggers based on a monster being asleep). It doesn't contribute to an independent strategy and is likely included only to enable another effect, making it a “necessary brick” – undesirable to draw.
justification_2: The card’s effect is entirely reliant on another card's activation (implied by "sometimes it's better to let Sleeping Lions lie"), serving only to facilitate a combo/engine and being an undesirable card to draw, aligning with the definition of a 'Garnet'.

card_text:
2 Machine monsters
Cannot be used as Link Material the turn it is Link Summoned. During your Main Phase: You can Special Summon 1 Level 4 or lower Machine monster from your hand to your zone this card points to. If this card is destroyed by battle or card effect: You can Special Summon 1 Level 4 "Gadget" monster from your Deck. You can only use each effect of "Platinum Gadget" once per turn.
justification_1: The card requires other monsters to be on the field to be summoned (via Link Summon) and then can Special Summon another monster from the hand, extending existing plays. Its second effect, which summons a "Gadget" from the Deck upon destruction, also functions as a way to continue plays after a disruption.
justification_2: This card requires other Machine monsters to be on the field for its Link Summon, and its effect to Special Summon another monster from hand continues existing plays rather than starting them.

card_text: If this card battles a monster, neither can be destroyed by that battle. At the end of the Damage Step, if this card battled an opponent's monster: You can take control of that opponent's monster. Once per turn, at the start of the Damage Step, if another monster attacks: You can destroy 1 card on the field.
justification_1: The card's primary effects, taking control of an opponent's monster after battle and destroying a card when another monster attacks, are focused on neutralizing and removing opponent's established threats, which aligns with the definition of a board breaker.
justification_2: This card excels at neutralizing opponent's established threats through battle, either by taking control of an opponent's monster it battled, or by destroying a card on the field when another monster attacks. These effects are primarily reactive and focus on dismantling the opponent's board, fitting the role of a board breaker.

card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
If this card is Synchro Summoned: You can activate this effect; during the End Phase of this turn, send 1 Equip Spell from your Deck to the GY, then add 1 Warrior monster from your Deck to your hand. During the Main Phase, if this card is in your GY (Quick Effect): You can target 1 Warrior monster you control; equip this card to that monster you control as an Equip Spell that gives it 500 ATK. You can only use each effect of "Infernoble Knight Captain Roland" once per turn.
justification_1: While its Synchro Summon effect can search a Warrior monster, which is a good starting point, its primary role appears to be extending plays. The Quick Effect to equip itself from the GY to a Warrior monster provides an ATK boost and can facilitate further plays or protect that monster, implying it comes online after other monsters are already on the field.
justification_2: While this card can be Synchro Summoned to start a play, its primary utility in the GY as a Quick Effect to equip itself and boost ATK, or to trigger its GY effect to add a Warrior monster to hand, serves to extend existing combos or provide follow-up plays after a disruption. It doesn't typically initiate a combo by itself but supports ongoing strategies.

card_text: If this card is destroyed by battle and sent to the Graveyard: Target 1 Plant-Type monster in your Graveyard, except "Lord Poison"; Special Summon that target.
justification_1: This card can only be activated after it has been sent to the Graveyard, typically from a previous play or effect, allowing for further combo extension by Special Summoning another monster.
justification_2: This card’s effect requires a Plant-Type monster to be in the Graveyard in order to activate, extending plays and offering additional value after a core combo has been established.

card_text: If this card is sent to the Graveyard for the Synchro Summon of a Dragon-Type monster: That monster gains 800 ATK, also banish that face-up monster during the End Phase.
justification_1: This card's effect is solely designed to facilitate the Synchro Summon of another card; it doesn't contribute to the player’s overall strategy once the Synchro Summon has occurred, aligning with the definition of a “Garnet.”
justification_2: This card's effect only triggers when it is sent to the Graveyard for a Synchro Summon, meaning it has no inherent activation condition that allows it to be a Starter, Handtrap, or Board Breaker on its own. It also doesn't extend plays or act as a combo piece that is undesirable to draw.

card_text:
1 "Ojama" monster + 1 "Ojama" monster
Select up to 2 of your opponent's Monster Card Zones. The selected zones cannot be used.
justification_1: This card’s effect allows you to disrupt your opponent’s plays by taking control of their monster zones, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap. The activation condition (opponent’s monster zones) makes it a reactive effect triggered during the opponent’s turn.
justification_2: This card is a Fusion Monster that requires specific materials and has an effect that manipulates the opponent's Monster Card Zones. It doesn't fit the criteria for a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet as its primary role is not to initiate plays, disrupt from hand, extend combos, break boards, or serve as a necessary but undesirable draw. Its effect is a board control/disruption tool but is not typically activated from the hand nor is it a primary combo-enabler.

card_text: You can target 1 Ritual Monster you control; Special Summon 1 "Nouvelles Token" (Fiend/DARK/Level 1/ATK 50/DEF 50). This Token's Level becomes the same as that monster's. You can send this face-up card from your Spell & Trap Zone to the GY; Ritual Summon 1 "Nouvelles" Ritual Monster from your hand by Tributing monsters from your hand or field whose total Levels exactly equal the Level of that Ritual Monster. You can only use each effect of "Recette de Personnel (Staff Recipe)" once per turn.
justification_1: This card's effects are used to continue and build upon an existing board state rather than initiating one. Its first effect requires you to already control a Ritual Monster to generate a Token, and its second effect performs a Ritual Summon after this card is already on the field. Both effects serve to extend your plays after your initial setup has begun, not start them from scratch.
justification_2: This card's effects require you to have already started your plays. The first effect needs a Ritual Monster on the field to generate a Token, and the second effect provides an additional Ritual Summon from the field. Both effects serve to continue or expand your board state after an initial play has been made, rather than starting a combo from scratch, fitting the role of an extender.

card_text:
(This card is always treated as an "Archfiend" card.)
The equipped monster gains 1000 ATK. When this card is sent from the field to the Graveyard: You can Tribute 1 monster; place this card on the top of your Deck.
justification_1: This card provides a stat boost and a recursive effect, but it does not directly initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend existing plays, break a board, or function as a necessary brick. Its primary utility is enhancing a monster or returning to the deck, neither of which directly fits the defined categories.
justification_2: This card's effects do not activate from the hand, nor do they directly start or extend a combo, break a board, or serve as a mandatory, undesirable brick for an engine. Its effects are passive buffs and a graveyard recursion effect that doesn't fit the specific roles defined.

card_text: Tribute 1 non-Token Insect monster; Special Summon up to 1 "Beetrooper Token" (Insect/EARTH/Level 3/ATK 1000/DEF 1000) for every 1000 original ATK of that Tributed monster. You can only activate "Beetrooper Squad" once per turn.
justification_1: This card requires an existing non-Token Insect monster on the field to activate, meaning it cannot start a combo from an empty board. Its effect converts an already established monster into multiple tokens, which can then be used to continue or extend plays, such as for Link Summons, after an initial play has been made.
justification_2: This card requires an Insect monster to already be on the field to activate, converting it into multiple tokens. This allows for further Special Summons (e.g., Link, Synchro, Xyz) by providing additional materials, thereby continuing or extending plays after an initial summon has occurred.

card_text: During damage calculation, if your opponent's monster attacks (Quick Effect): You can discard this card; you take no battle damage from that battle.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand during the opponent's turn in response to a battle, allowing the player to take no battle damage. This disruption from hand aligns with the definition of a handtrap.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand during the opponent's turn, specifically during damage calculation, to mitigate battle damage. Its ability to disrupt the opponent's offensive pressure without being on the field classifies it as a handtrap.

card_text: Neither player can activate Spell Cards during Main Phase 1.
justification_1: This card's effect is a continuous negation that affects both players without a specific activation trigger or a defined strategic role as a starter, extender, board breaker, or handtrap. It doesn't fit neatly into any of the provided categories.
justification_2: This card's effect is a continuous effect that restricts both players from activating Spell Cards during Main Phase 1. It doesn't fit the criteria for starting a combo, disrupting the opponent from the hand, extending plays, breaking a board, or being a necessary but undesirable draw.

card_text: You can send this card from your hand or field to the GY; Special Summon 1 "Elemental HERO" monster or 1 "Neo-Spacian" monster from your hand or GY, but negate its effects if Summoned from the GY. If you Special Summon an "Elemental HERO" Fusion Monster, while this card is in your GY (except during the Damage Step): You can banish this card; draw 2 cards, then place 1 card from your hand on the bottom of the Deck. You can only use each effect of "Cross Keeper" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be sent from the hand to Special Summon an "Elemental HERO" or "Neo-Spacian" monster from the hand or GY. This allows a player to establish a monster on the field without consuming their Normal Summon, effectively initiating or significantly advancing their main combo line, making it a strong opening play.
justification_2: The first effect facilitates the Special Summon of a "HERO" or "Neo-Spacian" monster, which helps continue or extend plays by providing more material for Fusion or Link Summons after something has already started. The second effect provides draw power after an "Elemental HERO" Fusion Monster has been summoned, allowing for resource replenishment and further combo progression, fitting the definition of an extender.

card_text: Equip only to an "Ancient Gear" monster. At the end of the Damage Step, if the equipped monster battled a monster and is still on the field: Destroy the monster it battled.
justification_1: This card’s effect is used to eliminate an opponent’s monster after a battle, directly targeting and destroying a threat on the field – a classic board-breaking strategy.
justification_2: This card is an Equip Spell with no activation condition that can be initiated from the hand or field. Its effect triggers only after a battle has concluded and the equipped monster remains on the field. It does not initiate plays, disrupt opponents, extend combos, or break boards on its own, nor is it a desirable brick for an engine. Therefore, it does not fit any of the defined categories.

card_text:
(This card is not treated as a "Valkyrie" card.)
When this card is Normal Summoned, if your opponent controls a monster and you control no other cards: You can banish 2 "Nordic" monsters from your hand; Special Summon 2 "Einherjar Tokens" (Warrior/EARTH/Level 4/ATK 1000/DEF 1000) in Defense Position.
justification_1: This card can be Normal Summoned as the first play if your opponent controls a monster and you control no cards, and its effect allows it to special summon other monsters, thus starting your combo.
justification_2: This card can begin a combo by Special Summoning itself and then "Einherjar Tokens" if specific conditions are met (opponent controls a monster, you control no other cards, and you have 2 "Nordic" monsters in hand). This ability to establish a board presence from a relatively open state, by using cards from the hand to summon monsters, fits the definition of a starter.

card_text: When this card inflicts Battle Damage to your opponent: Your opponent sends 1 Spell Card from their Deck to the Graveyard.
justification_1: This card's effect triggers when it inflicts battle damage. It does not initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend plays, break boards, nor is it a necessary-but-undesirable draw. It is a reward for a successful attack.
justification_2: This card's effect triggers based on inflicting battle damage, which is a game state outcome rather than a specific activation condition that aligns with the defined categories. It doesn't initiate a combo, disrupt from the hand, extend plays, break boards, or serve as a desirable-to-draw piece for another card.

card_text: Target 1 Synchro Monster you control; return it to the Extra Deck, and if you do, destroy all face-up monsters your opponent controls with ATK less than or equal to that Synchro Monster's original ATK.
justification_1: This card's effect is designed to clear the opponent's established board by destroying their monsters based on the ATK of a Synchro Monster you control. It is most effective when going second to dismantle the opponent's setup.
justification_2: This card's effect targets a monster on your own field to destroy your opponent's monsters, making it primarily useful for clearing an opponent's established board, especially when going second.

card_text: During the Main Phase: Immediately after this effect resolves, Normal Summon 1 Level 4 or lower Winged Beast monster. If you Tribute Summon a Level 7 or higher monster while this card is in your GY: You can banish this card; change all monsters your opponent controls to face-down Defense Position. You can only use this effect of "Floowandereeze and the Dreaming Town" once per turn.
justification_1: The primary effect allows for an additional Normal Summon, which is crucial for extending plays in a deck focused on multiple Normal Summons, allowing the player to push further into their combo. The second effect, which flips opponent's monsters, provides additional value and control after a Tribute Summon, further extending the impact of a play rather than initiating it.
justification_2: The card's primary effect grants an additional Normal Summon for a Level 4 or lower Winged Beast monster during the Main Phase. In a Floowandereeze deck, which heavily relies on chaining multiple Normal Summons to establish its board and win condition, this effect can be used as the initial play to begin the main combo line, especially if the player has a Floowandereeze monster in hand to summon. It directly facilitates the deck's core strategy from the start of the turn.

card_text: Once per turn: You can target up to 2 monsters your opponent controls; your opponent can reveal 1 monster in their hand to negate this card's effect, otherwise destroy those monsters.
justification_1: This card's effect allows the player to destroy opponent's monsters if they cannot respond, which is typically used to clear an established board and is most effective when going second.
justification_2: This card's effect directly targets and destroys opponent's monsters, aiming to clear their established board. While it can be negated by the opponent revealing a monster from their hand, its primary function is to dismantle the opponent's field, fitting the description of a board breaker. It doesn't initiate plays, extend combos, or disrupt from the hand.

card_text:
FLIP: You can target up to 2 Spells/Traps your opponent controls; return them to the hand.
You can target 1 "Penguin" monster you control; change it to face-down Defense Position. You can only use this effect of "Penguin Ninja" once per turn.
justification_1: The second effect of "Penguin Ninja" allows you to change a "Penguin" monster you control to face-down Defense Position. This is a key utility effect within the "Penguin" archetype, as it enables the re-use of FLIP effects from other "Penguin" monsters (or "Penguin Ninja" itself), or helps to protect them. This clearly fits the definition of an Extender, as it continues or extends your plays by generating more value from your existing board or setting up future interactions after an initial play has been made. While the FLIP effect provides disruption, the card's overall strategic role, especially with its archetypal support effect, is to facilitate and extend internal combo lines.
justification_2: The primary FLIP effect of "Penguin Ninja" allows it to return up to 2 of the opponent's Spells/Traps to the hand, directly addressing and clearing established threats on the opponent's field. While it's a FLIP effect and thus slower than many dedicated going-second board breakers, its strategic role is to neutralize the opponent's backrow, aligning with the definition of a card used to eliminate or answer established threats.

card_text: If your opponent Special Summons a monster(s): You can change as many Attack Position "Superheavy Samurai" monsters you control as possible to Defense Position, and if you do, the ones changed by this effect gain 500 DEF until the end of this turn.
justification_1: This card's effect can be activated from the hand when the opponent Special Summons a monster, allowing for disruption of their plays and a change in the battle positions of your monsters. This fits the definition of a handtrap as it interacts with the opponent during their turn from the hand.
justification_2: This card's effect is activated in response to the opponent's Special Summon, allowing for the repositioning and boosting of your own "Superheavy Samurai" monsters. It helps continue your plays or recover from an opponent's summon, rather than initiating your own combo.

card_text: Equip only to a Warrior monster. You can only control 1 "Noble Arms - Clarent". Once per turn: You can pay 500 LP; the equipped monster can attack directly this turn. If this face-up card on the field is destroyed and sent to the GY: You can target 1 Warrior "Noble Knight" monster you control; equip this card to that target. You can only use this effect of "Noble Arms - Clarent" once per turn.
justification_1: This card is an Equip Spell that requires a Warrior monster to be on the field to be equipped. Its effect to allow direct attacks can extend an offensive turn, and the ability to re-equip itself from the GY after being destroyed allows for continued advantage or resource loop within an established board or combo. It does not initiate plays on its own but enhances existing ones.
justification_2: This card provides a benefit (direct attacks) to an equipped monster, extending the utility of existing monsters on the field and potentially creating further offensive pressure or enabling OTKs. It doesn't initiate plays on its own, nor is it a disruptive handtrap or a dedicated board breaker.

card_text:
2 or more Level 4 monsters
Once per turn, you can also Xyz Summon "Zoodiac Chakanine" by using 1 "Zoodiac" monster you control with a different name as Xyz Material. (If you used an Xyz Monster, any Xyz Materials attached to it also become Xyz Materials on this card.) This card gains ATK and DEF equal to the ATK and DEF of all "Zoodiac" monsters attached to it as Materials. Once per turn: You can detach 1 Xyz Material from this card, then target 1 "Zoodiac" monster in your Graveyard; Special Summon it, but for the rest of this turn, it has its effects negated, also it cannot be used as an Xyz Material for a Summon.
justification_1: This card requires existing monsters on the field to be Xyz Summoned and then uses its effect to revive another monster, extending existing plays rather than initiating them.
justification_2: This card requires other "Zoodiac" monsters to be on the field to be Xyz Summoned and its effect to Special Summon another "Zoodiac" monster from the Graveyard extends existing plays, making it an extender.

card_text: When a monster(s) would be Summoned, OR a Spell/Trap Card is activated: Pay half your LP; negate the Summon or activation, and if you do, destroy that card.
justification_1: This card's effect can be activated from the hand during either player's turn when a Summon or Spell/Trap activation occurs, negating it and destroying the card. This disruption from the hand, independent of board presence, fits the definition of a Handtrap.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand during either player's turn to negate a summon or spell/trap activation, disrupting the opponent's plays.

card_text:
2 Xyz Monsters with the same Rank
(This card's original Rank is always treated as 1.)
Gains ATK/DEF equal to the combined Ranks of all Xyz Monsters you control and in your opponent's GY x 500. Your opponent's monsters cannot target monsters for attacks, except this one, also your opponent cannot target other cards on the field with card effects. Once per turn, when your opponent activates a card or effect on the field (Quick Effect): You can detach 1 material from this card; take control of 1 monster your opponent controls, also for the rest of this turn, this card cannot be destroyed by battle or card effects.
justification_1: This card is a "boss monster," summoned as the end goal of a combo, not as a component to start or extend it. Its summoning condition requires an already established board of two Xyz monsters, disqualifying it as a Starter. Its role is to establish a powerful on-field presence with protection and a Quick Effect to disrupt the opponent, not to continue your own combo chain (Extender) or to be used reactively from the hand (Handtrap). While it can remove an opponent's monster, its primary function is as a proactive setup on your turn, not as a tool to dismantle an existing board (Board Breaker).
justification_2: This card's Quick Effect is activated on the opponent's turn to take control of one of their monsters. The strategic role of this effect is to eliminate a threat and disrupt the opponent's board, directly aligning with the definition of a Board Breaker.

card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
If "Vaylantz World - Shinra Bansho" is in a Field Zone or if you control a WATER "Vaylantz" monster: You can Special Summon this card to your Main Monster Zone in its same column. You can only use this effect of "Nazuki the Vaylantz Ninja" once per turn.

[ Monster Effect ] 
You can target 1 other monster in either Main Monster Zone; move that monster to an adjacent (horizontal) Monster Zone. If this card in the Monster Zone moves to another Monster Zone: You can target 1 "Vaylantz" Monster Card in your Spell & Trap Zone; Special Summon it to your Main Monster Zone in its same column. You can only use each effect of "Nazuki the Vaylantz Ninja" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's Pendulum Effect requires a pre-existing condition (a specific Field Spell or a WATER "Vaylantz" monster on the field) to Special Summon itself. It also isn't a necessary but undesirable combo piece (Garnet). Its role is primarily defensive and reactive during the opponent's Battle Phase, which doesn't align with the defined categories.

card_text: If this card battles a LIGHT monster, it gains 700 ATK during the Damage Step only.
justification_1: This card’s effect is directly triggered by an opponent’s action (battling with a Light monster), and its purpose is to disrupt the opponent's board and strategic advantage, fitting the role of a board breaker.
justification_2: The provided text only describes a battle-related ATK boost under a specific condition (battling a LIGHT monster). It does not have an activation condition that aligns with the defined categories of Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet, nor does it inherently represent a strategic role within those categories.

card_text: If you control a "Salamangreat" card: You can discard this card, then target 1 face-up monster on the field; it gains 500 ATK until the end of this turn. If you control a "Salamangreat" monster other than "Salamangreat Spinny" and this card is in your GY: You can Special Summon this card, but banish it when it leaves the field. You can only use each effect of "Salamangreat Spinny" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be Special Summoned from the GY if a "Salamangreat" monster is on the field, allowing it to extend existing plays or revive a previously used monster, fitting the definition of an extender. The discard effect can also be used to boost an existing monster.
justification_2: This card can be special summoned from the GY if a "Salamangreat" monster is already on the field, allowing for further plays and extending combos. While it can discard itself for an ATK boost, its primary strategic role is to be summoned to continue a board presence after an initial play.

card_text: When this card in your possession is destroyed by your opponent's card (either by battle or by card effect) and sent to your Graveyard: Shuffle this card into the Deck. When this card is Special Summoned: You can add 1 "Madolche" Spell/Trap Card from your Deck to your hand. You must control a face-up Beast-Type "Madolche" monster to activate and to resolve this effect.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand when summoned to search for a key Spell/Trap, directly advancing the deck's game plan without requiring prior setup, fitting the definition of a starter.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand when Special Summoned to search for a crucial Spell/Trap for the "Madolche" archetype, directly initiating a combo by adding essential resources.

card_text: Once per turn: You can pay 1000 LP, then target 1 face-up monster your opponent controls and 1 Psychic-Type monster you control, except "Psychic Jumper"; switch control of both those targets, and if you do, their battle positions cannot be changed this turn.
justification_1: This card's effect allows you to switch control of an opponent's monster, effectively removing a threat from their board and disrupting their plays, which aligns with the definition of a board breaker, particularly when going second.
justification_2: This card's effect allows you to switch control of an opponent's monster, which is a powerful way to remove an established threat, especially when going second, fitting the definition of a board breaker.

card_text: Target 1 face-up monster you control (except a monster that is attacking directly); it gains 1000 ATK, also it cannot attack your opponent directly for the rest of this turn. During the End Phase of this turn, it loses 2000 ATK.
justification_1: This card effect is a continuous spell that provides an ATK boost and restriction, but does not fit the criteria for a starter, handtrap, extender, board breaker, or garnet. justification_2: This card's effect provides an ATK boost but also imposes a restriction on direct attacks and causes a further ATK reduction. It does not directly start a combo, disrupt an opponent from the hand, extend existing plays, break a board, or fit the description of a "Garnet." It is a conditional, self-contained effect that doesn't align with the primary functions of the defined categories.

card_text:
FLIP: You can target 1 "Shaddoll" card in your GY; add it to your hand.
If this card is sent to the GY by a card effect: You can target 1 monster on the field; change its battle position. (Flip monsters' effects are not activated at this time, except "Shaddoll" monsters'.) You can only use 1 "Shaddoll Hound" effect per turn, and only once that turn.
justification_1: The first effect of "Shaddoll Hound" can add a "Shaddoll" card from the GY to hand, which helps extend combos by recovering resources. The second effect, which changes a monster's battle position, can also be used to facilitate further plays or disrupt the opponent, but it's reactive and not a primary starter on its own. Neither effect is designed to initiate a new play from scratch or disrupt the opponent from the hand.
justification_2: This card's FLIP effect allows it to retrieve a "Shaddoll" card from the GY, which can extend plays by providing resources. The second effect, triggered when sent to the GY, can also facilitate further plays by changing monster battle positions. Neither effect directly starts a combo but rather supports or continues existing ones.

card_text: When an opponent's monster declares an attack: Special Summon 1 Illusion monster from your hand or GY, and if you do, return the attacking monster to the hand. If a face-up Illusion monster(s) you control leaves the field by an opponent's card effect, while this card is in your GY (except during the Damage Step): You can banish this card; Special Summon 1 Illusion monster from your hand or GY. You can only use this effect of "Mirage Mirror Force" once per turn.
justification_1: This card's primary effect is activated reactively to remove a threat from the opponent's field. When an opponent's monster attacks, this trap removes that monster from the field, directly answering an established threat and breaking the opponent's battle plan.
justification_2: This card is a reactive Trap that provides defensive disruption against an opponent's attack and offers recovery if your Illusion monster is removed. It does not initiate your main combo (not a Starter), cannot be activated from the hand (not a Handtrap), does not extend your own combo progression (not an Extender), nor does it proactively dismantle an established opponent's board (not a Board Breaker). It is also not a necessary but undesirable draw for a combo (not a Garnet). Its role is primarily defensive and reactive field disruption/recovery.

card_text: When this card is sent to your Graveyard: You can banish this card from your Graveyard; Special Summon 1 "Peten the Dark Clown" from your hand or Deck.
justification_1: This card's effect activates when it is sent to the Graveyard, allowing for the Special Summon of another monster. This typically occurs after a previous play has been made or a card effect has caused it to be sent to the Graveyard, thus extending or continuing a combo.
justification_2: This card's effect activates when it is sent to the Graveyard, allowing for further plays or extension of a combo after it has been used for a Synchro or Xyz Summon, or discarded for another card's effect. It requires another card or play to have already occurred for its Graveyard effect to trigger.

card_text: While face-up on the field, this card is also WIND-Attribute. You can only use each of the following effects of "Simorgh, Bird of Perfection" once per turn. If this card is Normal Summoned: You can send 1 "Simorgh" monster from your Deck to the GY; add 1 "Simorgh" Spell/Trap from your Deck to your hand.

Its second effect, while providing a search on Normal Summon, doesn't negate its primary function as an extender in a Winged Beast strategy.

This condition requires a play to have already started, and the effect then allows for further progression or more material for combo plays, fitting the definition of an Extender. While its Normal Summon effect can search, its role in extending existing combos is more prominent.

card_text: This card is used to Ritual Summon "Garlandolf, King of Destruction". You must also Tribute monsters whose total Levels equal 7 or more from the field or your hand. During your Main Phase, you can remove from play this card from your Graveyard. Its graveyard effect offers a minor benefit but doesn't fit the criteria for the other categories. It's not a handtrap as it doesn't disrupt the opponent from hand, not a board breaker as it doesn't directly remove threats, and not a Garnet as its main purpose is the Ritual Summon itself, not to enable another card that you wouldn't want to draw.

card_text: If you control a "Marincess" Link Monster: Target 1 face-up monster your opponent controls; negate that face-up monster's effects until the end of this turn, then, if you control a Link-2 or higher "Marincess" monster, all face-up monsters you currently control are unaffected by your opponent's card effects until the end of this turn. card_text: Cannot be Normal Summoned/Set. Must be Special Summoned (from your hand) by having exactly 5 WIND monsters in your GY. 3 Normal Monsters

card_text: If a Level 7 or lower "Red-Eyes" monster(s) you control is destroyed by your opponent's attack or card effect and sent to your GY, while this card is in your hand: You can Special Summon this card in Defense Position, and if you do, Special Summon as many of those destroyed monsters as possible in the same position they were in when destroyed. card_text: Activate by sending 2 cards from your hand to the Graveyard while your opponent has more than 3000 Life Points. Inflict 2000 damage to your opponent. If this card is in the Graveyard, you can add it to your hand instead of conducting your normal draw during your Draw Phase.

It does not initiate a combo, disrupt an opponent from the hand, extend a combo, break a board, or function as a "necessary brick" in the way defined by the categories.

card_text: Once per turn, during each of your Standby Phases, you can pay 100 Life Points to look at 1 card on the top of your opponent's Deck and return it to its original position. Your opponent cannot look at the card.
