card_text: Activate this card only if your opponent controls a card in this Set card's column; return all other cards in this card's column to the hand. Neither player can use the unused zones in this card's column. You can only control 1 "Sour Scheduling - Red Vinegar Vamoose".
justification_1: This card's primary effect is to return cards from the opponent's field to their hand, specifically targeting threats in a column. Its activation condition requires the opponent to have an established card, and its strategic role is to eliminate or answer those threats on the opponent's field, aligning with the definition of a Board Breaker.
justification_2: This card's primary effect is to return cards in a column to the hand and prevent further use of that column, directly eliminating or neutralizing established threats on the opponent's field. While it's a Trap and needs to be Set, its strategic role is to disrupt and dismantle the opponent's board, fitting the definition of a board breaker.

card_text: When this card is Normal Summoned, you can add 1 Level 4 "R-Genex" monster from your Deck to your hand.
justification_1: This card's effect triggers on Normal Summon, allowing it to add a crucial "R-Genex" monster directly from the Deck to the hand, which is a common and effective way to begin a combo.
justification_2: This card initiates plays by adding a key combo piece from the Deck to the hand upon Normal Summon, allowing for the start of a meaningful line of play.

card_text: This monster gets the following effect(s) while there is a monster(s) with the following Attribute(s) on the field: ● WATER: Control of this card cannot switch. ● WIND: If this card destroyed your opponent's monster by battle, it can attack once again in a row.
justification_1: This card’s effect is entirely dependent on other monsters being on the field, serving only to facilitate a combo/engine rather than advancing plays on its own. It’s a “necessary brick” for a potential strategy but isn’t a desirable card to draw.
justification_2: This card's effects are passive buffs that depend on the Attributes of monsters on the field and do not initiate combos, disrupt opponents, extend plays, break boards, or function as a necessary-but-undesirable combo piece.

card_text: Cannot be Special Summoned from the Deck. If you control no monsters, you can Normal Summon this card without Tributing. Cannot be destroyed by battle or card effects. You take no battle damage from attacks involving this card. At the end of the Battle Phase, if this card battled and your LP is less than 4000: Your LP becomes 4000. Once per turn, during your Standby Phase: Shuffle this card into the Deck.
justification_1: This card can be Normal Summoned without Tributes if you control no monsters, allowing you to begin your plays on an empty board and establish a presence. Its immunity to destruction and battle damage also allows it to consistently survive and continue to be a starting point for further actions.
justification_2: This card can be Normal Summoned without Tributing if you control no monsters, providing an immediate presence on the field and allowing for potential further plays, making it a self-sufficient starting piece.

card_text: When this card is Normal Summoned: You can target 1 Level 5 "Chronomaly" monster in your Graveyard; Special Summon that target. You cannot Special Summon monsters the turn you activate this effect, except "Chronomaly" monsters.
justification_1: This card can be Normal Summoned to immediately Special Summon another monster from the Graveyard, initiating a combo line by bringing out a key piece for the "Chronomaly" archetype.
justification_2: This card's effect requires it to be Normal Summoned, and then it can revive another "Chronomaly" monster from the graveyard. This allows for further plays and extending combos after the initial Normal Summon has been performed.

card_text: When this card is Tribute Summoned: You can banish 1 Spell Card from your Graveyard; add 1 card with the same name as the banished Spell Card from your Deck to your hand.
justification_1: This card can be Tribute Summoned as an opening play to search for another copy of itself from the Deck, initiating a combo by adding a crucial piece to your hand.
justification_2: This card's effect triggers upon Tribute Summoning, allowing it to search for another copy of itself or a key Spell card from the Deck. This directly initiates a combo or progression towards a win condition, fulfilling the role of a starter.

card_text: Target any number of "Vylon" Monster Cards you control that are Equip Cards; Special Summon them in face-up Defense Position. Banish them when they leave the field.
justification_1: This card's effect requires existing "Vylon" monsters to be controlled and equipped to other monsters. It then special summons them from the equipped state, allowing for further plays or extending an existing combo.
justification_2: This card's effect requires the player to control "Vylon" Monster Cards that are currently Equip Cards. Its primary function is to Special Summon these monsters from the field, continuing a combo or establishing a board presence after initial plays have been made, thus extending the player's turn.

card_text: Once per turn, you can either: Target 1 face-up "Six Samurai" monster you control; equip this card to that target, OR: Unequip this card and Special Summon it in face-up Attack Position. While equipped by this effect, the equipped monster gains 500 ATK and DEF. If the equipped monster destroys an opponent's monster by battle: Draw 1 card. (A monster can only be equipped with 1 Union Monster at a time. If the equipped monster would be destroyed, destroy this card instead.)
justification_1: This card’s effect requires an already-established board (a "Six Samurai" monster) to utilize its summoning effect and attached bonuses. It’s designed to extend the plays of an existing combo, providing additional value and potential for drawing cards after a main play has started.
justification_2: This card's primary function is to be equipped to a "Six Samurai" monster, extending its utility by providing an ATK/DEF boost and a potential draw effect upon destroying an opponent's monster by battle. It does not initiate plays on its own, but rather enhances existing board presence.

card_text: Special Summon any number of "Valkyrie" monsters with different names from your hand. If you Special Summoned 3 or more monsters with this effect, you take no battle damage until the end of the next turn. During the End Phase of the turn you activated this card, shuffle all monsters you control into the Deck. You can banish this card from your GY; add 1 "Mischief of the Time Goddess" from your Deck to your hand. You can only use this effect of "Ride of the Valkyries" once per turn.
justification_1: This card's primary effect allows for the Special Summon of multiple "Valkyrie" monsters from the hand, initiating a combo by establishing a board presence and facilitating further plays within the archetype.
justification_2: This card allows for the Special Summon of multiple monsters from the hand by itself, which can initiate a combo and advance the player's game plan. While it has a downside of shuffling monsters back into the deck, its primary function is to start plays by swarming the field.

card_text: If your opponent draws a card(s): Increase this card's Level by the number drawn. While this card's Level is higher than its original Level, it gains ATK equal to the difference x 500. When your opponent activates a Spell Card or effect, while you control "Shining Sarcophagus" (Quick Effect): You can negate the activation, and if you do, increase this card's Level by 1. You can only use this effect of "Silent Magician Zero" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's primary interactive effect, a spell negate, is entirely conditional upon controlling another specific card ("Shining Sarcophagus"). This means it cannot initiate plays by itself but must be added to an established board to provide its disruptive value, thus extending the capabilities and resilience of your existing field.
justification_2: The card's primary role is to act as an on-field interruption by negating a spell, but this effect requires it to already be on the field alongside "Shining Sarcophagus". It does not start plays, nor does its effect extend them (it is the goal of a combo, not a facilitator). It is not a Handtrap as it works on the field, not a Board Breaker as it's preventative, and not a Garnet as it's a desired part of the strategy. Its role as a payoff/interruption piece does not fit the provided categories.

card_text: When this face-down card is destroyed and sent to the Graveyard, Special Summon 1 "Wicked Token" (Fiend-Type/DARK/Level 4/ATK 1000/DEF 1000) on your side of the field.
justification_1: This card's effect activates when it is destroyed and sent to the Graveyard, which is a triggered effect that occurs after the card has been removed from the field, rather than being activated from the hand or to proactively start plays. It does not fit the definition of a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet.
justification_2: This card's effect activates when it is destroyed and sent to the Graveyard, meaning it needs to be on the field and resolved in a specific way. It does not proactively start a combo, disrupt the opponent from the hand, extend plays, or break boards. It also doesn't fit the "Garnet" criteria of being a necessary but undesirable draw for an engine.

card_text:
2 Level 4 DARK monsters
Once per turn (Quick Effect): You can detach 1 material from this card; this face-up card is unaffected by other monster effects this turn.
justification_1: This card’s effect can be activated from the hand during either player’s turn to negate other monster effects, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap. It's a defensive tool to protect itself, rather than advancing a combo.
justification_2: This card's effect requires another card (a monster to be detached) to activate, meaning it cannot start a play by itself but can extend existing combos by providing protection or furthering a board state.

card_text:
While this card is face-up in the Spell & Trap Zone, Warrior monsters you control gain 500 ATK/DEF. Once per turn: You can discard 1 card; Special Summon this card as an Effect Monster (Warrior/LIGHT/Level 4/ATK 2000/DEF 2000) with the following effect (this card is also still a Trap), then you can add 1 "Argostars" card from your Deck to your hand, except "Argostars - Lightning Tydeu".
● Once per turn (Quick Effect): You can place this card face-up in your Spell & Trap Zone.
justification_1: This card's main effect Special Summons itself as a monster and then searches an "Argostars" card from the deck. This sequence of establishing a body on the field and adding a key combo piece to the hand is the primary function of a Starter. While as a Trap it is slower than a typical monster starter, its purpose is to initiate the deck's main line of play.
justification_2: This card must be Set for a turn before its main effect can be used. On a subsequent turn, it can be activated to Special Summon itself and search for another "Argostars" card. This action is ideal for extending plays after a turn's main combo has already started (e.g., after a Normal Summon), as it provides an additional monster and a search at the cost of a discard to build a more powerful board.

card_text: You can target 1 Fish monster you control and 1 monster your opponent controls; banish both monsters until your next Standby Phase. If a Fish monster(s) is Special Summoned to your field while any card is banished: You can banish this card from your GY or your face-up Spell & Trap Zone; all Fish monsters you currently control gain 100 ATK for each currently banished card, until the end of this turn. You can only use each effect of "Ghoti Fury" once per turn.
justification_1: The primary effect allows you to banish an opponent's monster, effectively removing an established threat from their field, which aligns with the definition of a Board Breaker. While it requires you to control a Fish monster and also banishes your own, its main strategic utility is interacting with and neutralizing the opponent's set-up. The second effect provides an ATK boost, which can be seen as an extender, but the core disruptive power comes from the first effect.
justification_2: The first effect requires a Fish monster to already be on your field to target an opponent's monster for removal, which helps clear threats or interruptions to continue your plays. The second effect provides a combat boost after a Fish monster is Special Summoned and cards are banished, directly extending the value of an ongoing combo or board state rather than starting it.

card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
If this card is Synchro Summoned: You can banish 1 Level 6 or lower Fish monster from your Deck. If this card is sent to the GY as Synchro Material: You can target 1 Fish monster in your GY; banish that target, then you can add 1 Fish monster with an equal or lower Level from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Arionpos, Serpent of the Ghoti" once per turn.
justification_1: As a Synchro Monster, it requires other monsters to be on the field to be summoned. Its effects activate upon Synchro Summon or when used as Synchro Material for another summon, both of which serve to advance existing plays, generate resources (banishing from Deck, searching from Deck), and extend combo lines, fitting the definition of an extender.
justification_2: As a Synchro Monster, this card cannot be an opening play and requires a Tuner and non-Tuner monster already on the field to be summoned. Its first effect triggers upon its Synchro Summon, enabling further plays by banishing a Fish monster from the Deck. Its second effect triggers when it's used as Synchro Material for another summon, providing a search that extends your combo. Both effects require an existing setup and serve to continue or further develop your board and resources, fitting the role of an Extender.

card_text: Once per turn: You can target 1 face-up Defense Position monster your opponent controls; discard 1 card and equip that target to this card. (You can only equip 1 monster at a time to this card with this effect.) This card gains the ATK of the equipped monster. If this card attacks, it is changed to Defense Position at the end of the Battle Phase. During the End Phase: The monster equipped to this card by this card's effect is Special Summoned to your opponent's side of the field in face-up Defense Position.
justification_1: This card's effect is primarily about equipping an opponent's monster and gaining its ATK, and then returning it to the opponent's field. It doesn't directly initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend plays, or break a board as its primary function. It's more of a situational effect that manipulates monsters.
justification_2: This card's effect involves taking an opponent's monster but does not have an immediate combo-starting or disruptive function, nor does it solely serve to extend an existing combo or break a board. It's a utility effect that doesn't clearly fit the defined categories.

card_text: If this card is in your hand or GY (Quick Effect): You can Tribute 1 "@Ignister" monster; Special Summon this card, but banish it when it leaves the field. When a card or effect is activated that targets a Cyberse monster(s) you control, and you control a Cyberse monster that was Special Summoned from the Extra Deck (Quick Effect): You can Tribute this card; negate that effect. You can only use each effect of "Kurikurinku @Ignister" once per turn.
justification_1: This card's primary effect requires you to already control an "@Ignister" monster to Special Summon itself from the hand or GY. This condition means it cannot start a play on its own, but instead serves to continue a combo by providing an additional monster on the field, which is the key function of an Extender.
justification_2: The card's primary effect is to Special Summon itself from the hand or GY by Tributing an "@Ignister" monster you already control. This activation condition requires a pre-existing board presence, making it a classic extender that continues your plays rather than starting them. Its ability to get an extra body on the field facilitates further combo lines, such as Link Summoning.

card_text: Reveal 1 Fusion Monster in your Extra Deck, add 1 of the Fusion Materials whose name is specifically listed on that card from your Deck to your hand, then you can add 1 "Polymerization" from your Graveyard to your hand.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand to search for a key combo piece (a Fusion Material) and potentially recover a "Polymerization," allowing the player to begin their Fusion Summoning strategy by itself.
justification_2: This card initiates plays by searching a key Fusion Material from the Deck, which is a common starting point for Fusion-based combos, and can even recover "Polymerization."

card_text: During the Battle Phase: Shuffle into the Deck, from your hand, field, or GY, the Fusion Materials that are listed on a "Gladiator Beast" Fusion Monster, then Special Summon that Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, ignoring its Summoning conditions. You can only activate 1 "Gladiator Beast United" per turn. You cannot declare attacks the turn you activate this card, except with "Gladiator Beast" monsters.
justification_1: This card is activated during the Battle Phase and requires existing "Gladiator Beast" Fusion Materials to be present on the field, in hand, or in the GY to facilitate further plays and summon a Fusion Monster. It extends existing "Gladiator Beast" strategies by providing an additional summon and ignoring summoning conditions.
justification_2: This card's effect allows the player to summon a "Gladiator Beast" Fusion Monster by utilizing resources from their hand, field, and GY, effectively breaking the opponent's board by bringing out a powerful monster. The restriction on attacking the turn it's activated reinforces its role as a setup for a subsequent turn rather than a direct offensive tool.

card_text: When this card is activated: You can add 1 LIGHT Machine Union monster from your Deck to your hand. Once per turn, if a LIGHT Machine Union monster(s) is Normal or Special Summoned to your field (except during the Damage Step): You can target 1 of those monsters; equip 1 appropriate LIGHT Machine Union monster, with a different name, from your Deck to that monster, but the Union monster you equipped cannot be Special Summoned this turn. You can only activate 1 "Union Hangar" per turn.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand to search for a key combo piece (a LIGHT Machine Union monster), allowing for meaningful progress in starting your deck's main strategy.
justification_2: This card's effect allows the player to search for a key combo piece (a LIGHT Machine Union monster) from the Deck upon activation, which is a common characteristic of a Starter card that initiates a deck's strategy.

card_text: If this card is Normal Summoned: Look at the top 3 cards of your Deck, you can reveal 1 Ritual Monster or 1 Ritual Spell Card among them and add it to your hand, also after that, place any remaining cards on the top of your Deck in any order.
justification_1: This card's effect triggers upon Normal Summon, allowing you to search for a Ritual Monster or Ritual Spell directly from your Deck, which is a strong opening move to begin your strategy.
justification_2: This card initiates plays by allowing you to search for a Ritual Monster or Ritual Spell upon being Normal Summoned, directly advancing your deck's strategy from the start.

card_text: If a face-up "Fire King" monster you control is destroyed by a card effect (except during the Damage Step): You can Special Summon this card from your hand. If this card is destroyed and sent to the Graveyard: You can destroy 1 card in your hand or that you control. You can only use this effect of "Fire King Avatar Yaksha" once per turn.
justification_1: This card is Special Summoned from the hand when a "Fire King" monster is destroyed, which typically happens after initial plays have begun. Its effect then allows for further board development or disruption, extending the combo.
justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand when a "Fire King" monster is destroyed, allowing it to extend plays after an initial monster effect or destruction has occurred on the field. Its own effect to destroy a card also helps to continue board presence or clear a path for further plays.

card_text: Once per turn: You can target 1 other Plant-Type monster on the field that has a Level; this card's Level becomes that monster's current Level, until the end of this turn.
justification_1: This effect allows you to temporarily copy the Level of another monster, extending your combos by mimicking a key stat for potential Xyz or Synchro plays, assuming your deck utilizes Level-based summoning strategies.
justification_2: This card's effect requires another Plant-Type monster on the field to modify its Level. This suggests it is used to extend existing plays by enabling access to higher-Level Xyz or Synchro plays after an initial monster has already been summoned.

card_text: You can discard 1 card, then declare 1 Attribute, except WATER; Special Summon this card from your hand to either field, then this card becomes that Attribute (until the end of this turn), then if you Special Summoned this card to your opponent's field, you can Special Summon 1 Level 4 or lower Fiend monster with an original Attribute other than the declared one, from your hand or GY to your field, also for the rest of this turn, you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck, except Fiend monsters. You can only use this effect of "Dipsea Fiend" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand with no prior board presence, requiring only another card to discard. By Special Summoning itself to the opponent's field, it generates a Special Summon of a Fiend monster from the hand or Graveyard to your field, effectively starting a combo line and establishing your board from an empty state.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand with no prior field setup, requiring only a discard as cost. Its effect Special Summons a Fiend monster from the hand or Graveyard, which initiates a line of play. In Fiend-centric decks, the discard cost can even be beneficial, making this an excellent way to start a combo on an empty board.

card_text: During the Main Phase: Fusion Summon 1 Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, using monsters from your hand or field as material, including a "Memento" monster. If a monster(s) in your possession was destroyed by card effect this turn, you can also shuffle "Memento" monsters from your GY into the Deck as material. During your Main Phase: You can banish this card from your GY; destroy 1 monster you control, and if you do, add 1 "Memento" Spell/Trap from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Mementotlan Fusion" once per turn.
justification_1: This card's primary effect to Fusion Summon requires existing monsters in the hand or on the field, meaning a play must have already started. Its secondary effect activates from the Graveyard to destroy a monster you control and search another card, which serves as a follow-up or continuation of your combo line, fitting the definition of an Extender.
justification_2: The card's primary effect requires monsters on the field or in hand to function as Fusion Material, meaning a play must have already been initiated. It serves to extend an existing board into a more powerful Fusion Monster. Furthermore, its GY effect provides follow-up and continues plays by destroying one of your own monsters (often to trigger a Memento effect) to search for another Spell/Trap, perfectly matching the role of an Extender.

card_text:
3 Level 10 monsters
Once per turn: You can detach 1 Xyz Material from this card; destroy all Spell and Trap Cards your opponent controls. Your opponent cannot activate Spell/Trap Cards in response to this effect's activation.
justification_1: The card's effect is activated to destroy all of the opponent's Spell and Trap Cards, which is a powerful way to clear an established board. It does not require any setup from the player to initiate its effect, and its primary function is to dismantle the opponent's backrow, fitting the definition of a board breaker.
justification_2: The card's effect, which destroys all of the opponent's Spell and Trap Cards, directly addresses and removes established threats on the opponent's field, making it a powerful tool for breaking an opponent's backrow, especially when going second.

card_text: Target 1 "Meklord" monster you control; your opponent takes no battle damage from attacks involving that face-up monster until the end of this turn, also its ATK becomes (permanently) the combined original ATK of all "Meklord" monsters you currently control. If a "Meklord" monster(s) you control would be destroyed by battle or card effect, you can banish this card from your GY instead. You can only activate 1 "Meklord Deflection" per turn.
justification_1: The card's primary effect requires a "Meklord" monster already on the field to target, enhancing its ATK and providing a specific battle property. Its secondary effect in the GY provides protection for "Meklord" monsters. Both effects serve to continue or extend the presence and effectiveness of your established "Meklord" board, rather than starting a new line of play or disrupting the opponent.
justification_2: This card requires a "Meklord" monster to already be on the field to activate its primary effect, providing an ATK boost that can help close out games or push through defenses. Its secondary effect offers graveyard protection for "Meklord" monsters, allowing them to survive and extend plays by maintaining board presence.

card_text: This clown appears from nowhere and executes very strange moves to avoid enemy attacks.
justification_1: The description "appears from nowhere and executes very strange moves to avoid enemy attacks" strongly implies an effect that activates during the opponent's turn to disrupt them or protect oneself, fitting the role of a Handtrap. Without the full card text, this is the most logical classification based on the provided flavor text.
justification_2: The provided text is a flavor description and not a card effect, making it impossible to classify its strategic role or activation conditions.

card_text:
FLIP: You can target 1 "Shaddoll" monster you control; that face-up monster is unaffected by monster effects this turn, except its own.
If this card is sent to the GY by card effect: You can target 1 Effect Monster on the field; neither player can activate that target's effects on the field this turn. You can only use 1 "Nehshaddoll Genius" effect per turn, and only once that turn.
justification_1: This card provides additional value and disruption after a play has already begun. Its "sent to the GY by card effect" trigger, common in Shaddoll archetypes (e.g., via Fusion Summons), allows for targeted monster effect negation, extending the utility and impact of a combo piece. The FLIP effect also extends a monster's protection once it's on the field.
justification_2: This card's primary utility comes from its effect when sent to the GY by a card effect, which allows for targeted monster effect negation. This effect requires an initial play (like a Fusion Summon or a send effect) to trigger, extending the value of that play by adding a disruptive layer rather than starting a combo itself.

card_text: If this card is Special Summoned from your hand by a Spell effect: You can target 1 "Mischief of the Time Goddess" in your GY; add it to your hand. If you control a "Valkyrie" monster other than "Valkyrie Erste" (Quick Effect): You can banish 1 monster from your opponent's GY, then this card's ATK becomes equal to the banished monster's original ATK until the end of this turn. You can only use each effect of "Valkyrie Erste" once per turn.
justification_1: The card’s first effect allows it to be added to the hand, which can then be used to continue plays. Its second effect requires control of another "Valkyrie" monster to activate, indicating it extends existing board presence.
justification_2: The card's first effect requires a Spell to Special Summon it, and its second effect requires control of another "Valkyrie" monster to activate. These conditions indicate it extends existing plays rather than starting them.

card_text: Cannot be Special Summoned. Once per turn, if another Spirit monster is Normal or Special Summoned while this monster is on the field: You can draw 1 card. Once per turn, during the End Phase, if this card was Normal Summoned or flipped face-up this turn: Return it to the hand.
justification_1: This card's primary effect requires another Spirit monster to be summoned while it is on the field, allowing it to extend plays by drawing a card. While it has a self-return effect, its main strategic role is to facilitate further plays after an initial Spirit monster has been summoned.
justification_2: The card's effect to draw a card requires another Spirit monster to be Normal or Special Summoned while it is on the field, meaning it extends existing plays rather than starting them independently. Its return-to-hand effect also implies it's meant to be on the field to facilitate further actions.

card_text: When there are 3 face-up "7" cards on your side of the field, draw 3 cards from your Deck. Then destroy all "7" cards. When this card is sent directly from the field to your Graveyard, increase your Life Points by 700 points.
justification_1: This card has a highly specific activation condition, requiring three "7" cards on the field to draw 3 cards, then destroys those cards. This effect does not start a main combo, disrupt the opponent from the hand, provide generic extension after a play has begun, or break an opponent's board. It's a payoff for a very niche and specific combo, rather than fitting any of the broadly defined strategic roles. It is not a Garnet as it is the desired outcome of its own specific engine, not an undesirable component required for another card's effect.

card_text:
2 Cyberse monsters
Once per turn, at the end of the Damage Step, if your monster this card points to attacked an opponent's Link Monster: You can activate this effect; the monster that attacked gains 1 additional attack on an opponent's Link Monster during this Battle Phase for each Link Monster you currently control, except this card. Your monsters (except that monster) cannot attack the turn you activate this effect.
justification_1: This card's effect activates during the Damage Step and requires a specific combat scenario (your monster attacking an opponent's Link Monster) to enable additional attacks. This means it requires an existing board state and a battle to have already occurred, thus extending existing plays rather than starting them.
justification_2: This card's effect triggers after a battle and requires a monster the card points to to have attacked an opponent's Link Monster. It then allows for additional attacks, extending the player's offensive capabilities once a battle has already occurred.

card_text:
This card can be used as a "Black Sheep Token".


*If used for another Token, apply that Token's Type/Attribute/Level/ATK/DEF.
justification_1: The provided text only describes the card's ability to be used as a "Black Sheep Token" and does not detail any activation conditions or strategic roles that would classify it as a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet.
justification_2: This card's text only states its function as a token, providing no inherent effect for activating combos, disrupting the opponent, extending plays, or breaking boards.

card_text: If this card is sent from the hand or Deck to the GY: You can target 1 "Springans" monster in your GY, except "Springans Brothers"; Special Summon it in Defense Position. If this card is in your hand, field, or GY: You can target 1 "Springans" Xyz Monster you control; attach this card to it as material. justification_1: The card's first effect triggers when it's sent from the hand or Deck to the GY, allowing a Special Summon from the GY, which continues or extends plays after another card has initiated a send to the GY. Its second effect allows it to attach itself as Xyz material from various locations to a "Springans" Xyz Monster already on the field, further supporting existing plays and enabling more uses of Xyz monster effects. Both effects require an existing setup or a preceding action to be effective, fitting the role of an Extender.
justification_2: The card's first effect special summons another "Springans" monster after being sent to the GY, allowing for continuation of plays. Its second effect provides additional Xyz material for a "Springans" Xyz Monster already on the field, enabling further effects or extending the monster's utility. Both effects require prior setup or an existing board state, fitting the role of an extender that pushes plays further or adds value.

card_text: Target any number of cards on the field (the max. number of targets on each side is the number of that player's "Sinful Spoils" cards that are banished or in their GY); destroy them. If this Set card is destroyed or banished by your opponent's activated effect: You can shuffle up to 2 cards from the field into the Deck. You can only activate 1 "Sinful Spoils Struggle" per turn.
justification_1: This card's primary effect is to destroy cards on the field. Its main purpose is to eliminate established threats from the opponent's board, fitting the definition of a board breaker. It does not start or extend your own plays, but rather answers your opponent's setup.
justification_2: The card's primary effect is to destroy multiple cards on the field, a function designed to eliminate established threats. While it requires setup by having "Sinful Spoils" cards in the GY or banished, its strategic role is to clear the opponent's board, fitting the definition of a Board Breaker.

card_text: You can Special Summon this card (from your hand) by removing 4 Ice Counters from anywhere on the field. Other monsters with Ice Counters cannot attack or change their battle positions.
justification_1: This card's Special Summon condition relies on the presence of "Ice Counters" on the field, indicating that other cards or effects must first establish these counters. Once summoned, its effect to prevent monsters with Ice Counters from attacking or changing battle positions can further extend plays by controlling the opponent's board.
justification_2: This card requires an existing board state with "Ice Counters" to be activated, allowing for further plays after a combo has already begun, fitting the definition of an extender.

card_text: Special Summon 1 Level 3 or lower Psychic-Type monster from your hand or Deck, but banish it during the End Phase of this turn.
justification_1: This card’s effect allows for extending a combo by providing a specific monster to summon, suggesting it’s meant to be used after an initial play has already begun, fitting the role of an extender.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand and allows the player to Special Summon a monster from their hand or Deck, directly initiating a turn's plays and progressing towards a win condition.

card_text: A dragon formed by huge boulders that are also used to attack the enemy.
justification_1: This is flavor text and does not describe a card effect that can be classified into any of the given categories.
justification_2: The provided text is flavor text and does not describe an effect that can be classified into any of the given categories.

card_text: Once per turn: You can pay LP in multiples of 100; other face-up monsters on the field lose that much ATK. Once per turn: You can target 1 monster on the field with ATK less than or equal to this card's; destroy it. If this card is destroyed by battle or card effect: All face-up monsters on the field lose 1000 ATK.
justification_1: This card's effects are activated on the field and focus on ATK reduction and destruction. It does not inherently start a combo, disrupt from hand, extend a play, or primarily break a board. Its primary function seems to be battlefield control or attrition, which doesn't fit the provided categories.
justification_2: This card does not have a specific activation condition that aligns with starting a combo, disrupting from the hand, extending existing plays, breaking a board, or being a necessary but undesirable draw. Its effects are primarily passive or require specific conditions to activate on-field.

card_text: A fiend guarding the treasures of the netherworld, it's most at home in the dark.
justification_1: The provided text is flavor text and does not describe a card effect or activation condition, making it impossible to classify.
justification_2: The card's effect, preventing opponent's monster effect activation, is a reactive effect used to disrupt the opponent's plays during either player’s turn, fitting the definition of a handtrap.

card_text: Once per turn, during your Main Phase, you can select 1 face-up Attack Position monster your opponent controls with the same Attribute as this card, and destroy it. This card cannot attack the turn you activate this effect.
justification_1: This card's effect allows the player to destroy an opponent's monster by targeting a monster with the same attribute. This is useful for removing established threats on the opponent's field, especially when going second, fitting the definition of a board breaker. The restriction on attacking the turn the effect is used reinforces its role as a defensive/reactive removal tool rather than an offensive combo piece.
justification_2: This card's effect allows for the destruction of an opponent's monster, directly addressing and removing a threat on their board. While it has a condition of matching Attributes and a self-imposed restriction, its primary function is to clear the opponent's established monsters, aligning with the role of a board breaker. It does not inherently start a combo or extend one without prior setup, nor is it a reactive handtrap.

card_text: When a monster(s) would be Pendulum Summoned: Negate the Pendulum Summon, and if you do, shuffle that monster(s) into the Deck.
justification_1: This card's effect can be activated when your opponent attempts to Pendulum Summon, allowing you to disrupt their plays from your hand during their turn, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.
justification_2: This effect can be activated during the opponent's turn when they attempt a Pendulum Summon to disrupt their plays by negating the summon and returning the monsters to the Deck, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.

card_text: FLIP: You can change all face-up Attack Position monsters your opponent controls to face-up Defense Position. Monsters changed to Defense Position by this effect cannot change their battle positions.
justification_1: This card's FLIP effect directly disrupts the opponent's established board by changing their monsters' battle positions and locking them in place, fulfilling the role of a board breaker, especially when going second.
justification_2: The card’s effect is used to disrupt the opponent's board by changing their monsters' battle positions, which is particularly useful when going second to neutralize established threats. It directly answers the opponent's board setup.

card_text:
(This card is always treated as an "Armored Xyz" card.)
An Xyz Monster equipped with this card gains these effects.
● Cannot be destroyed by battle.
● Once per turn, if this card battled this turn (Quick Effect): You can return 1 card equipped to this card to the hand, then immediately after this effect resolves, Xyz Summon 1 WATER Xyz Monster.
Once per turn, during the End Phase, if this card is in the GY because it was sent there this turn: You can equip this card to 1 Xyz Monster you control. You can only activate 1 "Reincarnation Unveiling Mail" per turn.
justification_1: This card requires an Xyz Monster to be on the field and to have battled before its main effect can activate, indicating it's not a starter. Its Quick Effect to perform an Xyz Summon after battle allows for further plays and board development during your turn or even the opponent's, fitting the definition of an extender that builds upon an existing setup. Its GY effect also allows for continued utility.
justification_2: This card is an Equip Spell that requires an Xyz Monster to be on the field to activate its primary effect. Its Quick Effect allows for an additional Xyz Summon after battle, extending your plays by bringing out another monster, and its GY effect enables future extensions by re-equipping itself.

card_text: This card cannot be Normal Summoned or Set. This card cannot be Special Summoned except with "Attack Reflector Unit". Once per turn, while this card is in Attack Position, your opponent's next attack is negated.
justification_1: This card's effect is a passive negation of attacks that occurs during the opponent's turn but requires the card to be on the field in Attack Position. It does not activate from the hand and does not intrinsically start a combo, extend a play, break a board, or function as a "Garnet." Therefore, it does not fit into the provided categories.
justification_2: This card’s effect primarily provides a defensive bonus to itself and does not inherently start a combo, extend plays, break boards, or disrupt the opponent from hand. Its summoning condition is also very restrictive.

card_text: You can target 1 WIND monster you control; return it to the hand, then you can Normal Summon 1 WIND monster. You can only use this effect of "Harpie Dancer" once per turn. This card's name becomes "Harpie Lady" while on the field or in the GY.
justification_1: This card requires a WIND monster to be controlled to activate its effect, which allows for a Normal Summon. This means it needs a prior play or setup to be effective, allowing for further combo continuation rather than initiating plays.
justification_2: This card allows for an additional Normal Summon of a WIND monster after returning another WIND monster to the hand. This typically follows an initial Normal Summon or Special Summon, extending the current turn's plays rather than initiating them.

card_text: FLIP: You can Special Summon 1 Flip monster from your hand or GY in face-down Defense Position, except "Tindangle Angel", then, if it is your opponent's Battle Phase, end the Battle Phase. You can only use this effect of "Tindangle Angel" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand or GY and Special Summons another monster from the hand or GY. This allows it to start plays by bringing out combo pieces, fitting the definition of a starter.
justification_2: The card’s effect is activated from the hand during the opponent’s Battle Phase to disrupt their plays by ending the phase, fitting the reactive, disruptive nature of a handtrap.

justification_1: The second effect allows a "Megalith" Ritual Monster to be Ritual Summoned directly from the Deck, which is a powerful way to begin the deck's main combo by bringing out a key monster (like a searcher or combo enabler) and establishing board presence.
justification_2: The card's primary effect allows for a Ritual Summon of a "Megalith" monster directly from the Deck, which is a powerful way to establish your main combo pieces and begin your deck's strategy without requiring a prior board presence or specific card already played.

card_text: Each turn, the first face-up Beast, Beast-Warrior, or Plant-Type monster you control that would be destroyed by battle, is not destroyed.
justification_1: This card’s effect is activated in response to an opponent's attack, disrupting the opponent's play by negating the destruction of a monster, fitting the definition of a reactive handtrap.
justification_2: This card is a continuous effect that protects your monsters from destruction by battle. It does not have a specific activation condition that fits the definitions of Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet, nor does it initiate a combo or disrupt the opponent.

card_text: Select 1 face-up monster on the field. Increase the selected monster's ATK by 100 points for each monster in your Graveyard during the turn this card is activated. The number of monsters is applied when this card is activated.
justification_1: This card provides a stat boost based on the number of monsters in the graveyard, but it does not have an activation condition that clearly fits starter, handtrap, extender, board breaker, or garnet. It is a passive buff that requires no specific setup to activate and doesn't directly disrupt or break boards.
justification_2: This card provides a temporary ATK boost based on the number of monsters in the Graveyard, but it doesn't inherently start a combo, disrupt an opponent, extend plays, break boards, or act as a desirable-to-draw-but-necessary-for-combo piece. Its activation condition is simple (select a monster), and its strategic role is a straightforward stat buff, not fitting neatly into the defined categories.

card_text: Declare 1 Type of monster. Any monster of the declared Type cannot declare an attack during the turn it is Normal Summoned, Flip Summoned or Special Summoned.
justification_1: This card effect is a continuous effect that applies a restriction to a declared monster type when summoned. It does not inherently activate to start a combo, disrupt the opponent, extend plays, or break boards. It lacks a clear strategic role within the defined categories.

card_text: You can Normal Summon this card without Tributing. If you do, during the End Phase: Send it to the Graveyard.
justification_1: This card can be Normal Summoned without a tribute, allowing it to be easily played as a first move to initiate a turn's plays. While it sends itself to the Graveyard later, its initial uninhibited summon makes it a potential starting point for various strategies.
justification_2: This card's effect allows for a Normal Summon without a tribute, which can be used to extend plays or make a stronger board, especially when combined with other cards that benefit from Normal Summons or have effects upon being sent to the Graveyard. It doesn't inherently start a combo on its own but facilitates further development.

card_text: You can Set this card from your hand to your Spell & Trap Zone as a Spell. During your opponent's turn, if this Set card in the Spell & Trap Zone is destroyed and sent to your GY: Special Summon it. If this card is Special Summoned during your opponent's turn: Destroy all cards in your Spell & Trap Zones. When an opponent's monster declares a direct attack: You can Special Summon this card from your hand.
justification_1: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand when the opponent declares a direct attack, allowing for disruption or a defensive play during the opponent's turn, fitting the role of a handtrap.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand when the opponent declares a direct attack, disrupting their offensive plays and fitting the role of a handtrap. Its ability to be Special Summoned from the Spell & Trap Zone when destroyed also allows for disruption on the opponent's turn.

card_text:
2+ monsters, including a Fish, Sea Serpent, or Aqua monster
Your opponent cannot target WATER monsters this card points to with card effects. You can only use each of the following effects of "Mermail King - Neptabyss" once per turn. If a WATER monster(s) is sent to the GY to activate a card or effect (except during the Damage Step): You can take 1 "Abyss-" Equip Spell from your Deck or GY and either add it to your hand or equip it to this card. If this card in its owner's possession is destroyed by an opponent's card: You can add 1 "Atlantean" or "Mermail" monster from your Deck to your hand.
justification_1: This card is a Link Monster that requires 2+ monsters, including a Fish, Sea Serpent, or Aqua monster, to be on the field for its Summon, meaning it cannot initiate a combo by itself. Its effects then trigger based on actions already happening (sending WATER monsters to the GY for cost or activation), providing additional resources or recovery ("Abyss-" Equip Spells or "Atlantean"/"Mermail" monsters), which extends ongoing plays or builds further advantage after a main combo has begun.
justification_2: This card is a Link Monster that requires at least two monsters on the field to be summoned, meaning a play must have already started. Its main effect triggers in response to another WATER monster being sent to the GY for an effect's cost, allowing it to search an "Abyss-" Equip Spell. This function does not start a line of play but rather adds value and resources to an already established combo, fitting the definition of an Extender.

card_text: When this face-up card on the field returns to the hand, Special Summon it. This card cannot attack during the turn it was Special Summoned by this effect.
justification_1: This card's effect allows it to be brought back to the hand, disrupting the opponent’s plays by negating the activation of other cards, aligning with the reactive and disruptive nature of a handtrap.
justification_2: The card's effect allows it to be Special Summoned to the field when it leaves the field and returns to the hand. This can be used to extend plays by providing another monster on the field without needing to commit resources to summoning it from the hand or deck, thus continuing a combo after an initial play has been made.

card_text: Target 1 Level 3 or lower WATER monster you control; Special Summon 1 monster with the same name from your Deck, but you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck while you control that face-up Special Summoned monster.
justification_1: The card requires a specific monster to already be on the field ("Target 1 Level 3 or lower WATER monster you control") to activate, and its effect brings out another monster from the Deck. This serves to continue or extend existing plays by providing additional monster presence, even with the restriction on Extra Deck Special Summons.
justification_2: This card requires a Level 3 or lower WATER monster to already be on your field to activate, meaning it cannot initiate a combo on its own. Its effect Special Summons another monster, allowing for additional plays or resources after an initial monster has been established, thereby extending existing plays rather than starting them.

card_text: Once per turn, if a Fusion Monster is Fusion Summoned: You can target 1 "Metalfoes" monster in your Graveyard with a lower Level than that Fusion Monster; Special Summon it. If this card is sent from the field to the Graveyard: You can add 1 "Metalfoes" monster from your Deck to your hand.
justification_1: The first effect requires a Fusion Monster to be successfully summoned to activate, allowing for further plays after a combo has begun by reviving a "Metalfoes" monster. The second effect triggers upon being sent to the graveyard, which can be a consequence of a combo or by strategically discarding it to search for another piece.
justification_2: The first effect can only be activated after a Fusion Monster has been summoned, which requires prior setup. This allows for continuation and extension of plays. The second effect, while searchable, requires the card to be on the field and then sent to the graveyard, typically as part of a combo.

card_text:
1 "Ritual Beast Tamer" monster + 1 "Spiritual Beast" monster
Must be Special Summoned (from your Extra Deck) by banishing the above cards from your field and/or GY. You can only Special Summon "Ritual Beast Ulti-Nochiudrago(s)" once per turn. Your opponent cannot target other "Ritual Beast" monsters you control with card effects. (Quick Effect): You can return this card to the Extra Deck, then target 1 of your banished "Ritual Beast" monsters; Special Summon it, but it cannot attack directly.
justification_1: This card is an Extra Deck monster that requires specific Ritual Beast materials to be summoned. Its Quick Effect allows it to return itself to the Extra Deck and Special Summon a banished Ritual Beast monster, which is a core "tag-out" mechanic used by Ritual Beast decks to extend combos, dodge interruptions, or recover resources after an initial play has been made.
justification_2: This card requires prior setup to be summoned (a "Ritual Beast Tamer" and "Spiritual Beast" on the field or in the GY). Its core function is its Quick Effect to "tag out," returning itself to the Extra Deck to summon another monster. This effect is central to the archetype's combo loops, allowing a player to extend their plays, generate more resources, and dodge opponent's targeting effects, thus fitting the role of an Extender.

card_text: Equip only to a "Penguin" monster. It gains 800 ATK. When the equipped monster inflicts battle damage to your opponent: You can return 1 card your opponent controls to the hand. If a face-up card your opponent controls leaves the field by a "Penguin" card's effect and returns to the hand or is banished: Negate the activated effects and effects on the field of cards with that original name, until the end of the next turn. You can only use this effect of "Penguin Sword" once per turn.
justification_1: This card requires a "Penguin" monster to already be on the field to be equipped, and its effects (ATK boost, battle damage bounce, and negation follow-up to other "Penguin" effects) all enhance or extend the plays initiated by "Penguin" monsters, fitting the definition of an extender.
justification_2: This card requires a "Penguin" monster already on the field to be equipped and its primary control effect (negation) only triggers after another "Penguin" card's effect has removed an opponent's card. It does not initiate a play but rather extends the value and impact of an existing "Penguin" strategy by providing additional disruption after a successful "Penguin" effect.

card_text: If an Xyz Monster(s) is on the field: You can Special Summon this card from your hand. You can send 1 other card from your hand or field to the GY; add 1 "Xyz", "Onomat", "Zexal", or "Number" Spell/Trap, from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Astraltopia" once per turn.
justification_1: This card requires an Xyz Monster to already be on the field to Special Summon itself, indicating it is used to extend plays after an initial summon or combo has begun. Its subsequent search effect further aids in continuing or expanding existing plays rather than initiating them.
justification_2: This card can Special Summon itself from the hand only if an Xyz Monster is already on the field, meaning it requires an established board to be activated. It then provides an additional body and a search for a Spell/Trap, allowing for further combo progression or recovery after initial plays have begun.

card_text: Target 1 card in either player's Pendulum Zone and declare a Pendulum Scale from 1 to 10; this turn, that card becomes that Pendulum Scale.

card_text: After damage calculation, when this card is destroyed by battle with an opponent's monster: Banish that monster, also banish this card.

It does not initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend plays, break boards, or act as a desirable piece for another card's effect. card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
Once per turn, during your Main Phase: You can place 1 "Nemleria" Continuous Spell from your Deck or GY face-up on your field, and if you do, add this card to your Extra Deck face-up. You cannot Special Summon "Dreaming Nemleria" the turn you activate this effect.

[ Monster Effect ] 
Cannot be Normal Summoned/Set. Must be Special Summoned (from your face-up Extra Deck) by having no cards in your Extra Deck, except "Dreaming Nemleria". You can only Special Summon "Dreaming Nemleria(s)" once per turn. If this card is Special Summoned: For every 3 of your face-down banished cards, you can banish up to 1 card from your opponent's field and/or GY, face-down, then shuffle 1 of your face-down banished cards into your Deck for each card you banished by this effect.
justification_1: This card's Pendulum Effect can be activated during your Main Phase to place a key "Nemleria" Continuous Spell from your Deck directly onto the field. This is the primary opening play for its archetype, initiating the entire strategy of setting up its Extra Deck for its monster effect, thus fitting the definition of a Starter.
justification_2: The card's Pendulum Effect can be activated from the hand with no prior setup. This effect places a key "Nemleria" Continuous Spell directly from the Deck or GY onto the field, which is the primary way the archetype begins its core strategy and sets up its resources for the rest of the turn. This makes it an ideal opening card to start your main combo.

card_text: Discard 1 card, then target 1 face-up monster on the field; banish that target.

justification_2: This card's effect allows the player to remove an opponent's monster from the field by banishing it. While it has a cost (discarding a card), its primary function is to eliminate a threat on the opponent's board, making it most effective when going second.

card_text: Activate only when a Synchro Monster is Special Summoned to your opponent's side of the field. Take control of that Synchro Monster. Remove it from play when it is destroyed. Destroy this card when that monster is removed from the field.

card_text: Activate only during Main Phase 1. card_text:
2 Effect Monsters
You can target 1 face-up monster on the field; it becomes DARK until the end of this turn. You can pay 2000 LP and send 1 "Fusion" or "Polymerization" Normal or Quick-Play Spell from your Deck to the GY; this effect becomes that Spell's effect when that card is activated, also, you cannot Special Summon monsters for the rest of this turn. Its primary effect allows access to powerful Fusion Summons from the Deck, thereby continuing or extending a player's established combo or allowing them to achieve a specific powerful end-board after their initial plays.

card_text:

card_text: Each "tellarknight" Xyz Monster gains 200 ATK and DEF for each Xyz Material attached to it. When a "tellarknight" Xyz Monster you control is targeted for an attack: You can send 1 "tellarknight" card from your hand to the Graveyard; negate that attack.

It does not initiate plays on its own, disrupt the opponent from the hand, extend combos, break boards, or function as a desirable brick for another card's effect.
