Its effects are passive buffs and a reactive-defense that doesn't fit the defined categories.

card_text: This card's name becomes "Great Sand Sea - Gold Golgonda" while on the field or in the GY. Once per turn, if a card(s) you control would be destroyed by battle or card effect, you can send 1 Fusion Monster that mentions "Fallen of Albaz" as material from your Extra Deck to the GY, instead. During the End Phase, if you have "Fallen of Albaz" on your field or in your GY: You can Special Summon 1 "Springans" monster from your hand or Deck. You can only use this effect of "Decisive Battle of Golgonda" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's primary Special Summon effect is an End Phase activation that requires "Fallen of Albaz" to be present on the field or in the GY, indicating that a play must have already begun. Its other effect provides destruction protection, helping to maintain an established board. Both effects serve to continue or extend the value and presence of your resources after an initial setup, aligning with the role of an Extender.
justification_2: The card's effect to prevent destruction directly helps "push through interruptions" by protecting your board, which requires an established presence. The End Phase Special Summon also requires "Fallen of Albaz" to already be on the field or in the GY, providing additional value after plays have begun, aligning with the role of an extender.

card_text:
Activate only as Chain Link 2 or higher; apply the following effects, depending on the Chain Link number of this card, in sequence (skip over any that do not apply). You cannot activate this card if multiple cards/effects with the same name are in that Chain.
● 2+: Excavate the top cards of your Deck, equal to the Chain Link number of this card, then place them on the top of your Deck in any order.
● 3+: Send the top card of your Deck to the GY.
● 4+: Draw 1 card.
You can only activate 1 "Surprise Chain" per turn.
justification_1: This card can only be activated as Chain Link 2 or higher, meaning it cannot initiate a play and requires another card or effect to have already started a chain. Its effects (deck excavation, milling, and drawing) are used to further your own game state or gain card advantage, effectively extending or progressing your plays by leveraging an existing chain.
justification_2: This card can only be activated as Chain Link 2 or higher, meaning it cannot initiate a play. Its effects (deck stacking, milling, and drawing) provide additional resources or set up future plays, allowing for further combo progression or value generation after a primary action has begun.

card_text: If you control a monster: You can reveal 2 Equip Spells from your Deck with different names, your opponent randomly picks 1 for you to add to your hand, also place the remaining card on the bottom of your Deck. You can only use this effect of "Tool Box" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's effect requires a monster to already be on the field, meaning it cannot initiate a combo from an empty board. It serves to continue or extend plays by searching for an Equip Spell after some initial action has been taken, fitting the definition of an Extender.
justification_2: The card's activation condition ("If you control a monster") means it cannot be the very first play of a turn. It requires a monster to already be on the field, fitting the definition of an Extender that continues or extends plays after something has already started, by searching for specific resources (Equip Spells).

card_text:
1 DARK monster + "Fallen of Albaz"
If this card is Fusion Summoned: You can discard 1 card; Fusion Summon 1 Level 8 or lower Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, except "Lubellion the Searing Dragon", by shuffling Fusion Materials mentioned on it into the Deck, from your monsters on the field, GY, and/or face-up banished cards. For the rest of this turn, this card cannot attack, also you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck, except Fusion Monsters. You can only use this effect of "Lubellion the Searing Dragon" once per turn.
justification_1: This card requires a Fusion Summon to activate its effect, meaning another card or action (like "Branded Fusion") must have initiated the play. Once summoned, its ability to immediately Fusion Summon another monster from the Extra Deck allows the player to extend their combo and build a stronger board, fitting the role of an extender.
justification_2: "Lubellion the Searing Dragon" requires a Fusion Summon to activate its effect, meaning an initial play (the Fusion Summon of Lubellion itself) must have already occurred. Its effect then allows for an additional Fusion Summon, effectively continuing and extending the player's combo by bringing out another monster and recycling materials, fitting the definition of an extender.

card_text: You can Tribute this card; Special Summon 1 "Constellar" monster from your hand or Graveyard in face-up Defense Position, except "Constellar Rasalhague".
justification_1: This card's effect requires tributing itself to Special Summon another "Constellar" monster, meaning it needs another monster on the field to activate and extend existing plays, rather than starting them.
justification_2: This card's effect requires you to control a monster to Tribute, and it Special Summons another monster from your hand or Graveyard. This is characteristic of an extender, which is used to continue or extend a combo after an initial play has been made.

card_text: You can reveal this card in your hand; your opponent randomly chooses 1 card from your entire hand, then you discard the chosen card. Then, if the discarded card was not "Danger! Nessie!", Special Summon 1 "Danger! Nessie!" from your hand, and if you do, draw 1 card. If this card is discarded: You can add 1 "Danger!" card from your Deck to your hand, except "Danger! Nessie!". You can only use this effect of "Danger! Nessie!" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand to initiate a play by revealing itself, potentially Special Summoning itself and drawing a card, which can lead into further combos.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand, potentially Special Summoning itself and drawing another card, which is a strong opening play that can initiate a combo without requiring other specific cards.

card_text: You are unaffected by the effects of "Clear World". You can discard 2 cards, including this card; add 2 cards from your Deck to your hand, that are "Clear World" or Spells/Traps that mention it. You can only use this effect of "Clear Phantom" once per turn. If this card is destroyed: You can target 1 monster your opponent controls; destroy it, and if you do, send the top 3 cards of their Deck to the GY.
justification_1: This card's primary effect is activated from the hand to search for the core components of its archetype ("Clear World" and related Spells/Traps). By discarding itself and another card, it allows the player to access their key engine pieces from the Deck at the beginning of the game, directly initiating the deck's strategy. This role of setting up the main line of play from the opening hand fits the definition of a Starter.
justification_2: This card's main effect allows you to discard it and another card to search for two core components of its archetype ("Clear World" and its support Spells/Traps). This effect is designed to be used at the start of your turn to set up your primary engine, fitting the definition of a Starter.

card_text: Target 1 monster with 0 ATK in your GY; Special Summon it in Attack Position, and if you do, equip it with this card. When this card, equipped by this effect, leaves the field, destroy that monster. You can only activate 1 "Stonehenge" per turn.
justification_1: This card can Special Summon a monster from your GY, but requires a specific condition (a 0 ATK monster in the GY) to be met. This means it cannot initiate a combo from scratch, but rather extends plays by bringing back a resource after something has already occurred (like sending the monster to the GY), allowing for further summons or combo progression.
justification_2: This card revives a monster from the GY, which implies a prior action has occurred (e.g., a monster being sent to the GY). It allows for the continuation of plays by bringing back resources, but it cannot initiate a combo from an empty state.

card_text: Each time a Spell Card is activated, place 1 Spell Counter on this card when that Spell resolves (max. 3). You can remove 3 Spell Counters from this card; draw 1 card.
justification_1: This card's effect requires another card to be activated first (a Spell Card) and only provides a minor advantage (drawing a card) without significantly advancing a combo or disrupting the opponent. It doesn't fit the primary functions of a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet.
justification_2: This card's effect is a passive accumulation of Spell Counters that, when a certain number is reached, allows the player to draw a card. It doesn't directly start a combo, disrupt an opponent, extend a play, break a board, or act as a necessary but undesirable brick. Its primary function is card advantage generation through a specific condition (Spell activations) which doesn't align with the defined categories.

card_text:
1 Tuner + 1 or more non-Tuner monsters
If this card is sent from the Monster Zone to the Graveyard: You can target 1 face-up monster your opponent controls; equip this card from your Graveyard to that monster. That monster cannot attack, also its effects are negated. If this card leaves the field while equipped to an opponent's monster by this effect: Take control of the monster this card was equipped to.
justification_1: This card's primary effect activates when it is sent to the Graveyard, meaning it needs to be on the field and sent there by another card's effect (like a Synchro Summon). This allows for continued plays or recovery after an initial summon, fitting the role of an extender.
justification_2: This card is a Synchro Monster whose effect activates when it is sent to the Graveyard or leaves the field while equipped. It does not initiate plays on its own, nor does it primarily disrupt the opponent from the hand. It also doesn't break established boards nor is it an engine piece specifically undesirable to draw. Its effects are passive or triggered by game events rather than proactive activations that fit the other categories.

card_text:
During the Battle Phase: Declare a whole number from 1 to 6; choose 1 Effect Monster your opponent controls. Multiply the Effect Monster's Level x the declared number, then add the number of cards your opponent controls, and check if the result equals the number of cards in your GY.
● If yes: Send cards from the top of your Deck to the GY, up to the declared number, and if you do, shuffle cards your opponent controls into the Deck, up to the number sent to the GY.
● If no: You lose LP equal to the declared number x 500.
You can only activate 1 "Linear Equation Cannon" per turn.
justification_1: This card's effect is used to remove established threats on the opponent's board by shuffling them into the Deck, a primary function of a board breaker, and is often best used when going second.
justification_2: This card is designed to disrupt the opponent's board by shuffling their cards into the Deck, and it is most effective when the opponent has established a board with multiple cards. The condition of needing to match a calculation based on their monster's Level, a declared number, and cards in your GY, along with the potential LP loss if the condition isn't met, suggests a proactive tool to dismantle an opponent's setup rather than a starting point for your own plays.

card_text: During the Battle Phase, if you take battle or effect damage: You can Special Summon this card from your hand. During either player's Battle Phase in which this card was Special Summoned by its effect, you can: Immediately after this effect resolves, Synchro Summon 1 Synchro Monster, using only WIND monsters you control, including this card. If this card is sent to the Graveyard as a Synchro Material: You can send the top card of your Deck to the Graveyard, and if the sent card was a "Speedroid" monster, the Synchro Monster that used this card as Synchro Material gains 1000 ATK.
justification_1: This card’s effect requires an existing board (Wind monsters) to utilize its Synchro Summoning ability, extending a combo that has already begun.
justification_2: This card's primary activation condition is taking battle or effect damage, which is not a guaranteed opening play. Its main utility is to facilitate further plays, specifically Synchro Summons, after a board state or condition has been met, fitting the role of an extender.

card_text: "Dark Contract" cards you control cannot be destroyed by your opponent's card effects. You can banish this card from your Graveyard, then target up to 3 of your other banished "D/D" cards; shuffle them into the Deck.
justification_1: This card provides passive protection and graveyard recursion for "D/D" cards. It does not initiate plays, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend combos, or break boards. It also does not fit the description of a "Garnet" as it's not a necessary brick for a combo engine.
justification_2: This card’s effect is reactive, allowing you to disrupt your opponent’s attempts to destroy your banished "D/D" monsters, fitting the definition of a handtrap that disrupts the opponent's plays.

card_text: When your opponent activates a card or effect that targets a Spellcaster monster(s) you control, or targets it for an attack (Quick Effect): You can target 1 card your opponent controls, or 1 "Witchcrafter" Spell in your GY; Special Summon this card from your hand, and if you do, return that targeted card to the hand. You can only use this effect of "Witchcrafter Golem Aruru" once per turn. Once per turn, during your opponent's Standby Phase: Return this card to the hand.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand during the opponent's turn as a Quick Effect to disrupt the opponent by returning a targeted card to their hand, fitting the definition of a disruptive handtrap.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand as a Quick Effect to disrupt the opponent by bouncing a targeted card or Special Summoning itself and returning a card to the hand. Its ability to be activated from the hand during the opponent's turn to interfere with their plays aligns with the characteristics of a handtrap.

card_text: During either player's turn: You can Tribute this card; this turn, face-up "Amazoness" monsters you currently control cannot be targeted by monster effects, and cannot be destroyed by card effects.
justification_1: This card’s effect allows the player to negate monster effects targeting "Amazoness" monsters, activated from the hand during either player’s turn to disrupt the opponent’s plays, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.
justification_2: This card can be activated during either player's turn by Tributing itself to protect "Amazoness" monsters from monster effects and card effects. This reactive protection fits the definition of a disruptive handtrap that interacts with the opponent's plays without being on the field.

card_text: Before damage calculation, if this card attacks a Defense Position monster: Destroy that monster.
justification_1: This card's effect is activated from the hand during either player’s turn to disrupt the opponent's plays by destroying a monster, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.
justification_2: This card's effect is a passive combat trick that only activates during a battle involving the card itself. It does not initiate plays, disrupt opponents, extend combos, break boards, or fit the profile of a "Garnet." Its effect is too situational and lacks the proactive or reactive strategic impact required for the other categories.

card_text: You can Tribute 1 Machine monster; Special Summon 1 non-WIND "roid" monster from your Deck. You can pay half your LP and banish any number of Machine monsters from your GY, including this card; Special Summon from your Extra Deck, 1 "roid" Fusion Monster with the same Level as the number of those banished monsters, ignoring its Summoning conditions, but destroy it during the End Phase. You can only use each effect of "Mixeroid" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can initiate plays by tributing a Machine monster to Special Summon a "roid" monster from the Deck, directly advancing the player's strategy.
justification_2: This card can initiate plays by tributing a Machine monster to Special Summon a "roid" monster from the Deck, directly advancing the combo.

card_text: Neither player can add cards from their Deck to their hand except by drawing them. During either player's turn, when your opponent would Special Summon exactly 1 monster: You can send this face-up card to the Graveyard; negate the Special Summon, and if you do, destroy it.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand during either player's turn to negate a specific type of opponent's Special Summon, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand during either player's turn to negate a specific type of Special Summon, serving as disruption against the opponent's plays.

card_text: Fusion Summon 1 Fiend or Fairy Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, by shuffling Fiend and/or Fairy monsters from your GY into the Deck as material. If this card is in your GY: You can send 1 Fiend or Fairy monster from your hand or face-up field to the GY; add this card to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Mutiny in the Sky" once per turn.
justification_1: The primary effect requires Fiend/Fairy monsters to already be in the GY, meaning it extends plays by utilizing resources accumulated from previous actions rather than starting them from scratch. Its secondary effect further supports this by allowing the card to recycle itself, enabling continued or extended plays.
justification_2: This card performs a Fusion Summon using materials from the GY, requiring previous plays to have established monsters there. Its ability to recycle itself from the GY further extends its usability and allows for continued resource generation or additional summons after initial plays have been made.

card_text: Once per turn: You can toss a coin and call it. If you call it right, destroy all monsters your opponent controls. If you call it wrong, destroy as many monsters you control as possible, and if you do, take damage equal to half the total ATK those destroyed monsters had while face-up on the field.
justification_1: This card's effect is activated by choice, not by a specific condition related to the opponent's actions or the player's board state. It doesn't initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend plays, break boards, or function as a necessary brick. Its activation is a gamble with a significant downside, making it not fit any of the defined strategic roles.
justification_2: This card's effect is a coin toss that can destroy the opponent's monsters, but it does not have a specific activation condition that fits any of the defined categories (Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, Garnet) and is not primarily a strategic piece for combo extension or disruption.

card_text: Cannot be destroyed by battle or card effects while your opponent has a face-up card in their Field Zone. You can only use each of the following effects of "I.A.S. -Invasive Alien Species-" once per turn. If your opponent has a face-up card in their Field Zone: You can target 1 monster they control; destroy it, and if you do, this card gains 1000 ATK. If your opponent has a face-up card in their Field Zone, while this card is in your GY: You can Special Summon this card.
justification_1: This card's primary effect is to destroy an opponent's monster, a role designed to dismantle an established field. Its activation condition requires the opponent to already have a card in their Field Zone, making it most effective for breaking an opponent's setup rather than starting your own plays. Its self-revival effect from the GY further supports its role as a persistent threat that can repeatedly clear opposing monsters.
justification_2: The card's primary effects—destroying an opponent's monster, its own protection, and its revival from the Graveyard—are all conditional on the opponent controlling a face-up card in their Field Zone. This design makes it an ideal tool for going second to dismantle an opponent's established board by destroying a monster.

card_text: If a face-up "Red-Eyes" monster(s) you control, except "Red-Eyes B. Chick", is destroyed by battle or card effect: Target 1 of those monsters; both players take damage equal to its original ATK. You can only activate 1 "Red-Eyes Burn" per turn.
justification_1: This card's effect is a conditional damage effect that triggers when specific "Red-Eyes" monsters are destroyed. It does not initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend a current play, or break an established board. It's primarily a payoff for having a board and having it destroyed.
justification_2: This card's effect activates when a "Red-Eyes" monster is destroyed. It does not start a combo, disrupt the opponent from the hand, extend an existing play, break a board, or act as a "Garnet." Its primary function is to punish the opponent for destroying a specific type of monster, which doesn't fit neatly into the defined categories.

card_text:
You can send 2 other monsters from your hand to the GY; Special Summon this card from your hand. You can only use this effect of "Elementsaber Lapauila Mana" once per turn. Apply the following effect(s) to "Elementsaber" and "Elemental Lord" monsters you control, depending on the original Attributes of any "Elementsaber" monsters sent to the GY to activate this card's effect.
● EARTH or WIND: Cannot be destroyed by battle.
● WATER or FIRE: Cannot be destroyed by effects.
● LIGHT or DARK: Your opponent cannot target it with card effects.
justification_1: This card’s effect requires an already established board (specifically “Elementsaber” monsters) to utilize its effect, representing a continuation or extension of a combo after a play has begun.
justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand by sending other monsters from the hand to the GY. While it can initiate a play by itself, its primary function is to be Special Summoned to further extend plays, especially by providing Attribute-based protection to other monsters on the field. It is not typically the card you would want to open with as your sole play, but rather to continue an established board or recover from disruption.

card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
Face-up cards in your Spell & Trap Zone cannot be destroyed by card effects. You can only use each of the following effects of "Centur-Ion Auxila" once per turn. If this card is Special Summoned: You can add 1 "Centur-Ion" card from your Deck to your hand. During the End Phase: You can place 1 of your non-Synchro "Centur-Ion" monsters that is banished or in your GY, in your Spell & Trap Zone as a face-up Continuous Trap.
justification_1: This card is a Synchro monster summoned from the Extra Deck, meaning it requires an established board to be brought out. Its effect activates upon being Special Summoned to search for a "Centur-Ion" card, which continues your plays and generates more value after your main combo has already started.
justification_2: This is a Synchro Monster that requires monsters already on the field to be summoned. Its effect on summon is to search for a "Centur-Ion" card, which continues your plays after you've already started a combo. It builds upon an established board rather than initiating plays by itself.

card_text: Armed with a pair of shovels, this monster will destroy anything that stands in its way.
justification_1: The card's effect directly addresses and removes threats (anything standing in its way) on the opponent's field, fitting the role of a board breaker.
justification_2: The provided text is flavor text and does not contain any card effect information, making it impossible to classify the card's activation condition or strategic role.

card_text: You can Fusion Summon Fusion Monsters that must be Special Summoned with "Dark Fusion", with effects other than "Dark Fusion". Once per turn, during damage calculation, if your Fiend monster battles an opponent's monster: You can send 1 "Evil HERO" monster from your Deck or Extra Deck to the GY; your monster gains ATK equal to the Level of the monster sent to the GY x 200, until the end of this turn (even if this card leaves the field).
justification_1: This card's effects are primarily focused on powering up its own battles or facilitating Fusion Summons of specific monsters, but it doesn't have a clear activation condition that fits the defined categories of starting a combo, disrupting the opponent from hand, extending existing plays, breaking boards, or being a necessary but undesirable draw. It's more of a support or engine piece that doesn't neatly fit into the provided classifications.
justification_2: This card's effects do not fit the criteria for Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet. It provides a fusion summon option and a battle phase effect that buffs its own monster, but it doesn't initiate plays, disrupt opponents from hand, extend existing combos, break boards, or act as a necessary but undesirable draw.

card_text: Banish 1 WATER monster from your GY; add 1 Level 4 or lower Sea Serpent monster from your Deck to your hand. You can only activate 1 "Deep Sea Aria" per turn.
justification_1: This card allows you to banish a WATER monster from your GY to search for a Level 4 or lower Sea Serpent monster from your Deck, which is a common way to start plays by adding a key combo piece to your hand.
justification_2: This card allows for the addition of a key monster from the Deck to the hand, initiating plays and progressing the deck's strategy, making it a prime starter.

card_text: If you Tribute Summon this card, the Tribute must be Winged Beast-Type. This card is unaffected by the effects of Trap Cards.
justification_1: This card's effects are primarily about its own protection and Tribute Summoning requirements, with no clear trigger condition for disruption, combo extension, or board breaking. It doesn't fit the role of a starter, handtrap, extender, board breaker, or a garnet.
justification_2: This card's effects primarily provide protection and tributing conditions for itself. It does not inherently initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from the hand, extend an existing play, break an established board, or serve as a necessary but undesirable draw like a Garnet. Its effects are passive and do not align with any of the provided categories.

card_text: Destroy 1 face-up Fusion Monster on the field.
justification_1: This card's effect is to destroy an opponent's Fusion Monster, directly addressing and removing an established threat on the opponent's field, which is the primary function of a board breaker, especially when going second.
justification_2: This card's effect directly targets and destroys an opponent's monster on the field, aiming to remove an established threat, which is characteristic of a board breaker. It does not initiate a combo or extend one, nor is it a handtrap or a card that is detrimental to draw in isolation.

card_text: If you have an Xyz Monster in your field or GY, you can Special Summon this card (from your hand). If Summoned this way, you cannot Special Summon from the Extra Deck for the rest of this turn, except Rank 4 Xyz Monsters. You can only Special Summon "Node Ryzeal" once per turn this way. You can send 1 card from your hand or field to the GY, then target 1 "Ryzeal" monster in your GY, except "Node Ryzeal"; Special Summon it in Defense Position, but negate its effects. You can only use this effect of "Node Ryzeal" once per turn.
justification_1: The card’s Special Summon effect from the hand requires an Xyz Monster to already be on the field or in the Graveyard, meaning it cannot initiate plays on its own. It functions to continue a pre-existing combo by providing an additional body on board, fitting the role of an Extender.
justification_2: This card's primary effect to Special Summon itself from the hand can only be activated if you already have an Xyz Monster on your field or in your Graveyard. This condition requires a play to have already been started, and summoning this card serves to continue or "extend" that play by providing another body on the field, typically for another Xyz Summon.

card_text: If this card is in your Graveyard: You can send 2 cards, except "Fabled Soulkius", from your hand to the Graveyard; Special Summon this card from the Graveyard.
justification_1: This card's effect requires it to be in the Graveyard and necessitates discarding other cards from the hand to Special Summon itself. This means it cannot initiate a play on its own but can extend existing plays or revive itself after being sent to the Graveyard as part of a combo, fitting the definition of an extender.
justification_2: This card can be summoned from the Graveyard by discarding other cards. This allows for extending plays by bringing a monster back to the field to facilitate further combos or resource generation, but it requires a prior setup (cards in hand to discard and this card in the Graveyard) to be activated.

card_text:
2+ monsters, including a Winged Beast monster
Cannot be used as Link Material. Your opponent cannot target this card or a Winged Beast monster(s) this card points to with card effects. If this card would be destroyed by battle, you can destroy 1 other "Simorgh" card you control instead. During the End Phase: You can Special Summon 1 Winged Beast monster from your hand or Deck, with a Level equal to or lower than the total number of unused Spell & Trap Zones on the field. You can only use this effect of "Simorgh, Bird of Sovereignty" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be easily summoned by using 2+ monsters, and its effect to special summon a Winged Beast monster from hand or deck during the End Phase can initiate a combo or extend plays. This flexibility allows it to be a strong opening card or a crucial piece in continuing a turn.
justification_2: This card's effect can be activated from the hand during either player’s turn to disrupt the opponent’s plays by preventing targeting, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.

card_text: If this card is Special Summoned by the effect of a "Metaphys" monster: You can banish all Set Spells and Traps on the field. If this card is banished, during the Standby Phase of the next turn: You can shuffle this banished card into the Deck; add 1 "Metaphys" card from your Deck to your hand, except "Metaphys Nephthys".
justification_1: This card's primary effect to banish Set Spells and Traps requires it to be Special Summoned by another "Metaphys" monster first, meaning it extends existing plays rather than starting them. The second effect activates after it's banished, further contributing to combo extension by adding other "Metaphys" cards.
justification_2: This card's primary effect, banishing Set Spells and Traps, is conditional on being Special Summoned by a "Metaphys" monster, implying it's not a primary starter but rather an extension of existing plays. Its second effect also requires itself to be banished, which is a consequence of plays rather than an initiation of them.

card_text: Gemini Monsters you control that are being treated as Effect Monsters cannot be destroyed by card effects.
justification_1: This card’s effect is reactive, specifically negating destruction effects targeting Effect Monsters. It’s a defensive handtrap, disrupting the opponent’s attempts to eliminate established threats, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.
justification_2: This card text only describes a passive effect that provides protection to Gemini Monsters. It does not have an activation condition that fits the criteria for Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet. It does not initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent, extend existing plays, break boards, or function as a "necessary brick."

card_text: If you control a "Mayakashi" card, other than "Yuki-Musume, the Ice Mayakashi" while this card is in your hand or GY: You can Special Summon this card, then send 1 Zombie monster from your Deck to the GY. You can only use this effect of "Yuki-Musume, the Ice Mayakashi" once per turn. You cannot Special Summon monsters from the Extra Deck, except "Mayakashi" monsters.
justification_1: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand if you control another "Mayakashi" monster, allowing it to start plays by sending a Zombie monster from the Deck to the GY, which is a key part of the "Mayakashi" archetype's strategy.
justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand if you control a "Mayakashi" card, and its effect sends a Zombie monster from the Deck to the GY, initiating a combo by setting up the graveyard for further plays.

card_text: If this card is added to your hand, except by drawing it: You can Special Summon this card. You can Tribute this card; Special Summon 1 "Tachyon" monster from your Deck or GY, except "Tachyon Cloudragon". If you Special Summon a Dragon Xyz Monster(s) (except during the Damage Step): You can target 1 of them; attach this card to that monster, from the GY (if it was there when the monster was Special Summoned) or hand (even if not), as material. You can only use each effect of "Tachyon Cloudragon" once per turn.
justification_1: This card's effects are designed to continue plays rather than start them. Its first effect requires it to be added to the hand by another card's effect to Special Summon itself. Its other effects require it to already be on the field (to tribute) or for you to have already Special Summoned a Dragon Xyz Monster (to attach as material). These conditions make it a classic extender that builds upon an already established board or combo.
justification_2: This card's primary effects are designed to continue or add value to existing plays. Its first effect allows it to be Special Summoned only after another card effect has added it to the hand, which requires a play to have already started. Its third effect attaches it as material to an Xyz monster you've just summoned, extending the value of that monster. While it can be Normal Summoned and Tributed to act as a starter, its main strategic role is to extend combos after a search.

card_text: Equip only to "White Magician Pikeru" or "Ebon Magician Curran"; it gains 800 ATK. During a turn that the equipped monster destroyed a Level 5 or higher monster by battle, you can Tribute the equipped monster and this card to Special Summon from your hand or Deck: 1 "Princess Pikeru" if you Tributed "White Magician Pikeru", or 1 "Princess Curran" if you Tributed "Ebon Magician Curran".
justification_1: This card's effect requires a specific monster to be equipped to it, and then for that equipped monster to destroy another monster by battle. This means it cannot initiate a play on its own and serves to extend existing board states or plays after a specific condition is met.
justification_2: This card is an Equip Spell that requires another monster on the field to be equipped to. It facilitates further plays by allowing the Special Summon of another monster from hand or Deck, but only after specific conditions involving the equipped monster are met, indicating it extends existing combos rather than starting them.

card_text: Neither player can Special Summon LIGHT monsters.

justification_1: This card can be discarded from the hand to search "Polymerization", which is a key component for many Fusion Summon strategies, allowing it to initiate a combo by setting up essential spell cards.
justification_2: The card can be discarded from the hand to search for "Polymerization," a key component for Fusion Summons, enabling the deck's main strategy and making it a strong opening play.

card_text: During the Battle Phase, you can send 1 card you control to the Graveyard, except this card, to end the Battle Phase.
justification_1: This card’s effect is activated from the hand during the Battle Phase, allowing the player to disrupt the opponent's progress by ending the phase, fitting the definition of a reactive, disruptive handtrap.
justification_2: This card's effect is an action that can be taken during the Battle Phase but does not meet the criteria for any of the defined categories. It doesn't initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from the hand, extend existing plays, break the opponent's board, or act as a necessary-but-undesirable combo piece.

card_text: You can send 1 "Black Luster Soldier" monster from your hand to the Graveyard; until your opponent's next End Phase, this card's ATK becomes 3000, and this card's name becomes "Black Luster Soldier". You can banish this card from your Graveyard; add 1 "Beginning Knight" or "Evening Twilight Knight" from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Super Soldier Soul" once per turn.

justification_2: This card requires another "Black Luster Soldier" monster to be sent from the hand to the graveyard to activate its primary effect, which buffs itself and changes its name. This means it needs a previous play or card in hand to function, fitting the role of an extender. While it can add a knight to the hand from the GY, this is a secondary effect and doesn't initiate a game-winning combo on its own.

card_text: Once per turn: You can send 1 "Ninja" monster from your hand to the GY; Set 1 "Ninjitsu Art" Spell/Trap directly from your Deck, except "Ninjitsu Art Notebook".
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand and directly searches a key Spell/Trap from the Deck, allowing a player to start their "Ninja" strategy by setting up essential combo pieces without needing other cards on the field.
justification_2: This card can be activated on its own during your Main Phase to send a "Ninja" monster from hand to the GY and directly search a "Ninjitsu Art" Spell/Trap from the Deck. This provides a strong initial advantage and begins a combo line, fitting the definition of a Starter.

card_text: If you control "Toon World": Target 1 face-up monster your opponent controls; Special Summon 1 Toon monster from your hand or Deck, whose Level is less than or equal to the Level/Rank of that monster, ignoring its Summoning conditions.
justification_1: This card's effect requires "Toon World" to be on the field to activate, meaning it cannot start a combo on its own and serves to extend existing Toon plays by Special Summoning monsters.
justification_2: This card's effect requires the presence of "Toon World" on the field to be activated, meaning it cannot be the first card to start a combo. It then allows for further plays by Special Summoning another Toon monster, extending the player's board presence and combo potential.

card_text: Once per turn: You can send 1 "Gem-Knight" or "Melodious" monster from your hand or Deck to the Graveyard, and if you do, this card's name becomes the sent monster's name, until the End Phase. If this card is in your Graveyard: You can banish 1 Fusion Monster from your Graveyard; Special Summon this card in Defense Position. You can only use this effect of "Crystal Rose" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can initiate a combo by sending a key "Gem-Knight" or "Melodious" monster from the Deck to the Graveyard, setting up future plays and functioning as a primary piece to start a turn's actions.
justification_2: This card can be sent from the Deck to the Graveyard to gain the name of a "Gem-Knight" or "Melodious" monster, enabling subsequent plays that require specific monster names, making it a good starting point for a combo.

card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters
Any monster sent from the hand or Main Deck to the GY is banished instead. When a Spell/Trap Card, or monster effect, is activated (Quick Effect): You can Tribute this card; negate the activation, and if you do, destroy that card. If this card is sent to the GY: You can add 1 Ritual Monster or 1 Ritual Spell from your Deck to your hand.
justification_1: The card can negate an opponent's Spell/Trap or monster effect activation as a Quick Effect by tributing itself, which can be done from the hand during either player's turn. This disruption from the hand is the hallmark of a handtrap.
justification_2: While its primary effect is a powerful negation that can be used on either player's turn, the Quick Effect to negate an activation by Tributing itself from the field makes it a reactive disruption. Its ability to be used from the hand is not explicitly stated, but its negation effect strongly aligns with the disruption role of handtraps when the context implies it can be used proactively during the opponent's turn or reactively to their plays. Furthermore, the second effect triggers upon being sent to the GY, allowing for further advantage, but the primary strategic value and classification comes from its disruptive negation.

card_text: Once per turn, during your Main Phase 1, you can select 1 face-up Synchro Monster you control. Remove that face-up Synchro Monster you control from play and inflict 600 damage to your opponent. If you use this effect, during your next Standby Phase the removed from play monster returns to the field.
justification_1: This card’s effect is reactive, allowing the player to interrupt the opponent’s plays by inflicting direct damage. It’s activated from the hand and designed to disrupt the opponent’s board presence, aligning with the characteristics of a handtrap.
justification_2: This card's effect requires control of a Synchro Monster to activate, and its primary function is to facilitate further plays by removing and then returning a monster from the field, extending existing combos rather than initiating them.

card_text: If this card is sent to the GY: Add 1 "Blue-Eyes White Dragon" from your Deck to your hand.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the Deck to search for "Blue-Eyes White Dragon," initiating plays and directly contributing to the deck's core strategy upon being sent to the GY.
justification_2: This card’s effect solely facilitates adding "Blue-Eyes White Dragon" to the hand. It does not initiate a combo on its own and is otherwise a brick that you wouldn't want to draw, fitting the definition of a Garnet.

card_text: Cannot be Normal Summoned/Set. Must be Special Summoned (from your hand) by controlling no cards, and cannot be Special Summoned by other ways. You cannot conduct your Battle Phase the turn you Special Summon this card. You cannot activate cards or effects, or Set any cards. During each of your Standby Phases, destroy this card unless you send your entire hand to the Graveyard (min.1).
justification_1: The card's effect allows it to be Special Summoned from the hand when the player controls no cards, and its primary function is to disrupt the opponent's board by preventing them from activating cards or effects or setting cards. This disruption, especially the inability for the opponent to activate cards or effects and the restriction on the player's own Battle Phase, aims to neutralize an established board or prevent a strong initial setup from the opponent, aligning with the strategic role of a board breaker.
justification_2: This card’s effect is entirely focused on triggering another card’s effect (destroying your hand), and the restrictive summoning condition (cannot be summoned under any circumstances) alongside the hand-destruction effect indicate it’s a “necessary brick” – a card included only to facilitate a combo, not something you'd want in your opening hand.

card_text: You can send this card from your hand or field to the GY, then target 1 Dragon monster you control; it gains 500 ATK/DEF until the end of your opponent's turn. If a Normal Monster(s) is sent to your GY, while this card is in the GY (except during the Damage Step): You can Special Summon this card, but banish it when it leaves the field. You can only use this effect of "Guardragon Promineses" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's primary effect of boosting a Dragon monster's ATK/DEF requires that monster to already be on the field. The second effect allows it to be Special Summoned from the GY, which typically occurs after a play has already begun and monsters have been sent to the GY, thus extending existing combos.
justification_2: This card's primary effect of Special Summoning itself from the GY requires a condition (Normal Monster(s) being sent to the GY) to be met first, indicating it's used to continue or extend plays after an initial setup, rather than starting them. The ATK/DEF boost also serves to enhance existing board presence.

card_text: During your opponent's Standby Phase: You can have as many monsters they control as possible gain 600 ATK, then destroy as many monsters they control as possible with 2500 or more ATK, and if you do, gain LP equal to the number of monsters destroyed x 500. (The ATK gain remains even if this card leaves the field.) You can only use this effect of "Gingerbread House" once per turn.

justification_2: This card activates during the opponent's Standby Phase to destroy multiple monsters on their field, specifically targeting those that meet or exceed 2500 ATK (even by boosting them first). This effect directly aims to eliminate or answer established threats on the opponent's board, making it a board-clearing card, fitting the definition of a Board Breaker.

card_text: Special Summon 1 "Mikanko" monster from your hand or GY, and if you do, equip it with this card, then you can Special Summon 1 monster from your opponent's GY to their field, but negate its effects. justification_2: This card's effect is a simple gain of Life Points based on a tribute, and it does not initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent, extend plays, break boards, or act as a necessary brick. It lacks the strategic complexity to fit any of the defined categories.

card_text:
