card_text: During the turn a "Gagaga" monster you control was destroyed by battle and sent to the Graveyard: Special Summon from your Graveyard as many monsters as possible that were destroyed by battle this turn, in face-up Defense Position, then take 600 damage for each monster Special Summoned by this effect. You can only activate 1 "Gagagaback" per turn.
justification_1: This card's effect activates *after* a "Gagaga" monster has already been destroyed by battle and sent to the Graveyard, meaning it requires a prior board state or event to be utilized. It then extends the play by Special Summoning multiple monsters, fitting the role of an extender.
justification_2: This card's effect activates in response to a specific condition (a "Gagaga" monster being destroyed by battle), allowing it to bring back fallen monsters and continue plays after an initial board presence has been established or disrupted. It doesn't initiate a combo but rather extends it.

card_text:
[ Pendulum Effect ] 
Once per turn: You can target 1 "Performapal" or "Odd-Eyes" monster you control; this turn, that face-up monster is treated as a Tuner, also its Level becomes 1 (even if this card leaves the field).

[ Monster Effect ] 
If this card, Special Summoned from the Extra Deck, is used as Synchro Material, banish it. When this card is Normal Summoned: You can target 1 Level 3 or lower "Performapal" or "Odd-Eyes" monster in your GY; Special Summon it, but it has its effects negated. Once per turn: You can target 1 card in your Pendulum Zone; Special Summon it, but it has its effects negated (if any), and if you do, immediately after this effect resolves, Synchro Summon 1 Synchro Monster using only that monster and this card.
justification_1: The monster effect allows for Synchro Summons by using this card and another monster, and the Pendulum effect can treat monsters as Tuners and change their levels. These effects extend plays by facilitating Synchro Summons and manipulating monster attributes, typically requiring an already established board or monster to utilize.
justification_2: This card’s primary function is to enable Synchro Summons, which requires an already established board (a Level 3 or lower monster) to utilize its effects. It extends the potential combos initiated by “Performapal” or “Odd-Eyes” monsters.

card_text: Your opponent cannot select a face-down Defense Position monster as an attack target.
justification_1: This card text describes a continuous effect that affects how the opponent can attack, but it does not have an activation condition that fits the criteria for Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet. It is a passive effect that is always active while the card is on the field.
justification_2: This card text is a rule that applies to all cards and effects in the game, rather than a specific card effect that can be activated or used strategically. It does not fit any of the defined categories.

card_text: If this card successfully attacks directly, you can look at 1 random card in your opponent's hand. If it is a Spell Card, send it to the Graveyard. If this card attacks, it is changed to Defense Position at the end of the Battle Phase. This card's battle position cannot be changed until the end of your next turn, except with a card effect.
justification_1: This card's effects are primarily combat-oriented and provide a minor advantage (revealing a random card from the opponent's hand) upon a successful direct attack. It doesn't initiate combos, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend plays, break boards, or serve as a necessary but undesirable brick. Its effects are passive buffs or triggered by battle, not fitting into the defined strategic roles.
justification_2: This card has an effect that triggers after a successful direct attack and a battle position restriction. It does not fit the criteria for any of the defined categories as it does not initiate combos, disrupt opponents from hand, extend plays, break boards, or serve as a necessary but undesirable draw.

card_text: Activate only if the 1 and only monster on your side of the field is "Chu-Ske the Mouse Fighter", "Monk Fighter", or "Master Monk". That monster cannot be destroyed as a result of battle, and is unaffected by any of your opponent's monster effects.
justification_1: The card's activation condition requires a specific monster to be already on the field, and its effect provides protection and continuation for that monster, fitting the role of an extender.
justification_2: This card's effect activates under a specific on-field condition (controlling only a particular monster), meaning it cannot start a play but can extend existing ones by protecting a key monster and potentially enabling further actions if that monster is the foundation of a combo.

card_text: Select up to 2 Monster Card(s) from your opponent's Graveyard. Remove the selected card(s) from play.
justification_1: This card’s effect allows the player to remove cards from the opponent's Graveyard, which can disrupt their plays and remove key resources. While it doesn't directly attack established threats on the field, it weakens the opponent's potential for future plays, serving as a form of disruption that is often best utilized when going second.
justification_2: This card's effect is to remove cards from the opponent's Graveyard and banish them, which directly disrupts their plays and weakens their established board or potential future plays, fitting the role of a board breaker.

card_text: During either player's Battle Step, while an attack is occurring: You can banish this card from your Graveyard; negate the attack. You can only use the effect of "Bacon Saver" once per Duel.
justification_1: While this card's effect negates an attack and is powerful, its primary activation condition (banishing from the Graveyard) means it needs to have been sent there first, and its effect is not as proactive or combo-starting as a typical starter. Its ability to disrupt plays during either player's turn, particularly during an attack, aligns it with disruptive handtrap-like effects even though it is activated from the graveyard.
justification_2: While its primary effect is to negate an attack, which occurs during the Battle Step of either player's turn and does not necessarily require this card to be on the field, the ability to banish it from the Graveyard to negate an attack is a form of disruption that doesn't rely on an established board. However, its restriction to "once per Duel" limits its ability to be a consistent starter or extender. Its primary function aligns with disrupting the opponent's battle phase, similar to how handtraps disrupt other phases. Given the options, "Handtrap" is the closest fit as it is a reactive disruption that can be accessed from the Graveyard (which is often a resource pool accessible without a field presence, akin to hand access). It doesn't fit the other categories as it doesn't start a combo, extend a combo, break a board, or is a necessary brick.

card_text: Target 3 Psychic-Type monsters in your Graveyard; shuffle all 3 into the Deck, then draw 2 cards.
justification_1: This card’s effect requires a pre-existing board presence (specifically, 3 Psychic-type monsters in the Graveyard) to function, extending the current plays and enabling additional value after a combo has started.
justification_2: This card's effect requires specific cards to be in the Graveyard to activate, meaning it cannot be used as an initial play but can extend your plays by providing card advantage after you have already established your GY.

card_text:
If you control a "Constellar" monster, except "Constellar Caduceus": You can Special Summon this card from your hand. During your Main Phase: You can add 1 "Constellar" Spell/Trap from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each of the previous effects of "Constellar Caduceus" once per turn. A "Constellar" Xyz Monster that has this card as material gains this effect.
● If this card battles a LIGHT or DARK monster, before damage calculation: You can banish that monster.
justification_1: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand if you control another "Constellar" monster, and it also searches for a crucial "Constellar" Spell/Trap, making it capable of initiating your deck's strategy.
justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand by controlling a "Constellar" monster, and then searches a "Constellar" Spell/Trap, making it capable of starting a combo and advancing your board by adding further plays.

card_text: Each time a Spell Card is activated, place 1 Spell Counter on this card when that Spell resolves. During the End Phase: You can remove 3 Spell Counters from this card, then target 1 of your banished Spells; add 1 card with the target's name from your Deck to your hand. You can only use this effect of "Megistric Maginician" once per turn.
justification_1: This card requires multiple Spell activations to accumulate Spell Counters and a banished Spell to activate its effect, meaning it cannot initiate a combo by itself. Its effect to add a banished Spell (or a copy) back to hand during the End Phase helps to replenish resources and set up plays for subsequent turns, effectively extending your overall strategy and ensuring long-term value after initial plays have been made.
justification_2: The card generates resources over time (Spell Counters) and provides a slow search effect during the End Phase, requiring specific conditions (3 Spell Counters, a banished Spell). It does not initiate a main combo (Starter), disrupt the opponent from hand (Handtrap), directly extend immediate plays or push through interruptions (Extender), or clear an opponent's board (Board Breaker). It is also not a card exclusively included as a necessary but undesirable component for a combo (Garnet). Its role is more akin to a long-term resource engine.

card_text: If this card is in your hand, field, or GY: You can target 1 "Springans" Xyz Monster you control; attach this card to it as material. You can banish this card and 1 "Springans" monster with a different name from your GY; add 1 "Springans" card from your Deck to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Springans Branga" once per turn.
justification_1: The first effect requires a "Springans" Xyz Monster already on the field to attach itself as material, extending the Xyz monster's utility. The second effect requires two "Springans" monsters in the GY to activate, meaning some plays have already occurred to fill the GY, allowing it to search for further combo pieces or provide recovery. Neither effect can initiate a play by itself; they both build upon an already established game state.
justification_2: The first effect requires a "Springans" Xyz Monster to already be on the field, allowing the card to provide additional material, which extends the utility or longevity of an existing Xyz play. The second effect, which searches a "Springans" card from the Deck by banishing itself and another monster from the GY, acts as a way to continue or recover resources for further plays after the initial combo has begun and filled the GY. Both effects fit the role of extending plays rather than starting them or disrupting the opponent.

card_text: The equipped monster gains 700 ATK. During each of your Standby Phases, the equipped monster loses 200 ATK.
justification_1: This card only provides a stat boost and a continuous ATK reduction, with no activation condition that fits any of the defined categories or a strategic role beyond a minor stat buff.
justification_2: This card is an Equip Spell that provides a stat boost and a continuous ATK reduction. It does not have an activation condition that aligns with the defined categories, nor does it disrupt the opponent, start combos, extend plays, break boards, or act as a necessary but undesirable draw. Its effect is a simple stat modification.

card_text: One time, during the rest of this turn, if either player(s) rolls a die (or dice), you can have the player(s) re-roll. (You can only gain this effect once per turn.)
justification_1: The card's effect is passive and conditional, requiring a die roll by either player. It doesn't directly initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent, extend plays, break boards, or act as a necessary but undesirable draw. Its utility is limited and doesn't fit any of the defined strategic roles.
justification_2: This card's effect is a passive ability that modifies die rolls. It does not fit the criteria of a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet, as it does not initiate plays, disrupt opponents from hand, extend combos, break boards, or act as a necessary but undesirable draw.

card_text:
1 Zombie-Type Tuner + 1 or more non-Tuner Zombie-Type monsters
If this card is Special Summoned: You can banish 1 Zombie-Type monster from your Graveyard; this card gains ATK equal to the banished monster's original ATK, until the end of this turn. If this card on the field is destroyed by battle or card effect and sent to the Graveyard: You can target 1 of your banished Zombie-Type monsters with 0 DEF; return it to the Graveyard. You can only Special Summon "Shiranui Shogunsaga(s)" once per turn.
justification_1: This card requires a Synchro Summon, meaning other monsters must already be on the field to facilitate its own Special Summon. Its effects then build upon an established board by gaining ATK or returning banished monsters, serving to extend plays.
justification_2: This card is a Synchro Monster that requires other monsters to be on the field for its summon. While its effects can provide a temporary ATK boost or graveyard recursion, they do not initiate a combo on their own and are more useful for continuing or enhancing an established play.

card_text:
The first time each "Witchcrafter" monster you control would be destroyed each turn, by battle or card effect, it is not destroyed. You can only use 1 of the following effects of "Witchcrafter Bystreet" per turn, and only once that turn.
● If a "Witchcrafter" monster you control would discard to activate an effect, you can send this card from your field to the GY instead.
● During your End Phase, if you control a "Witchcrafter" monster, while this card is in your GY: You can place this card face-up in your Spell & Trap Zone.
justification_1: This card primarily offers protection and field presence for "Witchcrafter" monsters, allowing plays to continue and recover from potential disruptions. Its effect to be sent from the field to the GY to substitute a discard for a "Witchcrafter" monster's effect acts as an extender by enabling further plays. The effect to place itself in the Spell & Trap Zone from the GY also allows for future setup, but the primary role demonstrated by the discard substitution is extending plays.
justification_2: This card provides protection for "Witchcrafter" monsters and acts as a resource by being able to be set from the GY, extending plays and providing ongoing advantage rather than initiating them.

card_text:
Activate 1 of these effects;
● Target 3 of your FIRE monsters that are banished and/or in your GY; shuffle 2 of the 3 into the Deck, and if you do, Special Summon the remaining monster, but for the rest of this turn, its effects are negated, also it cannot attack.
● If you control a FIRE Ritual, Fusion, Synchro, Xyz, or Link Monster(s) whose current ATK is different from its original ATK: You can target 1 card on the field; destroy it.
You can only activate 1 "Salamangreat Charge" per turn.
justification_1: The card's first effect requires three FIRE monsters to already be in the Graveyard or banished, meaning you must have already started your plays. It then Special Summons one of those monsters, providing an additional body on the field to continue or extend your combos, fitting the definition of an Extender. The second effect also requires an established board, further cementing its role as a card that enhances existing plays rather than initiating them.
justification_2: The card's first effect requires having at least three FIRE monsters in the Graveyard or banished, a condition met only after initial plays have started. It Special Summons a monster, which is used to continue combos. The second effect also requires an established board. Therefore, its role is to extend plays, not initiate them.

card_text: Once per turn, when the battle position of a Rock-Type monster you control is changed: You can target 1 Spell/Trap Card your opponent controls; destroy that target.
justification_1: This card’s effect is used proactively to destroy an opponent’s Spell/Trap Card when their Rock-Type monster’s position is changed, directly countering their plays and removing a threat – a classic board-breaking function.
justification_2: This card's effect to destroy an opponent's Spell/Trap card is triggered by a battle position change of a Rock-Type monster. While it can disrupt the opponent, it requires another Rock-Type monster to have its battle position changed first, meaning it needs an established play to activate and extend beyond the initial setup. It doesn't initiate a combo or directly break a board on its own, nor is it a handtrap.

card_text: The equipped monster gains 500 ATK and DEF for each Spell/Trap Card you control.
justification_1: This card provides a passive ATK/DEF boost to an equipped monster based on the number of Spell/Trap Cards the controller has. It does not activate a condition, disrupt an opponent, extend plays, break boards, or function as a necessary but undesirable draw like a Garnet.
justification_2: This card’s effect is entirely dependent on another card’s presence (Spell/Trap Cards). It doesn't initiate plays or disrupt opponents, it merely provides a bonus based on another card's effect – aligning with the definition of a “Garnet”.

card_text:
1 "Melodious Maestra" monster + 2 "Melodious" monsters
(Quick Effect): You can banish (until the End Phase) any number of "Melodious" monsters you control, then you can return up to that many face-up cards your opponent controls to the hand. You can only use this effect of "Flowering Etoile the Melodious Magnificat" once per turn. If this face-up Fusion Summoned card in its owner's control leaves the field because of an opponent's card: You can Special Summon 1 "Melodious" monster from your Deck or Extra Deck, except "Flowering Etoile the Melodious Magnificat".
justification_1: Once Fusion Summoned, this card's Quick Effect allows it to return multiple face-up cards your opponent controls to their hand. This effect directly neutralizes or eliminates established threats on the opponent's field, functioning as a powerful form of disruption and board clearing, which is a key characteristic of a Board Breaker, especially given its Quick Effect timing.
justification_2: This card is an Extra Deck "boss monster," which serves as the end goal or payoff of its deck's primary combo. It does not fit the other categories: it's not a Starter (it must be Fusion Summoned after establishing a board), not an Extender (it's the goal, not a mid-combo piece), not a Handtrap (it activates on the field), and not a Board Breaker in the traditional sense, as it is part of your own established board rather than a card used from the hand to clear an opponent's.

card_text: Select 1 face-up "Karakuri" monster on the field. Until the End Phase, it gains 1000 ATK but its effect(s) is negated.
justification_1: This card's effect provides a stat boost and negates effects, but it doesn't fit the primary activation conditions or strategic roles of a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet. It is a field spell or continuous spell that requires a monster on the field to activate its effect.
justification_2: This card's effect requires an existing "Karakuri" monster on the field to target, meaning it extends existing plays rather than starting them. It can increase the ATK of a monster and negate its effects, which can be useful for pushing through damage or changing a monster's role within a combo.

card_text:
3 "Frightfur" monsters
Must be Fusion Summoned and cannot be Special Summoned by other ways. If this card battles, your opponent cannot activate cards or effects until the end of the Damage Step. When this card destroys an opponent's monster by battle and sends it to the Graveyard: You can Special Summon that monster to your field, but its ATK is halved. You can only use this effect of "Frightfur Chimera" once per turn. This card gains 300 ATK for each monster you control that is owned by your opponent.
justification_1: This card is a Fusion Monster that requires a specific Fusion Summon condition and provides benefits during battle. It doesn't fit the criteria of a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet, as its primary function is to be a strong monster on the field for offensive purposes, rather than initiating plays, disrupting the opponent from hand, extending combos, or breaking boards.
justification_2: This card is a Fusion Monster that requires specific materials and has effects that activate after it is summoned or destroys a monster in battle. It does not fulfill the criteria for starting a combo, disrupting the opponent from hand, extending existing plays, breaking boards, or being a necessary but undesirable draw.

card_text: FLIP: Destroy all Level 4 monsters your opponent controls.
justification_1: The card's effect, "Destroy all Level 4 monsters your opponent controls," is used to eliminate or answer established threats on the opponent's field. While it's a FLIP effect and thus slower and more reactive than typical modern board breakers, its strategic role is to dismantle or clear a specific portion of the opponent's board, aligning with the definition of a Board Breaker. It does not start combos, extend plays, or disrupt from the hand.

card_text: When your opponent activates a monster effect on the field: You can banish 1 Pyro monster from your GY; inflict 500 damage to your opponent, then, if you banished a "Volcanic" monster to activate this effect, you can negate that effect. During your opponent's End Phase: You can target up to 2 of your "Volcanic" monsters that are banished and/or in your GY; place them on the bottom of your Deck in any order. You can only use each effect of "Volcanic Inferno" once per turn.
justification_1: This card is a trap that provides reactive disruption by negating an opponent's monster effect from the field. It is not a Handtrap, as it must be set first. It doesn't start or extend your own plays, and it isn't a proactive Board Breaker. Since it's a key interactive card for its archetype and not an undesirable draw, it's not a Garnet. Therefore, it does not fit into any of the specified categories.
justification_2: The card's primary effect is an on-field monster negation, which serves as a form of disruption during the opponent's turn. However, it does not activate from the hand, so it does not fit the definition of a "Handtrap". It also does not initiate a main combo ("Starter"), continue or extend the player's own plays ("Extender"), or broadly clear an opponent's established board ("Board Breaker"). Furthermore, it is not a card undesirable to draw but necessary for a combo ("Garnet"). Its strategic role as an on-field interruption does not align with the specific activation conditions or roles defined for the other categories.

card_text: Target 1 "Heraldic Beast" monster in your Graveyard; Special Summon it.
justification_1: This card's effect requires a "Heraldic Beast" monster to be in the Graveyard to activate, meaning it needs a preceding play to establish that condition. It then allows for further combo extension by bringing back a monster from the Graveyard.
justification_2: This card requires a monster to already be in the Graveyard to activate its effect of Special Summoning, meaning it needs another card or play to have occurred first to extend your board or combo.

card_text:
If you control no monsters: You can Special Summon 1 "Rose Dragon" monster or 1 Plant monster from your hand. You can only use this effect of "White Rose Cloister" once per turn. During your Draw Phase, before you draw: You can declare 1 card type (Monster, Spell, or Trap); reveal the top card of your Deck, and if it is the declared card type, apply this effect for the rest of this turn (even if this card leaves the field).
● Level 7 or higher Synchro Monsters you control gain 1000 ATK.
justification_1: The card's primary effect allows Special Summoning a monster from the hand when you control no monsters, making it an ideal first play to establish a presence on the field and begin a combo, fitting the definition of a Starter.
justification_2: The card's primary effect allows it to Special Summon a monster from hand if you control no monsters, making it an ideal card to open with on an empty board to begin your main combo line and establish a presence.

card_text: If a Level 3 or lower Normal Monster you control battles, during the Damage Step you can pay Life Points in multiples of 100. The ATK of the opponent's monster is reduced by the amount of Life Points you paid, until the End Phase. (You cannot pay more Life Points than the ATK of the opponent's monster.)
justification_1: This card's effect is a passive ability that modifies battle calculations. It doesn't activate to start a combo, disrupt an opponent, extend plays, break a board, or act as a necessary but undesirable draw.
justification_2: This card's effect is a continuous effect that modifies battle damage. It does not fit the activation condition or strategic role of any of the defined categories. It does not initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent from hand, extend a combo, break a board, or act as a necessary brick.

card_text:
2 Level 3 Rock-Type monsters
Once per turn, during either player's turn: You can detach 1 Xyz Material from this card, then target 1 face-up monster your opponent controls; its ATK becomes 0, and if it does, its effects are negated. These effects last until the end of this turn. Once per turn: You can target 1 monster on the field with 0 ATK; destroy it.
justification_1: This card requires the presence of two Level 3 Rock-Type monsters to be summoned, indicating it's used after an initial play has been made to further a combo or disrupt the opponent, fitting the role of an extender rather than a starter or board breaker. Its effects can be used during either player's turn to weaken opponent monsters.
justification_2: This card’s effect is triggered from the hand during either player’s turn, specifically to disrupt the opponent’s monsters by reducing their ATK to 0 and negating their effects. This reactive, disruptive ability aligns with the definition of a handtrap.

card_text: For each Equip Card equipped to this card, it gains 1 additional attack during each Battle Phase.
justification_1: This card's effect is a continuous effect that modifies its own stats and battle capabilities. It does not activate to disrupt the opponent, start a combo, extend a combo, break a board, or act as a necessary brick. Therefore, it does not fit into any of the provided categories.
justification_2: This card's effect is a passive bonus that increases its offensive capabilities based on equipped cards. It does not initiate plays, disrupt opponents, extend combos, break boards, or serve as a necessary brick.

card_text:
5 Spellcaster monsters
Must be Fusion Summoned. If this card is Fusion Summoned using 5 Spellcaster monsters with different names: You can destroy all cards your opponent controls. This face-up card on the field cannot be Tributed, nor used as Fusion Material, also it cannot be destroyed by card effects.
justification_1: This card's primary effect allows it to destroy all cards on the opponent's field if Fusion Summoned with specific conditions, making it a powerful tool for clearing established boards, especially when going second.
justification_2: This card's primary effect allows it to destroy all cards on the opponent's field upon successful Fusion Summon with specific materials. This directly addresses and neutralizes an established opponent's board, fitting the definition of a board breaker, especially when played going second.

card_text: Once per turn: You can Normal Summon 1 Level 4 LIGHT Thunder-Type monster from your hand, except "Pahunder", as an additional Normal Summon.
justification_1: This card allows an additional Normal Summon of a specific type of monster from the hand, enabling the start of combo plays without needing other cards on the field or in hand to initiate.
justification_2: This card allows an additional Normal Summon of a Level 4 LIGHT Thunder-Type monster from the hand, which can directly start a combo or extend existing plays by providing additional monsters on the field, fitting the definition of a starter.

card_text: A fiend that dwells in a blinding curtain of smoke.
justification_1: The provided text is flavor text and does not contain any card effect. Therefore, it cannot be classified into any of the given categories.
justification_2: This card text is a flavor text and does not contain any effects that can be activated or used to fulfill any of the specified card categories.

card_name: Scarm, Malebranche of the Burning Abyss
card_text:
If you control a monster that is not a "Burning Abyss" monster, destroy this card. You can only use 1 of these effects of "Scarm, Malebranche of the Burning Abyss" per turn, and only once that turn.
● If you control no Spell/Trap Cards: You can Special Summon this card from your hand.
● During the End Phase, if this card is in the Graveyard because it was sent there this turn: You can add 1 Level 3 DARK Fiend-Type monster from your Deck to your hand, except "Scarm, Malebranche of the Burning Abyss".
justification_1: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand if you control no Spell/Trap Cards, which is a condition easily met at the start of a turn. Once Special Summoned, it can then be sent to the Graveyard to search for another Level 3 DARK Fiend-Type monster, initiating a combo or engine.
justification_2: This card can be Special Summoned from the hand if you control no Spell/Trap Cards, allowing it to initiate plays by itself and then enable further plays by searching for other Burning Abyss monsters.

card_text:
Equip only to a Warrior-Type monster. Apply this effect, depending on its battle position.
● Attack Position: It gains ATK equal to its original DEF.
● Defense Position: It gains DEF equal to its original ATK.
justification_1: This card is an Equip Spell that only provides stat boosts based on the monster's battle position. It does not activate an effect to initiate plays, disrupt the opponent, extend combos, break boards, or serve as a necessary but undesirable brick.
justification_2: This card is an Equip Spell that provides ATK/DEF boosts based on the original stats of the equipped monster and its battle position. It does not activate from the hand, disrupt the opponent, extend a combo, or break a board. It is a stat booster without a specific strategic role that fits the provided categories.

card_text: If you control no monsters, or all monsters you control are Warrior monsters, you can Normal Summon this card without Tributing. If your EARTH Warrior monster battles, after damage calculation: You can target 1 Spell/Trap your opponent controls; destroy it, then all "War Rock" monsters you currently control gain 200 ATK until the end of your opponent's turn. You can only use this effect of "War Rock Mammud" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be Normal Summoned without Tributing if you control no monsters, allowing it to be a standalone first play to establish a monster on the field. This initiates the deck's main line of play by providing a body for battle and enabling further "War Rock" effects, fitting the definition of a Starter that begins progress towards the deck's win condition.
justification_2: This card can be Normal Summoned without Tributing if you control no monsters, allowing it to be easily played as an opening move to establish a monster on the field and begin the deck's strategy. Its ability to be summoned without additional resources makes it a primary card to start your main line of play.

card_text: If your opponent's monster attacks, during damage calculation (Quick Effect): You can discard this card; the damage you take from that battle is halved. When your opponent activates an effect that would inflict damage to you (even during the Damage Step) (Quick Effect): You can discard this card; the damage you take from that effect is halved. If this card is sent from the field to the GY: You can add 1 "Gouki" card from your Deck to your hand, except "Gouki Octostretch". You can only use this effect of "Gouki Octostretch" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can be activated from the hand during either player's turn to halve damage taken from battles or effects, serving as a defensive disruption to the opponent's plays.
justification_2: This card can be activated from the hand during the opponent's turn to mitigate battle damage or effect damage, directly disrupting the opponent's plays and fitting the definition of a handtrap.

card_text: You can target 1 monster you control and 1 card on the field; Special Summon this card from your hand, and if you do, destroy them. If this card is sent from the field to your GY: You can target 1 other monster in your GY; shuffle it into the Deck, then add 1 Level 1 Plant monster from your Deck or GY to your hand. You can only use each effect of "Sauge de Fleur" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's primary effect allows it to Special Summon itself from the hand by targeting a monster you control, which means it requires an existing board presence to activate. ● (Quick Effect): You can target 1 face-up monster on the field that was Special Summoned from the Extra Deck; until the end of this turn, that target's ATK becomes 0, also that target's effects are negated.
justification_1: The card can be discarded from the hand to search for a "Nekroz" Spell/Trap, initiating plays and setting up future turns, aligning with the role of a starter.
justification_2: This card's effect requires an already established board (a “Nekroz” monster) to use, allowing it to continue or extend plays after the initial board setup. The Quick Effect specifically targets an Extra Deck monster, further extending potential combos.

card_text: While this card is in face-up Attack Position, Special Summoned Level 5 or higher monsters on the field cannot activate their effects.
justification_1: This card’s effect is activated from the hand during the opponent’s turn to negate monster effects, disrupting their plays and fitting the definition of a reactive handtrap.
justification_2: This card's effect is a continuous effect that impacts the opponent's monsters on the field. It doesn't activate from the hand, initiate a combo, extend a play, or break a board on its own. It is a passive effect that applies while the card is on the field.

card_text:
1 Tuner + 1+ non-Tuner monsters with 2500 original ATK & DEF
While this Synchro Summoned card is in your Monster Zone, your opponent cannot target it with card effects, also it cannot be destroyed by your opponent's card effects. While you have 25 or more cards in your GY, this Synchro Summoned card gains 100 ATK/DEF for each card on the field and in the GYs. Once per turn, when a Spell Card is activated, while your opponent has 25 or more cards in their GY (Quick Effect): You can negate the activation, and if you do, destroy that card.
justification_1: This card is an Extra Deck monster that serves as a "boss monster" or the payoff for a combo. It does not start plays (not a Starter), nor does it extend them (not an Extender). Its effects are on-field, so it is not a Handtrap, and it lacks the effects to proactively dismantle an opponent's board (not a Board Breaker). Its role is to be a powerful end-board piece, which is a strategic role not covered by the provided categories.
justification_2: This card is an Extra Deck "boss monster" that serves as the end goal of a specific strategy. It does not fit the other categories: it cannot start plays (it must be Synchro Summoned), it does not extend combos, it cannot be activated from the hand, and it isn't a Garnet. While it has a disruptive negation effect, this ability is highly conditional and part of its overall role as a win condition, rather than a dedicated Board Breaker.

card_text: Target 1 of your banished "Crystron" monsters; Special Summon it, and if you do, if your opponent controls any face-up monsters, change their DEF to 0. During either player's turn, except the turn this card was sent to the Graveyard, when a card or effect is activated that targets a "Crystron" monster you control: You can banish this card from your Graveyard; negate that effect.
justification_1: The card can be activated from the Graveyard during either player's turn to protect a "Crystron" monster by banishing itself, and this effect can be used to extend plays by ensuring a combo piece remains on the field or to disrupt the opponent's plays without needing to be on the field initially. The first effect to special summon also requires a banished "Crystron" to target, implying a prior play has been made.
justification_2: The primary effect requires a banished "Crystron" monster, meaning a previous play must have occurred to banish it. This allows for further combo extension. The graveyard effect also requires this card to have been sent to the graveyard already, further supporting its role as an extender rather than a starter.

card_text: If you control a "Horus" monster and "King's Sarcophagus": You can reveal this card in your hand, then send 1 card from your hand or field to the GY; send 1 card on the field to the GY. If a face-up "Horus" monster(s) you control, except "Horus the Black Flame Deity", or a face-up "King's Sarcophagus" you control, leaves the field by an opponent's card effect (except during the Damage Step): You can Special Summon this card from your hand, then you can send all other monsters on the field to the GY. You can only use each effect of "Horus the Black Flame Deity" once per turn.
justification_1: The card's effects require an established board state (controlling a "Horus" monster and "King's Sarcophagus"). Its primary role is to add value and resilience to this setup, either by providing removal from the hand or by Special Summoning itself as a powerful follow-up play if your board is disrupted by the opponent, thereby extending your plays through an interruption.
justification_2: The card's primary strategic role is to eliminate threats on the field. Its first effect provides targeted removal by sending a card on the field to the GY, and its second effect is a powerful board wipe, activating from the hand to send all other monsters on the field to the GY in response to an opponent removing your key cards. Both effects are designed to dismantle an opponent's established board.

card_text: Gains 400 ATK for each Aqua monster you control. Once per turn: You can activate this effect; you can Normal Summon 1 WATER monster during your Main Phase this turn, in addition to your Normal Summon/Set (you can only gain this effect once per turn), also you cannot Normal or Special Summon monsters for the rest of this turn, except WATER monsters.
justification_1: This card grants an additional Normal Summon for a WATER monster during your Main Phase, allowing the player to summon two monsters in one turn from the hand without needing prior setup. This ability is crucial for establishing board presence and initiating main combos in a WATER-themed deck, despite the subsequent summoning restriction.
justification_2: This card's effect provides an additional Normal Summon for a WATER monster, effectively allowing you to put two monsters on the field with your Normal Summons alone. For a WATER-focused deck, this can be the primary way to initiate combos and establish a board, making it capable of beginning a main line of play by itself. The subsequent summoning restriction to WATER monsters reinforces its role as a dedicated opening for a specific strategy.

card_text: Destroy as many monsters on the field as possible, then Special Summon in Attack Position, 2 "Kaiju" monsters with different names from your Deck (1 on each side), but they cannot change their battle positions, and must attack, if able. During your Main Phase, except the turn this card was sent to the GY: You can banish this card from your GY; add 1 "Kaiju" monster from your Deck to your hand. You can only activate 1 "Interrupted Kaiju Slumber" per turn.
justification_1: This card's primary effect is to destroy multiple monsters on the field, clearing the opponent's board. While it also summons Kaiju monsters, the immediate destruction of existing threats aligns it with the strategic role of a board breaker, particularly for going second. The GY effect is a secondary benefit that helps extend future plays but does not define the card's initial role.
justification_2: This card's primary effect destroys all monsters on the field, directly addressing an established opponent's board. While it has an additional effect to search a "Kaiju" monster from the GY, its immediate impact is clearing the opponent's monsters, making it a powerful board breaker, especially when going second.

card_text: This card can only be activated during Main Phase 1. Select 1 face-up monster on the field. Each time the selected monster inflicts Battle Damage to your opponent's Life Points this turn, your opponent discards 1 random card.
justification_1: This card's effect activates during a specific phase (Main Phase 1) and targets a monster on the field to inflict additional discard effects based on battle damage. It does not fit the criteria of a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet as it requires specific field conditions and does not directly initiate a combo, disrupt an opponent from hand, extend plays, break a board, or serve as a necessary brick.
justification_2: This card is a Spell card that activates on the field during the Main Phase. It does not meet the activation conditions of a Starter (no self-summoning or direct combo enabling), a Handtrap (not activated from hand during opponent's turn), an Extender (doesn't continue existing plays), a Board Breaker (doesn't disrupt the opponent's board), or a Garnet (it's a powerful effect that you'd want to draw).

card_text: Fusion Summon 1 "Magikey" Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, using monsters from your hand or field as Fusion Material, OR Ritual Summon 1 "Magikey" Ritual Monster from your hand, by Tributing monsters from your hand or field whose total Levels equal or exceed its Level. If you control a Normal Monster, you can also send 1 Normal Monster from your Deck to the GY as material or Tribute.
justification_1: This card facilitates Fusion or Ritual Summons, acting as a crucial combo piece. Crucially, its powerful secondary effect (sending a Normal Monster from Deck to GY as material) only activates "if you control a Normal Monster," meaning it's most effective when you've already made a play or established a presence on the field, allowing you to push for further summons and resource generation. This fits the definition of an extender that continues or extends plays after something has started.
justification_2: This card directly facilitates the main summoning mechanics (Fusion and Ritual) for the "Magikey" archetype, allowing the player to bring out powerful monsters using materials from hand or field. It can be used as a primary turn 1 play to establish a board and begin the deck's core strategy, making it a key card to open with. The ability to use a Normal Monster from the Deck as material is an additional benefit, but not a prerequisite for its primary function.

card_text:
3 Tuners + 1+ non-Tuner Synchro Monsters
Must first be Synchro Summoned. Gains 500 ATK for each Tuner in your GY. Cannot be destroyed by your opponent's card effects. Once per turn, when your opponent's monster effect is activated, or when an opponent's monster declares an attack (Quick Effect): You can activate this effect; banish this card, also banish all cards your opponent controls. Once per turn, during your next End Phase after this card was banished by its own effect: Special Summon this banished card.
justification_1: While typically summoned as a powerful boss monster going first, this card's Quick Effect allows it to banish itself and *all* cards your opponent controls, providing a complete board wipe. This powerful ability to eliminate established threats on the opponent's field fits the definition of a Board Breaker, even though it's deployed as part of a proactive combo rather than activated from hand going second.
justification_2: This card's Quick Effect can be activated on the opponent's turn in response to a monster effect or attack, banishing all cards your opponent controls. This powerful board-wiping effect directly serves to eliminate established threats and neutralize the opponent's set-up, fitting the strategic role of a board breaker, even if it is summoned as an end-of-combo boss monster.

card_text: Each monster you control that is equipped with a "Dragunity" monster(s) gains 500 ATK. Once per turn, you can select 1 face-up monster you control, and equip it with 1 Dragon-Type "Dragunity" monster from your hand.
justification_1: This card's effect allows you to equip a "Dragunity" monster from your hand to a monster you control. This requires a monster already on the field to start, serving to extend your plays by providing further synergy and boosts for your "Dragunity" monsters.
justification_2: This card's primary effect allows a player to equip a "Dragunity" monster from their hand to a monster they control. This action extends the player's existing board state and combo by providing additional resources or synergy, which is characteristic of an extender. It doesn't typically start a play on its own but rather builds upon an established board.

card_text:
2 Level 6 monsters
You can detach 1 material from this card, then target 1 face-up monster your opponent controls; take control of it until the End Phase, but it cannot activate its effects or declare an attack. If a Trap is on the field: You can Tribute 1 monster, and if you do, destroy 1 face-up card on the field. You can only use each effect of "Jinzo - Layered" once per turn.
justification_1: This card is an Extra Deck monster whose effects are designed to dismantle the opponent's established board. Its first effect takes control of an opponent's monster, and its second effect destroys a face-up card, both of which serve to eliminate or answer threats on the opponent's field, making it most useful for breaking through an opponent's setup.
justification_2: This card's effects are primarily used to disrupt the opponent's board by taking control of their monsters or destroying face-up cards when a Trap is present, making it most effective when going second to break established boards.

card_text: This card can be treated as 2 Tributes for the Tribute Summon of a WIND Normal Monster.
justification_1: This card’s effect is entirely dependent on another card’s ability and is designed to facilitate a specific combo, making it a “necessary brick” rather than a card you’d want to draw. It doesn’t contribute to your own plays directly.
justification_2: This card's effect is a passive benefit that does not initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent, extend plays, or break boards. It does not fit any of the defined categories.

card_text:
"Naturia Beast" + "Naturia Barkion"
A Fusion Summon of this card can only be conducted with the above Fusion Material Monsters. While this card is face-up on the field, you can negate the activation of a Spell/Trap Card, and destroy it, by removing from play 1 card from your Graveyard, then sending the top card of your Deck to the Graveyard.
justification_1: This card is a Fusion Monster that requires specific Fusion Materials to be summoned. Its effect, negating Spell/Trap activation, is a powerful in-play effect that disrupts the opponent's strategy once it is on the field. It does not activate from the hand, initiate a combo by itself without other cards, extend an existing combo, or primarily serve to break an established board. Therefore, it does not fit the defined categories.
justification_2: This card is a Fusion Monster that provides a continuous negation effect on the field once summoned. It does not fit the criteria for a Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, or Garnet as it requires specific Fusion Materials to be summoned and its effect is primarily for on-field disruption rather than initiating plays, disrupting from hand, extending existing combos, or being a brick.

card_text: You can discard 1 other EARTH Fairy monster; Special Summon this card from your hand, then you can place 1 "Gravekeeper's Trap" from your Deck face-up in your Spell & Trap Zone. (Quick Effect): You can banish this card from your field or GY, then target up to 3 cards in any GY(s), or up to 5 if "Exchange of the Spirit" is on your field or in your GY; shuffle them into the Deck. You can only use each effect of "Mudora the Sword Oracle" once per turn.
justification_1: This card can initiate a main line of play by Special Summoning itself from the hand (with a discard cost for an easily accessible archetype/attribute) and setting a powerful Trap Card directly from the Deck, making meaningful progress towards establishing a board and enabling further combo potential or immediate disruption.
justification_2: The card's first effect allows it to Special Summon itself from the hand by discarding another specific monster (EARTH Fairy). This effect helps to extend your plays by providing an additional monster on the field and potentially setting up a Trap Card from the Deck, but it is not a standalone starter as it requires a discard cost. While its Quick Effect from the GY provides disruption, it does not activate from the hand, precluding it from the "Handtrap" category as defined. Its primary role in advancing your own board aligns with the definition of an Extender.

card_text: At the start of your Draw Phase, instead of drawing a card, you can pay 1500 LP to activate this Skill. Banish 1 Dinosaur monster and 1 non-Dinosaur monster from your hand and/or GY; Special Summon 1 Level 7 or higher Dinosaur monster from your hand or Deck, ignoring its Summoning conditions. This Skill can only be used once per Duel.
justification_1: This effect activates at the start of your turn to Special Summon a powerful Level 7 or higher Dinosaur monster directly from the Deck, ignoring its Summoning conditions. This provides an immediate, strong field presence that can initiate or accelerate your main strategy, even with the cost of skipping your draw and banishing specific monsters. It serves as a primary way to begin your deck's main line of play.
justification_2: This Skill activates at the very start of your Draw Phase, replacing your normal draw, allowing for a proactive opening play. It Special Summons a high-level boss monster directly from your Deck, bypassing its Summoning conditions, which is a primary way for Dinosaur decks to establish their main win condition. While it requires specific banish fodder, this is an achievable setup for a dedicated deck, making the Skill a consistent and powerful initiator for your turn's strategy.

card_text:
[At the start of the 5th turn, activate this Skill (count both players' turns).]

During your Main Phase, you can reduce your LP to 100 and discard 2 cards from your hand. Roll a six-sided die and draw cards equal to the result. This Skill can only be used once per Duel.
justification_1: This card is a Skill, not a Yu-Gi-Oh! card with monster, spell, or trap effects. Skills are game mechanics that provide passive bonuses or activate special abilities outside the standard card classifications.
justification_2: This card is a Skill, not a monster, spell, or trap card, and therefore does not fit any of the provided categories for card effects.

card_text: Activate this Skill during your Main Phase. Once per Duel, you can banish 3 cards from your GY, then draw 1 card.
justification_1: This Skill requires 3 cards to be in the GY, meaning it cannot typically be activated as the very first action in a turn unless significant milling has occurred. Its effect allows you to draw a card, which helps to continue or extend your plays by providing additional resources or combo pieces after some setup has already been established.
justification_2: This Skill requires at least 3 cards to be in the GY, meaning it can only be activated after some plays have already occurred. Its effect to draw a card provides additional resources and card advantage, which helps continue or extend existing combos or improve the board state after initial plays have been made, fitting the definition of an Extender.

card_text: If you have exactly 3 Traps in your GY, randomly add 1 Trap from your GY to your hand, randomly shuffle 1 Trap from your GY into your Deck, then randomly banish 1 Trap from your GY. This Skill can only be used once per Duel.
justification_1: This text describes a "Skill," which is a mechanic specific to the Master Duel and Duel Links formats, not a card effect that fits into the defined Yu-Gi-Oh! card categories (Starter, Handtrap, Extender, Board Breaker, Garnet). Skills are not played from the hand or field as cards with the same types of activation conditions.
justification_2: This Skill requires a specific condition (3 Traps in GY) to be met, meaning it cannot start a play. Instead, it allows a player to recycle used Trap cards, extending their ability to interact with the opponent or maintain their board presence over multiple turns by reusing resources. This fits the definition of an extender as it helps get more value after plays have started and continue a strategy.

card_text: You can only Summon/Set "Crystal" monsters and Dragon monsters while this Skill is face-up. Once per turn, during your Main Phase, you can send up to 3 cards from your hand to your GY, then choose that many "Crystal Beast" monster(s) with different names from your Deck and place them in your Spell & Trap Zone as Continuous Spell Card(s). This Skill can be used twice per Duel.
justification_1: This Skill Card's primary effect allows the player to quickly place multiple "Crystal Beast" monsters from the Deck into the Spell & Trap Zone, which is the core setup for the "Crystal Beast" archetype's main plays and win conditions. It initiates the deck's strategy from the start of the duel, fitting the definition of a Starter that begins a main line of play and makes meaningful progress towards the deck's win condition.
justification_2: This Skill Card allows the player to immediately set up multiple "Crystal Beast" monsters from the Deck into the Spell & Trap Zone by discarding cards from hand. This action is fundamental to the "Crystal Beast" strategy, as it establishes the core engine needed for subsequent summons and effects. It can be activated on Turn 1 to begin the deck's main line of play, making it a powerful opener.

card_text: Once per turn, you can discard 1 card to Fusion Summon 1 "Cyber Blader" by sending either "Etoile Cyber" or "Blade Skater" from your field to the GY as 1 Fusion Material, and the other material from your hand, field, or Deck to the GY. That "Cyber Blader" gains 400 ATK. This Skill can be used twice per Duel.
justification_1: This Skill requires a specific monster ("Etoile Cyber" or "Blade Skater") to already be on the field to activate, meaning it cannot initiate a combo by itself. Once this prerequisite is met, it enables a Fusion Summon, often utilizing a material directly from the Deck, which extends plays and helps build a stronger board by bringing out a boss monster.
justification_2: The effect requires "Etoile Cyber" or "Blade Skater" to be on your field as one of the Fusion Materials. This means you must have already established a monster on your board before you can activate this Skill, indicating it continues or extends a play rather than starting one from scratch.

card_text: During each of your Draw Phases, you gain 200 LP. During your Draw Phase, if you draw a Dinosaur monster for your normal draw, you can reveal it and place it on the bottom of your Deck, then draw 1 card. Once per turn, during your Main Phase, you can make 1 Dinosaur monster you control gain 200 ATK until the end of this turn.
justification_1: The card's effects are too minor and passive to fit any of the defined categories. It provides a negligible LP gain, a situational and minor draw cycle during the Draw Phase, and an insignificant ATK boost that requires a monster already on the field. It cannot initiate a main combo (not a Starter), disrupt the opponent from hand (not a Handtrap), significantly extend plays or push through interruptions (not an Extender), clear an opponent's board (not a Board Breaker), or serve as a necessary, but undesirable, combo piece (not a Garnet).
justification_2: The card's effects offer minor LP gain, a situational draw-fixing ability that requires drawing a specific monster type, and a negligible ATK boost. None of these abilities initiate significant combos, disrupt the opponent, extend plays in a meaningful way, or break an opponent's board. It does not fit the definition of a necessary 'Garnet' either, as it doesn't enable a specific engine while being undesirable to draw.

card_text: During your Main Phase, you can look at the top 3 cards of your Deck and return them in any order. You cannot activate Spell Cards the turn you use this Skill. Flip this card over at the end of the turn you activated this Skill.
justification_1: This effect allows deck manipulation for consistency but does not directly initiate a combo, extend plays, disrupt the opponent, or clear their board. The severe restriction of not being able to activate Spell Cards the same turn also prevents it from functioning as a typical starter or extender in most decks. It serves a utility purpose (deck-stacking) that doesn't align with the defined strategic roles.
justification_2: This "Skill" card allows for deck stacking, which is a form of consistency or future turn setup, but it does not directly initiate or extend a combo (Starter/Extender), nor does it disrupt the opponent (Handtrap) or clear their board (Board Breaker). The severe restriction of not being able to activate Spell Cards the turn it is used significantly hampers its ability to serve as a general starter or extender for most decks, as Spell Cards are fundamental for many combo lines. As a Skill Card, it is not drawn, so it cannot be a Garnet. Its primary role of manipulating future draws with a significant immediate cost does not fit the definitions of the provided categories.

card_text: If a face-up "Crystal Beast" monster you control would be destroyed, you can place a Crystallization Counter on it instead. Monsters with Crystallization Counters are considered Continuous Spell Cards in your Spell & Trap Zone instead of monsters. If a card with a Crystallization Counter would be Special Summoned, remove the counter. Cards with Crystallization Counters can be used as Fusion Material for "Ultimate Crystal" monsters.
justification_1: This card requires an existing "Crystal Beast" monster on the field to activate its effect (by protecting it from destruction), and then converts that monster into a resource (a Continuous Spell Card) that can be used for further plays, such as Fusion Summoning "Ultimate Crystal" monsters. This allows for continued combo progression or recovery after a play has started.
justification_2: This card requires "Crystal Beast" monsters to be on the field, and its effects allow for resource retention (preventing destruction and converting monsters to Continuous Spells) and further plays (making those transformed cards usable as Fusion Material for "Ultimate Crystal" monsters). This extends the utility and longevity of your resources, helping to push through interruptions and gain more value from your established board for later plays.

card_text: Activate this Skill during your Main Phase. Discard 1 card, then Fusion Summon 1 "Elemental HERO" Fusion Monster from your Extra Deck, using monsters from your hand or field as Fusion Material. If Summoning an "Elemental HERO" Fusion Monster this way that requires 3 or more materials, and you control 2 "Elemental HERO" monsters that are mentioned on it as materials, you can also use monsters in your Deck as Fusion Material. This Skill can only be used once per Duel.
justification_1: This Skill allows for a powerful Fusion Summon, potentially using materials directly from the Deck, by discarding only one card. This immediately progresses the deck's main strategy of bringing out strong Fusion Monsters, making it an ideal card to open with and begin the main line of play for an "Elemental HERO" deck.
justification_2: This Skill allows for a powerful Fusion Summon as a primary action during the Main Phase, potentially even utilizing materials directly from the Deck. This enables the deck's main win condition (Fusion Summoning powerful "Elemental HERO" monsters) by itself, making significant progress towards establishing a board without requiring prior extensive setup.

card_text: This card cannot be Special Summoned. This card must be Tribute Summoned by offering 3 Winged Beast-Type monsters on your side of the field as Tributes. If this card attacks your opponent's Life Points directly and makes them 0, the controller of this card wins the Match.
justification_1: This card's effect is a win condition that activates when it deals the final blow in battle. It does not initiate a combo, disrupt the opponent, extend a play, or break a board. It also doesn't fit the "Garnet" definition. Therefore, it does not fall into any of the provided categories.
justification_2: This card has a win condition but no effect that activates to begin plays, extend them, disrupt the opponent, or break their board. It is not a desirable draw for an engine nor is it a handtrap.
